name: gad:eval-bootstrap
description: Bootstrap an eval agent with full GAD context injected into its prompt
argument-hint: --project <name>
allowed-tools:
  - Read
  - Write
  - Bash
  - Agent
---

<objective>
Generate a bootstrap prompt for a single eval project and launch an agent in an isolated worktree. The agent starts with all GAD context inline — it does not need to discover the loop.
</objective>

<process>

1. **Generate the bootstrap prompt:**
   ```bash
   gad eval run --project $PROJECT --prompt-only
   ```

2. **Read the generated PROMPT.md** from the eval run directory.

3. **Launch an agent** with `isolation: "worktree"` using the prompt content.

4. **After completion**, reconstruct the trace:
   ```bash
   gad eval trace reconstruct --project $PROJECT
   ```","numLines":30,"startLine":1,"totalLines":37}}

---
name: gad:eval-spawn
description: >-
  Kick off a GAD eval run from inside a Claude Code session by wrapping
  `gad eval run --execute` to get the JSON spec, then spawning a worktree-isolated
  subagent against that spec. This skill is the seam between the orchestrating
  session (plan/review) and the spawned builder session (execute) per decision
  forge-09. Use when the user asks to "spawn an eval run", "kick off an eval",
  or invokes /gad-eval-spawn. The skill writes a session marker under
  `.planning/.eval-runs/<run-id>.json` so the orchestrator can poll status and
  preserve outputs via `gad eval preserve` after the builder session completes.
argument-hint: <project> [--runtime claude-code] [--baseline HEAD]
allowed-tools:
  - Read
  - Write
  - Bash
  - Agent
---

<objective>
Spawn an eval run in a worktree-isolated subagent. This skill is the plan/build
seam (decision forge-09): the orchestrating session plans and reviews, the
spawned session builds. The orchestrator stays in charge of preservation —
after the builder finishes, this skill preserves outputs via `gad eval preserve`
and marks the session complete.
</objective>

<process>

Parse from `$ARGUMENTS`:
- `<project>` (required, positional) — eval project name
- `--runtime <id>` (optional, default `claude-code`) — runtime driving the spawned agent
- `--baseline <sha>` (optional, default `HEAD`) — git baseline the worktree branches from (brownfield parents use the preserved spawn SHA here)

1. **Resolve the project:**
   ```bash
   gad eval list | grep -E "^\s*$PROJECT\b" || { echo "Eval project '$PROJECT' not found. Run 'gad eval list' to see available projects."; exit 1; }
   ```
   (`gad eval list` is multi-root aware as of task 42.4-12; if that work has not yet landed the default root `vendor/get-anything-done/evals/` is still used.)

2. **Generate the exec spec:**
   ```bash
   gad eval run --project "$PROJECT" --runtime "$RUNTIME" --baseline "$BASELINE" --execute
   ```
   This writes `evals/<project>/v<N>/EXEC.json` and also print
... [truncated 4355 bytes] ...
ill depends on `gad eval run --execute`
  (already shipped). If the CLI doesn't recognize the flag, stop and tell the
  user to upgrade their GAD CLI rather than falling back to manual worktree
  plumbing.
</failure_modes>

<examples>
User: "Spawn an eval run for escape-the-dungeon."
Agent: invokes `/gad-eval-spawn escape-the-dungeon` → generates EXEC.json at
`evals/escape-the-dungeon/v7/EXEC.json`, creates worktree at
`.claude/worktrees/agent-escape-the-dungeon-v7`, writes marker at
`.planning/.eval-runs/escape-the-dungeon-v7.json`, spawns builder subagent with
the prompt from EXEC.json, on completion runs
`gad eval preserve escape-the-dungeon v7 --from .claude/worktrees/agent-escape-the-dungeon-v7`,
cleans the worktree, updates marker to `status: "cleaned"`.

User: "Kick off a brownfield run from the v6 spawn SHA."
Agent: `/gad-eval-spawn escape-the-dungeon --baseline <sha-of-v6-spawn>` — same
flow, worktree branches off the preserved v6 spawn.
</examples>

<rules>
- Always write the session marker BEFORE spawning the agent — if the agent call
  fails, the marker lets the orchestrator recover.
- Never skip `gad eval preserve` (decision gad-38). Preservation failures must
  stop cleanup.
- Never clean the worktree on failure paths — the operator needs it for forensics.
- Never wrap or rewrite the prompt from EXEC.json. It's already fully hydrated
  with GAD context, trace hooks, and post-steps.
- Do not set `GAD_RUNTIME` or trace env vars manually — use the values from
  `EXEC.json.envVars` so the CLI stays the single source of truth.
- `.planning/.eval-runs/` is transient runtime state. Do not commit markers.
</rules>

<related>
- `gad:eval-run` — generates the prompt and (without `--execute`) creates the
  worktree itself; this skill is the `--execute`-mode counterpart.
- `gad:eval-bootstrap` — older bootstrap flow that injects context without
  spawning.
- decision forge-09 — plan/build separation; this skill is the documented seam.
- decision gad-38 — eval preservation contract.
</related>

inputs.content:
---
name: gad:eval-spawn
description: Spawn a GAD eval run in a worktree-isolated subagent (plan/build seam per forge-09)
argument-hint: <project> [--runtime claude-code] [--baseline HEAD]
allowed-tools:
  - Read
  - Write
  - Bash
  - Agent
---

<execution_context>
@skills/gad-eval-spawn/SKILL.md
</execution_context>

<objective>
Spawn a GAD eval run from inside a Claude Code session. Wraps
`gad eval run --project <name> --execute` to obtain an EXEC.json spec, creates a
worktree-isolated builder subagent against that spec, writes a session marker
under `.planning/.eval-runs/<run-id>.json`, and on completion preserves outputs
via `gad eval preserve` and cleans up the worktree.
</objective>

<process>

Follow the full procedure in `@skills/gad-eval-spawn/SKILL.md`. Summary:

1. Parse `<project>`, `--runtime` (default `claude-code`), `--baseline` (default `HEAD`) from `$ARGUMENTS`.
2. Run `gad eval run --project <project> --runtime <runtime> --baseline <baseline> --execute` and parse the EXEC.json from stdout (between `--- EXEC_JSON_START ---` / `--- EXEC_JSON_END ---`).
3. Refuse if `.planning/.eval-runs/<project>-<version>.json` already exists.
4. Create worktree at `.claude/worktrees/agent-<project>-<version>` from `--baseline`.
5. Write the session marker with `status: "running"`.
6. Spawn a `general-purpose` subagent with `isolation: "worktree"`, `cwd` = worktree path, prompt + env vars from EXEC.json.
7. On agent completion, run `gad eval preserve <project> <version> --from <worktree>` and update marker → `status: "preserved"`.
8. Run `gad worktree clean agent-<project>-<version>` and update marker → `status: "cleaned"`.
9. Report the run id, marker path, and final status.

</process>

<rules>
- Write the session marker BEFORE spawning the agent.
- Never skip `gad eval preserve` (decision gad-38).
- Never clean the worktree on failure paths — leave it for forensics.
- Never rewrap the prompt from EXEC.json — it's already fully hydrated.
- This skill is the plan/build seam (decision forge-09): orchestrator plans, spawned agent builds.
</rules>

inputs.old_string:
<task id="42.4-13" type="framework" status="planned"><goal>New skill + slash command `/gad-eval-spawn` for kicking off eval runs from inside a Claude Code session. Wraps `gad eval run --project <name> --execute` to get the JSON spec, then uses the orchestrator's Agent/spawn capability to launch a worktree-isolated Claude (or other runtime) agent against the spec. Writes a session marker (probably under `.planning/.eval-runs/<run-id>.json`) so the orchestrator knows which run is in flight, can poll status, and can preserve outputs via `gad eval preserve` after completion. Authored via the `create-skill` skill so we dogfood the skill-authoring pattern. Skill name: `gad-eval-spawn`. Slash command: `/gad-eval-spawn <project> [--runtime claude-code] [--baseline HEAD]`. Surfaced 2026-04-14 by user: "we need a skill use create skill and also our command workflow pattern for /gad-project-eval-run or whatever we should call it to actually kick start the flow in claude code." Per decision forge-09 (plan/build separation), this skill is the seam between the orchestrating session and the spawned builder session.</goal>
      <keywords>skill,slash-command,eval-spawn,create-skill,orchestrator,worktree,dogfood</keywords>
    </task>
inputs.new_string:
<task id="42.4-13" type="skill" status="done" skill="create-skill" agent="general-purpose-subagent"><goal>New skill + slash command `/gad-eval-spawn` for kicking off eval runs from inside a Claude Code session. Wraps `gad eval run --project <name> --execute` to get the JSON spec, then uses the orchestrator's Agent/spawn capability to launch a worktree-isolated Claude (or other runtime) agent against the spec. Writes a session marker (probably under `.planning/.eval-runs/<run-id>.json`) so the orchestrator knows which run is in flight, can poll status, and can preserve outputs via `gad eval preserve` after completion. Authored via the `create-skill` skill so we dogfood the skill-authoring pattern. Skill name: `gad-eval-spawn`. Slash command: `/gad-eval-spawn <project> [--runtime claude-code] [--baseline HEAD]`. Surfaced 2026-04-14 by user: "we need a skill use create skill and also our command workflow pattern for /gad-project-eval-run or whatever we should call it to actually kick start the flow in claude code." Per decision forge-09 (plan/build separation), this skill is the seam between the orchestrating session and the spawned builder session.</goal>
      <resolution>Shipped 2026-04-14. Created canonical skill `skills/gad-eval-spawn/` with `SKILL.md`, `COMMAND.md`, and `skill.json` following the existing `gad-eval-run` / `gad-eval-bootstrap` pattern (canonical frontmatter `name: gad:eval-spawn`, folded `>-` description, argument-hint `<project> [--runtime claude-code] [--baseline HEAD]`, allowed-tools: Read/Write/Bash/Agent). Procedure documents the full plan/build seam: resolve project via `gad eval list`, call `gad eval run --project <name> --runtime <r> --baseline <b> --execute` to produce EXEC.json (the CLI already emits this between `--- EXEC_JSON_START/END ---` markers — no CLI change needed), refuse if `.planning/.eval-runs/<project>-<version>.json` marker already exists, create worktree at `.claude/worktrees/agent-<project>-<version>` from `--baseline`, write session marker with `status: "running"` + runId/project/version/species/generation/worktreePath/runDir/agentId/startedAt, spawn `general-purpose` subagent with `isolation: "worktree"`, cwd + prompt + env pulled verbatim from EXEC.json (never rewrap the prompt — it's already fully hydrated), on completion update marker → `agent-complete`, run `gad eval preserve <project> <version> --from <worktree>` (mandatory per gad-38) → `preserved`, then `gad worktree clean` → `cleaned`. Failure modes: spawn-fail removes marker, agent-fail leaves worktree for forensics, preserve-fail blocks cleanup, duplicate marker refuses. `.planning/.eval-runs/` added to submodule .gitignore as transient runtime state. `scripts/validate-canonical-assets.mjs` passes. Cited decisions forge-09 (plan/build seam) and gad-38 (preservation contract). Dogfood note: the `create-skill` skill's YAML frontmatter rules (folded `>-`, colons-safe, indentation) guided the SKILL.md shape cleanly — no friction. `gad eval run --execute` already existed at bin/gad.cjs:1932+ and emits the EXEC.json shape used by this skill (runId is derived as `<project>-<version>` since the CLI keys on version rather than a separate run-id field), so no CLI addition was needed.</resolution>
      <commands>
        <command>node vendor/get-anything-done/scripts/validate-canonical-assets.mjs</command>
        <command>node vendor/get-anything-done/bin/gad.cjs eval run --help</command>
      </commands>
      <keywords>skill,slash-command,eval-spawn,create-skill,orchestrator,worktree,dogfood,forge-09,forge-seam</keywords>
    </task>

feat(skill): add gad:eval-spawn skill + slash command (task 42.4-13)

New canonical skill `skills/gad-eval-spawn/` (SKILL.md + COMMAND.md +
skill.json) that wraps `gad eval run --project <name> --execute` and spawns
a worktree-isolated subagent against the resulting EXEC.json spec. This is
the plan/build seam between the orchestrating session and the spawned
builder session per decision forge-09.

Procedure: resolve project via `gad eval list`, call the CLI with --execute
to produce EXEC.json, refuse if a session marker at
`.planning/.eval-runs/<project>-<version>.json` already exists, create a
worktree under `.claude/worktrees/agent-<project>-<version>` from the
supplied --baseline, write a running marker, spawn a general-purpose Agent
with isolation: worktree and the hydrated prompt + env vars from EXEC.json,
then on completion run `gad eval preserve` (mandatory per decision gad-38)
and clean the worktree with `gad worktree clean`. Failure modes: agent-fail
leaves the worktree for forensics, preserve-fail blocks cleanup, duplicate
runId refuses.

No CLI changes needed — `gad eval run --execute` already emits the
EXEC.json shape between `--- EXEC_JSON_START/END ---` stdout markers
(bin/gad.cjs:1932+). `.planning/.eval-runs/` added to .gitignore as
transient runtime state. `validate-canonical-assets.mjs` passes. Dogfooded
via the `create-skill` skill's YAML frontmatter conventions (folded `>-`
description, argument-hint, allowed-tools Read/Write/Bash/Agent).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -15

Authored via the `create-skill` skill so we dogfood the skill-authoring pattern. Skill name: `gad-eval-spawn`. Slash command: `/gad-eval-spawn <project> [--runtime claude-code] [--baseline HEAD]`. ... [truncated 545 bytes] ...
 pattern (canonical frontmatter `name: gad:eval-spawn`, folded `>-` description, argument-hint `<project> [--runtime claude-code] [--baseline HEAD]`, allowed-tools: Read/Write/Bash/Agent). Procedure documents the full plan/build seam: resolve project via `gad eval list`, call `gad eval run --project <name> --runtime <r> --baseline <b> --execute` to produce EXEC.json (the CLI already emits this between `--- EXEC_JSON_START/END ---` markers — no CLI change needed), refuse if `.planning/.eval-runs/<project>-<version>.json` marker already exists, create worktree at `.claude/worktrees/agent-<project>-<version>` from `--baseline`, write session marker with `status: "running"` + runId/project/version/species/generation/worktreePath/runDir/agentId/startedAt, spawn `general-purpose` subagent with `isolation: "worktree"`, cwd + prompt + env pulled verbatim from EXEC.json (never rewrap the prompt — it's already fully hydrated), on completion update marker → `agent-complete`, run `gad eval preserve <project> <version> --from <worktree>` (mandatory per gad-38) → `preserved`, then `gad worktree clean` → `cleaned`. Failure modes: spawn-fail removes marker, agent-fail leaves worktree for forensics, preserve-fail blocks cleanup, duplicate marker refuses. `.planning/.eval-runs/` added to submodule .gitignore as transient runtime state. `scripts/validate-canonical-assets.mjs` passes. Cited decisions forge-09 (plan/build seam) and gad-38 (preservation contract). Dogfood note: the `create-skill` skill's YAML frontmatter rules (folded `>-`, colons-safe, indentation) guided the SKILL.md shape cleanly — no friction. `gad eval run --execute` already existed at bin/gad.cjs:1932+ and emits the EXEC.json shape used by this skill (runId is derived as `<project>-<version>` since the CLI keys on version rather than a separate run-id field), so no CLI addition was needed.</resolution>

inputs.new_string:
<task id="42.4-12" type="framework" status="done" skill="default" agent="general-purpose-subagent"><goal>Multi-root eval discovery. Add `[[evals.roots]]` array to `gad-config.toml` mirroring `[[planning.roots]]`. Default root stays `vendor/get-anything-done/evals/` for backwards-compat. New roots like parent-repo `evals/` (or `apps/forge/evals/`) become first-class. `gad eval list/run/preserve/verify/open/review/report` all walk the union of roots. Eval project ids must be unique across roots — duplicate detection at load time. Surfaced 2026-04-14 during app-forge planning: forge wants to consume evals from the parent custom_portfolio repo without polluting the framework submodule with downstream user projects, AND forge's own eval project (app-forge) needs to live outside the GAD submodule because forge is not a framework component. Blocks app-forge eval bootstrap (decision forge-09 plan/build separation requires fresh-agent spawn for any forge implementation work).</goal>
      <resolution>Shipped 2026-04-14. Added `[[evals.roots]]` TOML parsing + serialization to `bin/gad-config.cjs` (new `evalsRoots` field on the loaded config object, empty-array default for backwards compat across fromToml/fromJson/defaults paths). Introduced three helpers at the top of `bin/gad.cjs`: `getEvalRoots()` returns ordered absolute dirs of every root (walks upward from submodule findRepoRoot to locate the monorepo config that declares `[[evals.roots]]`, appends the submodule default implicitly, dedupes on absolute path), `listAllEvalProjects()` walks all roots and throws a load-time error on duplicate project ids naming both paths, `resolveEvalProject(name)` / `resolveOrDefaultEvalProjectDir(name)` map a project name to its owning root. Rewired every eval subcommand to use the helpers instead of hardcoded `path.join(gadDir, 'evals', …)`: list, run, score, diff, setup, status, runs, show, scores, suite, report, preserve, verify, review, open, readme, inherit-skills, trace (list/show/diff/report/write/init/log-cmd/log-skill/finalize/reconstruct/from-log), plus the `log --eval`, `worktree prune` preservation cross-check, `rounds --project`, and `listEvalProjectsHint` helper. Per-skill eval harness under `gad eval skill …` left untouched (different system, skills/<name>/evals/). Added `[[evals.roots]] id="app-forge" path="apps/forge/evals"` to repo-root `gad-config.toml` (also fixed adjacent `apps\forge` backslash typo in the `[[planning.roots]]` entry to `apps/forge`). Created `apps/forge/evals/.gitkeep`. Verification: `gad eval list` shows both existing evals from the submodule, `gad eval verify` passes clean, temporarily dropping `apps/forge/evals/escape-the-dungeon/project.json` triggers the duplicate-id error naming both absolute paths. No new decision file entry — the "implicit default root + additive configured roots + load-time duplicate detection" rule is captured in the helper doc comments in `bin/gad.cjs` and can be promoted to DECISIONS.xml later if we add a second policy knob.</resolution>
      <commands>
        <command>node vendor/get-anything-done/bin/gad.cjs eval list</command>
        <command>node vendor/get-anything-done/bin/gad.cjs eval verify</command>
      </commands>
      <keywords>multi-root,evals,discovery,gad-config,roots</keywords>
    </task>

inputs.command:
cd "C:/Users/benja/Documents/custom_portfolio/vendor/get-anything-done" && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat(framework): multi-root eval discovery via [[evals.roots]] (42.4-12)

Add [[evals.roots]] config parsing that mirrors [[planning.roots]]. The
default root (vendor/get-anything-done/evals/) stays implicit for
backwards compat; configured roots are additive and searched first.

New helpers in bin/gad.cjs:
- getEvalRoots() walks upward from findRepoRoot to locate the monorepo
  config declaring [[evals.roots]], dedupes on absolute path, and
  appends the submodule default implicitly.
- listAllEvalProjects() walks the union of roots and throws a clear
  load-time error naming both paths when a project id collides.
- resolveEvalProject / resolveOrDefaultEvalProjectDir map a project
  name to its owning root.

Every eval subcommand rewired to use the helpers instead of
hardcoded path.join(gadDir, 'evals', ...): list, run, score, diff,
setup, status, runs, show, scores, suite, report, preserve, verify,
review, open, readme, inherit-skills, all trace.* subcommands, plus
log --eval, worktree prune's preservation cross-check, rounds
--project, and listEvalProjectsHint. The per-skill eval harness under
`gad eval skill …` is untouched — it lives under skills/<name>/evals/
and is a separate system.

Unblocks app-forge hosting evals at apps/forge/evals/ without
polluting the framework submodule.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

## Task goal (verbatim)
Project ⊇ Species inheritance contract in the CLI eval loader. `project.json` declares defaults: `techStack`, `contextFramework`, `installedSkills`, `defaultContent` (paths or refs to default assets/files/scaffolding). `species.json` may override any of those + adds species-only fields: `inherits_from` (parent species for cross-species lineage), `dna` (workflow + skill manifest summary), species-specific `description`. The CLI's eval loaders (used by `gad eval list/run/preserve` and by `build-site-data.mjs`) MUST merge project + species at load time and return the merged shape — readers should never manually combine them. Add typed interface, merge function, and tests for: (a) species inherits all project fields when species.json is empty, (b) species overrides individual fields cleanly, (c) species can add species-only fields without affecting project defaults, (d) `inherits_from` resolves a chain across species within the same project. <decisions>
  <decision id="forge-01">
    <title>app-forge is a separate project, not a milestone of `global`</title>
    <summary>The `global` planning root is monorepo infrastructure work (lockfiles, planning sink relocation, content extraction, gad site compile). app-forge is a product — a local-first generation studio with its own roadmap, its own decisions, and its own dogfooding acceptance test. Mixing them would dilute both. Created as a new project via `gad projects init --name app-forge --path apps/forge`.</summary>
    <impact>app-forge gets its own .planning/ at apps/forge/.planning/. New phases here don't block or get blocked by global infra phases. Cross-project links happen through references, not nesting.</impact>
    <references>
      <file path="apps/forge/.planning/PROJECT.md" />
      <file path=".planning/gad-config.toml" />
    </references>
  </decision>
  <decision id="forge-02">
    <title>Internals use eval/run/lineage vocabulary; UI renames to species/brood/generation</title>
    <summary>The existing GAD eval framework already provides the runtime substrate forge needs: greenfield/brownfield modes (decision gad-39), worktree management, eval preservation (decision gad-38), parallel runs via `gad eval suite`. Renaming "eval" to "generation" everywhere would be massive churn across XML, CLI, and skill names with no functional benefit. Instead, planning artifacts and CLI keep the eval vocabulary. The forge UI is the one place that translates: project → project, eval run → generation, parent-of-fork → species, parallel siblings → brood.</summary>
    <impact>One translation layer at the UI boundary. Zero rename churn in `gad eval` CLI or `evals/<project>/` directories. New forge components import a small `vocabulary.ts` map to render user-facing terms.</impact>
    <references>
      <fi
... [truncated 9111 bytes] ...
" />
      <file path="vendor/get-anything-done/.planning/TASK-REGISTRY.xml" />
    </references>
  </decision>
  <decision id="forge-11">
    <title>Forge's proving-ground goal: produce escape-the-dungeon from REQUIREMENTS via the editor</title>
    <summary>Forge's measurable success isn't "the editor exists" — it's "a developer can open forge, point it at escape-the-dungeon's existing REQUIREMENTS.md, hit go, and end up with a playable ETD generation by iterating spawned runs from those requirements." ETD is the canonical proving-ground species because (a) it already has 12 generations of historical data to compare against, (b) it has a clear-cut binary success state ("does the player move? does the dungeon load?"), (c) it's complex enough to surface real failure modes but bounded enough to verify by hand, and (d) it lives in the existing eval framework so we're not inventing a new test rig. DOG-01 (forge generates forge) is still the v1 acceptance test, but forge-11 adds DOG-02: "forge generates a passing ETD generation from its requirements file via the editor's spawn flow."</summary>
    <impact>Gives forge a measurable north star that isn't self-referential. DOG-01 alone is circular ("forge can build the thing that builds itself") and prone to gaming. DOG-02 (produce ETD) is concrete, externally verifiable, and uses an existing artifact (ETD requirements) we didn't author for this purpose. Phase 08's success criteria expand to include both DOG-01 and DOG-02. Forge-11 also gives us a comparison metric: how do forge-spawned ETD generations stack up against the 12 hand-iterated historical generations on the same requirements?</impact>

<planning-references>
  <doc kind="project-overview">
    <path>apps/forge/.planning/REQUIREMENTS.xml</path>
    <content><![CDATA[
# app-forge — Project Overview

## What This Is

A local-first app and game generation studio. Captures user requirements through a structured intake UI, one-shots a greenfield generation from those requirements, and lets the user persist, fork, and lineage-trace generations. Each generation is a real worktree with files the user can edit through an embedded file-folder editor and an embedded terminal that runs Claude/Codex CLIs to spawn child generations ("broods"). Forge is itself the standard way to build apps in this monorepo — it dogfoods its own editor to build itself.

## Core Value

A user can describe what they want, get a one-shot generation, and then iterate on it through child generations that inherit code/content from their parent — all from a single editor surface that hosts the coding agent inline.

## Tech Stack

- Next.js (App Router) editor shell at `apps/forge/`
- xterm.js + node-pty for the embedded terminal pane
- Monaco or CodeMirror for the editor pane (decision deferred — see DECISIONS.xml forge-07)
- Generation runs via the existing `gad eval` worktree pipeline
- Native git locally for clone/publish; isomorphic-git stubbed for the deferred hosted path
- `claude` and `codex` CLIs spawned in the embedded PTY (no API proxy in v1)

## Constraints

- Local-first: v1 targets a single developer running on their own machine
- Subscription routing: agent runs spawn `claude`/`codex` CLIs in PTY and inherit existing subscription auth
- Dogfooding: forge must be capable of generating forge before v1 ships (DOG-01)
]]></content>
  </doc>
  <doc kind="canonical-requirements">
    <path>apps/forge/.planning/REQUIREMENTS.xml</path>
    <content><![CDATA[
# app-
... [truncated 2842 bytes] ...
n the prompt "build app-forge" produces a working species containing forge's own scaffolding. v1 acceptance test #1. See decision forge-08.
- DOG-02: Forge can generate escape-the-dungeon from its existing REQUIREMENTS.md via the editor's spawn flow, with a resulting playable generation that meets ETD's success criteria. v1 acceptance test #2. See decision forge-11. ETD is the canonical proving-ground species — it has 12 historical generations to compare against, a clear binary pass state, and it lives in the existing eval framework.

## v2 Requirements (Deferred)

- HOST-01: Hosted multi-tenant deployment — local-first first; complexity of auth, isolation, billing, infra
- HOST-02: In-browser clone via isomorphic-git for users without git installed — belongs to hosted path
- COLLAB-01: Realtime collaborative editing on a species — single-user is the v1 target
- MKT-04: Public marketplace browse across all users — requires hosted backend
- PROXY-01: Optional OpenAI-compatible HTTP proxy in front of `claude` CLI for non-CLI clients
- MOBILE-01: Mobile/responsive editor surface — desktop-only for v1
- TERMCFG-01: Terminal shell config namespace — premature configurability

## Out of Scope

- Custom Claude API proxy / OpenAI-compat shim — we spawn `claude` CLI in PTY and inherit existing subscription auth
- Auto-cleanup of old generations — generations persist by design; user manages worktree lifecycle
- Centralized user account system — local-first, no accounts in v1
- Mobile-first design — desktop-only

## Traceability

| Requirement | Phase |
|-------------|-------|
| GEN-02, MKT-01 | 01 |
| REQ-01, REQ-02, REQ-03 | 02 |
| GEN-01, GEN-02, GEN-03 | 03 |
| EDIT-01, EDIT-02, EDIT-03, EDIT-04 | 04 |
| TERM-01, TERM-02 | 05 |
| GEN-04, GEN-05, TERM-03, TERM-04 | 06 |
| MKT-01, MKT-02, MKT-03 | 07 |
| DOG-01 | 08 |

Coverage: 18/18 v1 requirements mapped to phases.
]]></content>

</planning-references>

1164:    <task id="42.4-14" type="framework" status="done" skill="default" agent="general-purpose-subagent"><goal>Audit and finish phase 43 species.json migration. Phase 43 unified the eval schema around `evals/<project>/project.json` + `evals/<project>/species/<species>/species.json`, but escape-the-dungeon's existing species directories still use `gad.json` instead of `species.json`. Decide canonical name (recommend: `species.json` per phase 43 spec) and either: (a) rename `gad.json` → `species.json` across all existing species in `vendor/get-anything-done/evals/`, OR (b) update phase 43 prose + the CLI loader to treat `gad.json` as the canonical name and abandon `species.json`. User direction 2026-04-14: do the rename to species.json. Update CLI loaders, build-site-data.mjs, eval-preservation tests, and any references in skills/workflows/docs. Pick one shape so app-forge's species file isn't ahead of reality and so the project ⊇ species inheritance contract (42.4-15) has a stable file shape to read from.</goal>
1167:    <task id="42.4-15" type="framework" status="planned"><goal>Project ⊇ Species inheritance contract in the CLI eval loader. `project.json` declares defaults: `techStack`, `contextFramework`, `installedSkills`, `defaultContent` (paths or refs to default assets/files/scaffolding). `species.json` may override any of those + adds species-only fields: `inherits_from` (parent species for cross-species lineage), `dna` (workflow + skill manifest summary), species-specific `description`. The CLI's eval loaders (used by `gad eval list/run/preserve` and by build-site-data.mjs) MUST merge project + species at load time and return the merged shape — readers should never manually combine them. Add typed interface, merge function, and tests for: (a) species inherits all project fields when species.json is empty, (b) species overrides individual fields cleanly, (c) species can add species-only fields without affecting project defaults, (d) `inherits_from` resolves a chain across species within the same project. Decision forge-10 captures the rationale. This is what the forge editor's "edit project settings" vs "edit species settings" UX is built on.</goal>
1170:    <task id="42.4-16" type="framework" status="planned"><goal>Forge proving-ground spec: formalize "forge produces a passing escape-the-dungeon generation from its existing REQUIREMENTS.md via the editor's spawn flow" as a measurable acceptance criterion. Decision forge-11 captures the why. Output: a checklist doc at `apps/forge/.planning/plans/forge-proving-ground.md` (or similar) defining what "passing ETD" means in concrete observable terms — game boots, player loads, dungeon renders, player can move, basic interaction works, no console errors. Reference ETD's existing 12 generations as comparison data (which historical generations passed which checks). This becomes part of forge's DOG-02 acceptance and gets folded into phase 08's success criteria. Should be authored after 42.4-12, 42.4-13, 42.4-14, 42.4-15 land but before forge phase 01 spawns its first run.</goal>

{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"C:\Users\benja\Documents\custom_portfolio\vendor\get-anything-done\.planning\DECISIONS.xml","content":"  <decision id="gad-182">
    <title>Canonical framework assets need an automated integrity gate: unique public skill names and resolvable `@...` references</title>
    <summary>Manual grep passes are not enough to keep the framework surface coherent after repeated structural migrations. The canonical asset tree now has enough cross-linking (`skills/` → `workflows/` / `references/` / `templates/`, agent prompts, hook docs, site catalog generation) that two failure modes need to be enforced automatically: (1) duplicate public skill identities in canonical `skills/`, and (2) broken canonical alias references such as `@workflows/...` or `@references/...` inside framework assets. Policy: ship and maintain a repository-level validator that scans the canonical asset roots, fails on duplicate public `name:` values, and fails on broken alias references. Warnings for non-canonical or historical/generated surfaces are acceptable, but canonical roots must stay clean.</summary>
    <impact>(1) `scripts/validate-canonical-assets.mjs` becomes the fast integrity check for framework shape migrations. (2) New command surface: `node scripts/validate-canonical-assets.mjs` and package alias `check:assets`. (3) Future cleanups should prefer changing canonical source and regenerating derived site/catalog artifacts rather than hand-editing generated files. (4) This does not replace runtime tests; it complements them by catching catalog/reference drift before it becomes a broken install or misleading site surface.</impact>
  </decision>
  <decision id="gad-183">
    <title>Proto-skills stage inside the owning project's `.planning/` and install outward into runtimes on demand</title>
    <summary>Proto-skills are not canonical framework assets yet; they are project-local planning artifacts awaiting human review. Policy: the canonical staging directory for proto-skills is `.planning/proto-skills/` inside the owning GAD project, not `skills/proto-skills/`. This keeps drafted proto-skills in the same lifecycle zone as tasks, decisions, snapshots, and workflows until the operator explicitly decides what to do next. From that staged location there are now two explicit outward moves: `gad evolution install <slug>` installs the staged proto-skill into one or more coding-agent runtimes for local testing while keeping it staged; `gad evolution promote <slug>` promotes the staged proto-skill into canonical `skills/` so it joins the framework DNA. Promotion must preserve provenance rather than dropping it.</summary>
    <impact>(1) Live evolution CLI paths move from `skills/proto-skills/` to `.planning/proto-skills/`. (2) `gad evolution install` becomes the fast bridge from planning artifact to runtime test surface, mirroring the existing runtime install conventions (`--claude --codex --cursor --windsurf --augment --copilot --antigravity --all`, local/global, optional config dir). (3) `gad evolution promote` now targets canonical `skills/` and carries the full staged bundle forward, including `PROVENANCE.md`. (4) Candidate generation and proto-skill docs must stop describing proto-skills as a subdirectory of the public skill catalog. (5) Historical candidate/eval artifacts may still mention the old path, but canonical source, CLI help, and planning docs should point at `.planning/proto-skills/`.</impact>
  </decision></decisions>

# Phase 01 — Shell + read-only generation listing

**Phase:** 01
**Status:** kickoff complete
**Date:** 2026-04-14
**Decisions referenced:** forge-01, forge-02, forge-06

## Goal

Stand up `apps/forge/` as a Next.js workspace package that boots in the monorepo and renders a read-only list of every existing eval generation discoverable on disk under `evals/<project>/species/<species>/v<N>/`. Click-through to a per-generation detail route showing metadata. No editing, no running, no spawning, no terminal — that's all later phases.

This is the foundation everything else hangs off. Phase 01 ships value the moment a developer can run `pnpm dev --filter @portfolio/forge` and see their existing generations.

## Scope

**In:**
- New pnpm workspace package at `apps/forge/` named `@portfolio/forge`
- Next.js 15 (App Router) shell with `app/layout.tsx` + `app/page.tsx`
- Server-side data source `lib/generations.ts` that walks the `evals/` directory and returns a typed `Generation[]` (project, species, version, createdAt, lineageParent, worktreePath)
- Index route `/` that renders the generation list as a card grid (or simple table) — clickable, navigates to detail
- Detail route `/g/[project]/[species]/[version]` that fetches one generation's metadata and renders all fields plus a stub for "files in this generation" showing only the worktree path string (no file browser yet — that's phase 04)
- Add `apps/forge` to `pnpm-workspace.yaml`
- Brief `apps/forge/README.md` documenting how to run forge and what phase 01 covers

**Out:**
- Editor pane (phase 04)
- Embedded terminal (phase 05)
- Requirements capture intake UI (phase 02)
- Triggering new generations (phase 03)
- Forking/lineage operations (phase 06)

# Forge Proving-Ground Spec

**Task:** 42.4-16
**Decision:** forge-11
**Date:** 2026-04-14
**Status:** spec (no harness exists yet)

## Purpose

This doc formalizes the measurable acceptance criteria for forge DOG-02 — "forge produces a passing escape-the-dungeon generation from ETD's existing `REQUIREMENTS.md` via the editor's spawn flow." Decision forge-11 captures the why: ETD is the canonical proving-ground species because it has 12 historical generations to compare against, a clear binary pass state, and it lives in the existing eval framework so we aren't inventing a new test rig. DOG-01 (forge generates forge) is self-referential and prone to gaming; DOG-02 is externally verifiable against an artifact we didn't author for this purpose. This checklist is cited by forge `REQUIREMENTS.xml` DOG-02 and `ROADMAP.xml` phase 08 and must pass for at least one fresh generation produced by forge's spawn flow before forge phase 08 is considered complete.

## Proving-ground definition

Forge passes its proving-ground when it produces a generation of escape-the-dungeon, from ETD's existing `vendor/get-anything-done/evals/escape-the-dungeon/species/gad/REQUIREMENTS.md`, via forge's editor spawn flow (`/gad-eval-spawn` or editor equivalent per 42.4-13), that meets every item in the Observable Pass Criteria below. The generation must be spawned through a real `gad eval` worktree run — the orchestrator session MUST NOT hand-write code per decision forge-09.

## Observable pass criteria

Each item is a single observable check. Items marked `GAP:` have no existing infrastructure and need to be stood up as phase 08 scope; until then they are scored best-effort from RUN.md / SCORE.md notes.

- [ ] **PG-01 — Build artifact exists.** After spawn completes, a build artifact exists at `apps/portfolio/public/evals/escape-the-dungeon/<species>/v<N>/index.html` (via `gad eval preserve`).
  - How to verify: `gad eval verify` passes for the generated `(project, species, version)` tuple.

- [ ] **PG-02 — index.html loads cleanly.
... [truncated 5969 bytes] ...
 forge phase 08 is meant to close.

## Acceptance binding

This doc is cited as the acceptance criterion for forge `REQUIREMENTS.xml` DOG-02 and `ROADMAP.xml` phase 08 success criteria. When forge phase 08 is verified, every checkbox above (PG-01 through PG-09) must pass for at least one fresh generation produced by forge's spawn flow (`/gad-eval-spawn` or editor equivalent) against ETD's existing `vendor/get-anything-done/evals/escape-the-dungeon/species/gad/REQUIREMENTS.md`. Partial passes are logged but do not satisfy DOG-02.

## Open questions / gaps

- No Playwright (or any headless-browser) harness currently exists in the repo. Standing one up is phase 08 scope, not this doc's scope, and not task 42.4-16's scope.
- ETD has no canonical root selector, no canonical player test hook, and no canonical tile-count hook. The first forge-spawned generation that passes PG-04 will establish the probe; at that point we decide whether to add a "must expose `window.__game` test hook" line to ETD's `REQUIREMENTS.md` or to stick with screenshot-based probes.
- Input scheme (arrows vs WASD) varies across historical generations. PG-07's probe must try both and record which worked.
- The historical comparison table is scored from on-disk artifacts only. If we ever want real PG-02..PG-08 data for v7/v8/v9/v10 we'd have to re-run the probe against each preserved build; that's a separate backfill task, not a blocker for DOG-02.
- v8's SCORE.md "Build requirement gate PASSED" is the closest existing analogue to this checklist. That gate is a human-reviewed binary; forge's proving-ground replaces it with an automated 9-criterion checklist. Keeping both is fine — SCORE.md remains agent-reported self-scoring, forge-proving-ground is externally enforced.
- The historical record does not tell us whether any of v7..v10 would actually pass PG-07 (player moves on input). That is the single most load-bearing criterion and it is currently `?` for every historical generation. The first forge-spawned run that passes it sets the baseline.

    <content><![CDATA[
# app-forge — Project Overview

## What This Is

A local-first app and game generation studio. Captures user requirements through a structured intake UI, one-shots a greenfield generation from those requirements, and lets the user persist, fork, and lineage-trace generations. Each generation is a real worktree with files the user can edit through an embedded file-folder editor and an embedded terminal that runs Claude/Codex CLIs to spawn child generations ("broods"). Forge is itself the standard way to build apps in this monorepo — it dogfoods its own editor to build itself.

## Core Value

A user can describe what they want, get a one-shot generation, and then iterate on it through child generations that inherit code/content fr
... [truncated 5206 bytes] ...
ld generations — generations persist by design; user manages worktree lifecycle
- Centralized user account system — local-first, no accounts in v1
- Mobile-first design — desktop-only

## Traceability

| Requirement | Phase |
|-------------|-------|
| GEN-02, MKT-01 | 01 |
| REQ-01, REQ-02, REQ-03 | 02 |
| GEN-01, GEN-02, GEN-03 | 03 |
| EDIT-01, EDIT-02, EDIT-03, EDIT-04 | 04 |
| TERM-01, TERM-02 | 05 |
| GEN-04, GEN-05, TERM-03, TERM-04 | 06 |
| MKT-01, MKT-02, MKT-03 | 07 |
| DOG-01 | 08 |

Coverage: 18/18 v1 requirements mapped to phases.
]]></content>
  </doc>
</planning-references>

<task id="42.4-15" type="framework" status="planned"><goal>Project ⊇ Species inheritance contract in the CLI eval loader. `project.json` declares defaults: `techStack`, `contextFramework`, `installedSkills`, `defaultContent` (paths or refs to default assets/files/scaffolding). `species.json` may override any of those + adds species-only fields: `inherits_from` (parent species for cross-species lineage), `dna` (workflow + skill manifest summary), species-specific `description`. The CLI's eval loaders (used by `gad eval list/run/preserve` and by build-site-data.mjs) MUST merge project + species at load time and return the merged shape — readers should never manually combine them. Add typed interface, merge function, and tests for: (a) species inherits all project fields when species.json is empty, (b) species overrides individual fields cleanly, (c) species can add species-only fields without affecting project defaults, (d) `inherits_from` resolves a chain across species within the same project. Decision forge-10 captures the rationale. This is what the forge editor's "edit project settings" vs "edit species settings" UX is built on.</goal>
      <keywords>schema,inheritance,project-species,merge,loader,forge-10</keywords>
    </task>
inputs.new_string:
<task id="42.4-15" type="framework" status="done" skill="default" agent="general-purpose-subagent"><goal>Project ⊇ Species inheritance contract in the CLI eval loader. `project.json` declares defaults: `techStack`, `contextFramework`, `installedSkills`, `defaultContent` (paths or refs to default assets/files/scaffolding). `species.json` may override any of those + adds species-only fields: `inherits_from` (parent species for cross-species lineage), `dna` (workflow + skill manifest summary), species-specific `description`. The CLI's eval loaders (used by `gad eval list/run/preserve` and by build-site-data.mjs) MUST merge project + species at load time and return the merged shape — readers should never manually combine them. Shipped: new `lib/eval-loader.cjs` exporting `loadProject`, `loadSpecies`, `loadAllSpeciesRaw`, `loadResolvedSpecies`, `loadAllResolvedSpecies`, `mergeProjectSpecies`, `resolveInheritanceChain`. Merge semantics per decision gad-184: project defaults → inherits_from chain (deepest ancestor first) → target species; last write wins; arrays REPLACE not merge; species-only fields (inherits_from, dna, description) do not back-propagate to project; cycles throw. Wired `bin/gad.cjs eval list` to the loader and swapped `site/scripts/build-site-data.mjs scanEvalProjects` to use `loadResolvedSpecies` so the site sees the merged shape. Hermetic tests in `tests/eval-loader-inheritance.test.cjs` cover (a)-(e) plus loadAllResolvedSpecies + pure-function spot check (7/7 pass via `node --test`). Other legacy `gad.json` reads in bin/gad.cjs (eval run, eval verify, eval review, eval open/report) are orthogonal rename fallout and left for a separate surgical pass.</goal>
      <keywords>schema,inheritance,project-species,merge,loader,forge-10,gad-184</keywords>
    </task>

Multi-root eval discovery shipped (42.4-12): `[[evals.roots]]` in `gad-config.toml` mirrors `[[planning.roots]]`; new helpers `getEvalRoots` / `listAllEvalProjects` / `resolveEvalProject` in `bin/gad.cjs` rewire every eval subcommand to walk the union of configured roots plus the implicit submodule default, with load-time duplicate-id detection; `apps/forge/evals/` registered as the first non-default root so forge can host its own evals outside the framework submodule. Forge proving-ground spec shipped (42.4-16): `apps/forge/.planning/plans/forge-proving-ground.md` defines PG-01..PG-09 observable pass criteria (build preserved, clean headless load, zero console.error, game root mounts, player renders, tilemap renders, input moves player, no unhandled rejections, probe runtime < 30s) with a 12-row historical comparison table retroactively scoring all ETD gad-species generations — 4 of 12 (v7/v8/v9/v10) have preserved builds, exactly 1 (v8) has an explicit SCORE.md "Build requirement gate PASSED" annotation, and PG-02..PG-08 are `?` across the board because no mechanical probe has ever run against an ETD build. DOG-02 in forge `REQUIREMENTS.xml` and phase 08 success criteria in forge `ROADMAP.xml` now cite the checklist. 42.4-16 is the last of the 42.4-12..16 framework gates blocking app-forge phase 01; only 42.4-15 (Project ⊇ Species inheritance contract in the CLI eval loader) remains before forge phase 01 can spawn. Next logical step is 42.4-15 which will finally normalize project-level `gad.json` into `project.json` and define the merge semantics.</next-action>

Wired `bin/gad.cjs eval list` and `site/scripts/build-site-data.mjs scanEvalProjects` through the loader so CLI, site prebuild, and forge editor all see the same merged shape. All five of the 42.4-12..16 framework gates blocking app-forge phase 01 are now landed; forge phase 01 can spawn.

inputs.old_string:
<decision id="gad-183">
    <title>Proto-skills stage inside the owning project's `.planning/` and install outward into runtimes on demand</title>
    <summary>Proto-skills are not canonical framework assets yet; they are project-local planning artifacts awaiting human review. Policy: the canonical staging directory for proto-skills is `.planning/proto-skills/` inside the owning GAD project, not `skills/proto-skills/`. This keeps drafted proto-skills in the same lifecycle zone as tasks, decisions, snapshots, and workflows until the operator explicitly decides what to do next. From that staged location there are now two explicit outward moves: `gad evolution install <slug>` installs the staged proto-skill into one or more coding-agent runtimes for local testing while keeping it staged; `gad evolution promote <slug>` promotes the staged proto-skill into canonical `skills/` so it joins the framework DNA. Promotion must preserve provenance rather than dropping it.</summary>
    <impact>(1) Live evolution CLI paths move from `skills/proto-skills/` to `.planning/proto-skills/`. (2) `gad evolution install` becomes the fast bridge from planning artifact to runtime test surface, mirroring the existing runtime install conventions (`--claude --codex --cursor --windsurf --augment --copilot --antigravity --all`, local/global, optional config dir). (3) `gad evolution promote` now targets canonical `skills/` and carries the full staged bundle forward, including `PROVENANCE.md`. (4) Candidate generation and proto-skill docs must stop describing proto-skills as a subdirectory of the public skill catalog. (5) Historical candidate/eval artifacts may still mention the old path, but canonical source, CLI help, and planning docs should point at `.planning/proto-skills/`.</impact>
  </decision></decisions>
inputs.new_string:
<decision id="gad-183">
    <title>Proto-skills stage inside the owning project's `.planning/` and install outward into runtimes on demand</title>
    <summary>Proto-skills are not canonical framework assets yet; they are project-local planning artifacts awaiting human review. Policy: the canonical staging directory for proto-skills is `.planning/proto-skills/` inside the owning GAD project, not `skills/proto-skills/`. This keeps drafted proto-skills in the same lifecycle zone as tasks, decisions, snapshots, and workflows until the operator explicitly decides what to do next. From that staged location there are now two explicit outward moves: `gad evolution install <slug>` installs the staged proto-skill into one or more coding-agent runtimes for local testing while keeping it staged; `gad evolution promote <slug>` promotes the staged proto-skill into canonical `skills/` so it joins the framework DNA. Promotion must preserve provenance rather than dropping it.</summary>
    <impact>(1) Live evolution CLI paths move from `skills/proto-skills/` to `.planning/proto-skills/`. (2) `gad evolution install` becomes the fast bridge from planning artifact to runtime test surface, mirroring the existing runtime install conventions (`--claude --codex --cursor --windsurf --augment --copilot --antigravity --all`, local/global, optional config dir). (3) `gad evolution promote` now targets canonical `skills/` and carries the full staged bundle forward, including `PROVENANCE.md`. (4) Candidate generation and proto-skill docs must stop describing proto-skills as a subdirectory of the public skill catalog. (5) Historical candidate/eval artifacts may still mention the old path, but canonical source, CLI help, and planning docs should point at `.planning/proto-skills/`.</impact>
  </decision>
  <decision id="gad-184">
    <title>Project ⊇ Species inheritance contract: merge-at-read-time eval loader</title>
    <summary>Every eval project has one `evals/<project>/project.json` declaring defaults (`techStack`, `co
... [truncated 552 bytes] ...
es`, `loadAllSpeciesRaw`, `loadResolvedSpecies`, `loadAllResolvedSpecies`, `mergeProjectSpecies`, and `resolveInheritanceChain`. Merge semantics are: (1) start from project.json as base; (2) walk `inherits_from` deepest-ancestor first, applying each species on top; (3) apply target species last with last-write-wins; (4) array fields REPLACE, never concat (a future `_extends` convention could opt into merging but is out of scope); (5) species-only fields stay on the resolved shape and never back-propagate to the shared project defaults; (6) cycles throw a clear `inherits_from cycle detected: a -> b -> a` error; (7) unknown parent references throw. Cross-references forge-10 which captured the editor-UX rationale (separate "edit project" vs "edit species" surfaces demand a single canonical merger).</summary>
    <impact>(1) `lib/eval-loader.cjs` is the canonical location for project-species composition; adding or changing merge rules is one file, one function. (2) `bin/gad.cjs eval list` and `site/scripts/build-site-data.mjs scanEvalProjects` now route through the loader, replacing hand-rolled project.json + species.json combinations. (3) Hermetic inheritance tests live at `tests/eval-loader-inheritance.test.cjs` and lock the contract (5 required cases + 2 smoke checks, all green via `node --test`). (4) The forge editor can safely present "edit project settings" vs "edit species settings" as separate surfaces: whatever the operator edits at each level round-trips through the same merge on reload. (5) Other legacy `gad.json` reads inside `bin/gad.cjs` (eval run metadata, eval verify, eval review rubric, eval report) are orthogonal rename fallout from 42.4-14 and should migrate to the loader in a follow-up surgical pass — not refactored here to keep 42.4-15 scoped. (6) Arrays replacing (not concatenating) is a load-bearing choice: callers who want to "extend" a project-level skill list must spell out the full species list explicitly until an `_extends` convention is designed.</impact>
  </decision></decisions>

inputs.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat(framework): project ⊇ species inheritance contract in eval loader (42.4-15)

Add lib/eval-loader.cjs — the canonical merger for evals/<project>/project.json
+ evals/<project>/species/<species>/species.json. Exports loadProject,
loadSpecies, loadAllSpeciesRaw, loadResolvedSpecies, loadAllResolvedSpecies,
mergeProjectSpecies, resolveInheritanceChain.

Merge semantics (decision gad-184):
  1. project.json defaults are the base layer
  2. inherits_from chain walked deepest-ancestor first
  3. target species applied last, last write wins
  4. array fields REPLACE, not concat (no _extends convention yet)
  5. species-only fields (inherits_from, dna, description) stay local and
     never back-propagate to project defaults
  6. cycles throw a clear 'inherits_from cycle detected: a -> b -> a' error

Wire bin/gad.cjs `eval list` through loadEvalProject + loadAllResolvedSpecies
instead of hand-reading gad.json (which no longer exists after 42.4-14). The
site prebuild scanEvalProjects path was already updated in a prior piggybacked
commit (32055ad) and is left as-is.

Hermetic tests at tests/eval-loader-inheritance.test.cjs cover all five
required cases (a-e) plus loadAllResolvedSpecies + mergeProjectSpecies purity
spot checks — 7/7 pass under `node --test`.

Updates TASK-REGISTRY 42.4-15 to done, STATE next-action, DECISIONS gad-184.

Other legacy gad.json reads inside bin/gad.cjs (eval run metadata, eval verify,
eval review rubric, eval report) are orthogonal rename fallout from 42.4-14
and left for a follow-up surgical pass — not refactored here to keep this
change scoped.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

**OUT OF SCOPE**: DO NOT touch `gad projects init`. That is task 42.4-08, running after you, which will consume your definition doc. Your doc is the contract.

   ```

 projects init`."

- Another agent is working on **42.4-10** (sink/docs surface audit) in parallel. They touch `gad sink` / `gad docs` commands and will likely write a different references/ doc. Avoid touching those commands or writing a doc that overlaps their scope.

4. DECISIONS.xml entry is well-formed.

- Canonical optional file count

- Decision id allocated

Be concrete. The audit is the value — an honest "here's what's actually on disk" grid is worth more than a polished definition with no grounding.

It may read the filesystem, trigger `npx` commands, populate eval folders, and rebuild pages via a dev-server-only bridge. We are explicitly NOT shipping a Vercel-hosted API route that runs `npx` or mutates the filesystem in production. Vercel Sandbox could support this later but adds operational weight (cold starts, security boundaries, cost) that is not justified until the local editor proves the concept.     <title>One canonical skill body per public skill name; wrappers and variants must not create duplicate identities in the canonical catalog</title>
    <summary>The current skill tree still contains cases where two different directories declare the same public skill identity (`name:` in frontmatter) with materially different bodies. That is a catalog bug, not healthy optionality: routing becomes ambiguous, install/catalog output becomes misleading, and humans cannot tell which body is authoritative. The upstream create-skill guidance already says "update, don't duplicate," and `merge-skill` exists specifically because overlap is a failure mode. Policy: the canonical `skills/` catalog gets exactly one authoritative body per public skill name. If two skills overlap, either (a) merge them into one canonical skill, (b) rewrite one so it is a distinct skill with a distinct public identity, or (c) archive the legacy body outside the canonical shipped catalog. Thin wrappers are allowed only when they have a distinct public identity and a distinct job (`create-proto-skill` vs a neutral upstream `create-skill` is valid; two different bodies both named `gad:plan-phase` is not). Tool/runtime-specific adapters belong in packaging/runtime layers, not as duplicate skill identities.</summary>

<impact>(1) Adopt a naming convention that makes artifact type visually obvious: `skills/gad-<command>/`, `workflows/gad-<command>.md`, `references/<topic>.md`, `templates/<topic>.*`. (2) When consolidating duplicate or legacy skill content, salvage ordered procedure into the workflow file and salvage explanatory prose into references; do not keep two SKILL.md bodies for the same public command. (3) Validation should fail if canonical `skills/` contains duplicate public `name:` values or a plain-name alias for a canonical `gad-*` command skill. (4) This also clarifies parser expectations: templates are schemas/scaffolds only, not methodology containers, so catalog builders and future tooling can reason about artifact type from path and role rather than fuzzy content.</impact>

    <title>Canonical framework assets need an automated integrity gate: unique public skill names and resolvable `@...` references</title>
    <summary>Manual grep passes are not enough to keep the framework surface coherent after repeated structural migrations. The canonical asset tree now has enough cross-linking (`skills/` → `workflows/` / `references/` / `templates/`, agent prompts, hook docs, site catalog generation) that two failure modes need to be enforced automatically: (1) duplicate public skill identities in canonical `skills/`, and (2) broken canonical alias references such as `@workflows/...` or `@references/...` inside framework assets. Policy: ship and maintain a repository-level validator that scans the canonical asset roots, fails on duplicate public `name:` values, and fails on broken alias references. Warnings for non-canonical or historical/generated surfaces are acceptable, but canonical roots must stay clean.</summary>
    <impact>(1) `scripts/validate-canonical-assets.mjs` becomes the fast integrity check for framework shape migrations. (2) New command surface: `node scripts/validate-canonical-assets.mjs` and package alias `check:assets`. (3) Future cleanups should pre
... [truncated 2914 bytes] ...
`, `loadAllSpeciesRaw`, `loadResolvedSpecies`, `loadAllResolvedSpecies`, `mergeProjectSpecies`, and `resolveInheritanceChain`. Merge semantics are: (1) start from project.json as base; (2) walk `inherits_from` deepest-ancestor first, applying each species on top; (3) apply target species last with last-write-wins; (4) array fields REPLACE, never concat (a future `_extends` convention could opt into merging but is out of scope); (5) species-only fields stay on the resolved shape and never back-propagate to the shared project defaults; (6) cycles throw a clear `inherits_from cycle detected: a -> b -> a` error; (7) unknown parent references throw. Cross-references forge-10 which captured the editor-UX rationale (separate "edit project" vs "edit species" surfaces demand a single canonical merger).</summary>
    <impact>(1) `lib/eval-loader.cjs` is the canonical location for project-species composition; adding or changing merge rules is one file, one function. (2) `bin/gad.cjs eval list` and `site/scripts/build-site-data.mjs scanEvalProjects` now route through the loader, replacing hand-rolled project.json + species.json combinations. (3) Hermetic inheritance tests live at `tests/eval-loader-inheritance.test.cjs` and lock the contract (5 required cases + 2 smoke checks, all green via `node --test`). (4) The forge editor can safely present "edit project settings" vs "edit species settings" as separate surfaces: whatever the operator edits at each level round-trips through the same merge on reload. (5) Other legacy `gad.json` reads inside `bin/gad.cjs` (eval run metadata, eval verify, eval review rubric, eval report) are orthogonal rename fallout from 42.4-14 and should migrate to the loader in a follow-up surgical pass — not refactored here to keep 42.4-15 scoped. (6) Arrays replacing (not concatenating) is a load-bearing choice: callers who want to "extend" a project-level skill list must spell out the full species list explicitly until an `_extends` convention is designed.</impact>
  </decision></decisions>

# Canonical Planning Root Shape

> **Status:** normative. Source of truth for `gad projects init` (task 42.4-08)
> and `gad projects audit` (task 42.4-09, the "gad health" check).
> **Last reviewed:** 2026-04-14 (task 42.4-09, decision gad-185).

## 1. Purpose

Every GAD-tracked project lives under a *planning root* — a directory containing
a `.planning/` subdirectory populated with a known set of XML planning files and
optional supporting directories. Over the 40+ phases of framework evolution the
"known set" has drifted: some projects have `HUMAN-TODOS.xml`, some have
`phases/`, some have `plans/`, some have `proto-skills/`, some have `notes/`,
and no single file names the canonical shape. `gad projects init` scaffolds a
4-file markdown set that nobody actually uses, while `gad projects audit` only
checks `STATE.xml` + `ROADMAP.xml` as required and treats `REQUIREMENTS.xml`,
`DECISIONS.xml`, and `AGENTS.md` as interchangeable "recommended" files. This
drift surfaced concretely on 2026-04-14 during app-forge scaffolding: `gad
projects init` wrote markdown templates that had to be hand-converted to XML,
and nothing caught the shape mismatch until a human noticed (see
`ERRORS-AND-ATTEMPTS.xml` entry `forge-init-md-misaction-2026-04-14`).

This document pins the canonical minimum file set, the canonical optional file
set, the legacy/deprecated names to avoid, and the minimum valid XML header for
each required file. It is the contract both `gad projects init` and `gad
projects audit` MUST agree on.

## 2. Canonical minimum file set

Every `.planning/` directory MUST contain all of the following files. A planning
root that is missing any of these is "out of canonical shape" and `gad projects
audit` will flag it.

| File | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `STATE.xml` | Current phase, status, and next-action for the project. |
| `ROADMAP.xml` | Ordered phases + milestones. |
| `TASK-REGISTRY.xml` | Task ledger (every task with status, skill, agent, type). |
| `DECISIONS.xml` | Architecture / policy decision log. |

... [truncated 8948 bytes] ...
| gad | global | app-forge | magicborn | grime-time | mb-cli | repub-builder |

| `phases/` | Y | Y | - | - | Y | - | - |
| `plans/` | Y | - | Y | - | - | - | - |

| `workflows/` | Y | - | - | - | Y | - | - |

| `quick/` | - | - | - | - | Y | - | - |
| `skills/` (legacy) | - | - | - | - | Y | - | - |

### 6d. Out-of-canonical-shape flags

- **`grime-time`** — has legacy project-local `skills/` directory (decision
  gad-183 deprecated this in favor of `.planning/proto-skills/`). Remediation:
  move or archive. Otherwise canonical-minimum-clean.
- **`gad` (framework) and `global`** — have legacy `config.json` inside
  `.planning/`. Remediation: confirm no current CLI path reads it, then remove.
- **`magicborn`, `mb-cli-framework`, `repub-builder`** — missing both optional
  `REQUIREMENTS.xml` and `ERRORS-AND-ATTEMPTS.xml`. Not a shape violation (both
  are optional), but flagged as "thin" — these projects may benefit from
  capturing scope and negative results explicitly. Informational only.
- No root contains `PROJECT.xml`. Consistent with its optional status in §3.
- No root contains a legacy markdown `*.md` variant of the canonical XML set.
  The hand-converted forge scaffolding is the only known recent drift and has
  already been corrected.

## 7. Change log

| Date | Task | Change |
|---|---|---|
| 2026-04-14 | 42.4-09 | Initial version. Defined minimum (4 files), optional files + dirs, legacy list, per-file headers, and audit matrix. Decision gad-185. |

inputs.content:
# Sink MD → XML Conversion Audit (task 42.4-10)

## 1. Question

Does any existing `gad` command convert human-authored markdown planning prose
(`PROJECT.md`, `REQUIREMENTS.md`, `ROADMAP.md`, `STATE.md`) into the canonical
XML planning files (`STATE.xml`, `ROADMAP.xml`, `REQUIREMENTS.xml`,
`TASK-REGISTRY.xml`, `DECISIONS.xml`) that the rest of the framework reads?
Short answer: **no**. The sink surface is MD/XML-tolerant on the way *out* to
MDX, XML-only on the way *in* from MDX, and nothing in the tree parses MD
prose into the canonical XML shape. Details below.

## 2. Command surface audited

Read directly from `bin/gad.cjs` and `lib/docs-compiler.cjs` (not help text).

- **`gad docs compile`** (`bin/gad.cjs:1650`) — iterates configured roots, calls
  the same `compileDocs` path as `gad sink compile` under the hood. Planning
  root → MDX in `docs_sink`. One-way, source → sink.
- **`gad docs list`** (`bin/gad.cjs:1709`) — read-only enumeration of planning
  files, DOCS-MAP entries, and sink feature-docs. Does not transform.
- **`gad sink status`** (`bin/gad.cjs:7052`) — stat-based comparison between
  each `SINK_SOURCE_MAP` entry in `.planning/` and the compiled MDX. Reports
  `missing | stale | human-authored | ok`. No writes.
- **`gad sink diff`** (`bin/gad.cjs:7094`) — calls `diffSink()` in
  `lib/docs-compiler.cjs`, produces a unified diff between what `compile`
  *would* write and what's on disk in the sink. No writes.
- **`gad sink compile`** (`bin/gad.cjs:7132`) — delegates to
  `lib/docs-compiler.cjs:compile()`. For each `SOURCE_MAP` row it picks the
  first existing file among `[STATE.xml, STATE.md]`, etc. If the hit is `.xml`
  it runs `xmlToMdBody()` (dedicated per-file converters: `stateXmlToMd`,
  `roadmapXmlToMd`, `decisionsXmlToMd`, `taskRegistryXmlToMd`,
  `requirementsXmlToMd`, `errorsXmlToMd`, `blockersXmlToMd`). If the hit is
  `.md` it just calls `stripFrontmatter()` and emits the body verbatim wrapped
  in MDX frontmatter. **Nothing parses the MD into XML.**
- **`gad sink syn
... [truncated 7040 bytes] ...
0
LOC "extend decompile" budget the task goal floated — which is further
evidence this should be a new subcommand, not an extension. Do **not**
implement in 42.4-10; this is a spec for a future focused task.

Open design question for the follow-up:
- Do we want hydrate to be bidirectional-safe (i.e. after hydrate, can you
  `migrate-schema` back without loss)? Only relevant if the MD is
  round-trippable.
- Does `gad projects init` (42.4-08) want to call hydrate as a final step so
  markdown scaffolding users can get XML for free? Probably yes.

## 7. Related tasks

- **42.4-08** (`gad projects init`) — will benefit from hydrate when a user
  scaffolds a new project from MD templates. Not blocking, can wait.
- **42.4-09** (canonical project shape audit) — parallel; establishes which
  files *must* exist. Hydrate produces exactly that set.
- **42.4-11** (sink-pipeline coherence doc) — will cite this audit as the
  reference for "what MD→XML gap exists" and reference the hydrate spec as
  the planned fix. This doc stays narrow on the conversion gap; 42.4-11 owns
  the full pipeline map.

## Surprises from the audit

1. `sink decompile` is misnamed. It does not decompile. It creates empty
   shells. If a new developer read the help text they would expect it to read
   MDX and emit XML — it does neither. Worth a rename or a doc fix regardless
   of whether hydrate ships. (Not in scope for this task, flag for 42.4-11.)
2. `sink compile` silently accepts MD input and degrades to pass-through.
   There's no warning that "this MD file won't be queryable by `gad state` /
   `gad tasks` / etc." — the user just gets MDX in the sink and silence from
   the readers. A `gad health` warning (42.4-09's territory) for "MD present,
   XML missing" would be a cheap win.
3. `migrate-schema` archives XML after conversion. Running it against the
   decompile-generated empty stubs actively destroyed them (archived to
   `archive/xml/`). If anyone ever extends `decompile` to do real work, that
   interaction is a footgun.

<task id="42.4-10" type="framework" status="planned"><goal>Audit existing `gad sink` and `gad docs` command surface for the markdown→XML conversion gap before adding new commands. CORRECTION 2026-04-14: original framing of this task assumed no conversion path existed and proposed `gad migrate-schema --from md --to xml`. User pointed out we already have `gad docs compile`, `gad sink compile/decompile/sync/diff/status/validate` — bidirectional sink flow exists. `gad sink decompile` specifically "ensures .planning/ dirs exist for all projects; creates stubs for missing source files" which is close to but not identical to the MD→XML conversion needed. Audit: does any existing command convert markdown PROJECT/REQUIREMENTS/ROADMAP/STATE prose into canonical XML? If yes, document it and close this task. If no, decide whether to extend `gad sink decompile` to handle the case or add a focused subcommand. Don't add new commands until we've ruled out the existing surface.</goal>
      <keywords>cli,sink,docs,convert,md-to-xml,audit,decompile</keywords>
    </task>
inputs.new_string:
<task id="42.4-10" type="framework" status="done" skill="default" agent="general-purpose-subagent"><goal>Audit existing `gad sink` and `gad docs` command surface for the markdown→XML conversion gap before adding new commands. CORRECTION 2026-04-14: original framing of this task assumed no conversion path existed and proposed `gad migrate-schema --from md --to xml`. User pointed out we already have `gad docs compile`, `gad sink compile/decompile/sync/diff/status/validate` — bidirectional sink flow exists. `gad sink decompile` specifically "ensures .planning/ dirs exist for all projects; creates stubs for missing source files" which is close to but not identical to the MD→XML conversion needed. Audit: does any existing command convert markdown PROJECT/REQUIREMENTS/ROADMAP/STATE prose into canonical XML? If yes, document it and close this task. If no, decide whether to extend `gad sink decompile` to handle the case or add a focused subcommand. Don't add new commands until we've ruled out the existing surface.</goal>
      <keywords>cli,sink,docs,convert,md-to-xml,audit,decompile</keywords>
      <resolution>Determination (C) — no existing command handles it. `sink compile` reads MD but passes it through into MDX without structuring; `sink decompile` creates empty XML shells (name is misleading, it does not read sink content); `migrate-schema` goes XML→MD, opposite direction. Verified by running all three against a temp dir with a markdown-only planning root; zero commands transferred MD prose into canonical XML. Full audit + spec for new `gad planning hydrate` subcommand (spec only, not implemented) in references/sink-md-xml-audit.md. Extending `sink decompile` was rejected because its documented invariant "never overwrites existing source files" conflicts with the useful MD→XML case. Follow-up task needed to implement hydrate (~150 LOC).</resolution>
    </task>

docs(framework): 42.4-10 audit sink MD→XML gap — determination (C)

because its documented invariant "never overwrites existing source files"
conflicts with the useful MD→XML case.

Also flagged for 42.4-11: `sink decompile` should be renamed or doc-fixed,
and `sink compile` should warn when it falls back to MD pass-through.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -20

{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"C:/Users/benja/Documents/custom_portfolio/vendor/get-anything-done/.planning/DECISIONS.xml","content":"    <title>Project ⊇ Species inheritance contract: merge-at-read-time eval loader</title>
    <summary>Every eval project has one `evals/<project>/project.json` declaring defaults (`techStack`, `contextFramework`, `installedSkills`, `defaultContent`, domain/description/rubric metadata) and one `evals/<project>/species/<species>/species.json` per species. Species may override any project field and also carry species-only fields (`inherits_from`, `dna`, species-specific `description`). Readers — CLI (`gad eval list/run/preserve`), the site prebuild (`build-site-data.mjs`), and the forge editor — MUST NOT hand-merge project + species. Policy: all access goes through `lib/eval-loader.cjs` which exposes `loadProject`, `loadSpecies`, `loadAllSpeciesRaw`, `loadResolvedSpecies`, `loadAllResolvedSpecies`, `mergeProjectSpecies`, and `resolveInheritanceChain`. Merge semantics are: (1) start from project.json as base; (2) walk `inherits_from` deepest-ancestor first, applying each species on top; (3) apply target species last with last-write-wins; (4) array fields REPLACE, never concat (a future `_extends` convention could opt into merging but is out of scope); (5) species-only fields stay on the resolved shape and never back-propagate to the shared project defaults; (6) cycles throw a clear `inherits_from cycle detected: a -> b -> a` error; (7) unknown parent references throw. Cross-references forge-10 which captured the editor-UX rationale (separate "edit project" vs "edit species" surfaces demand a single canonical merger).</summary>
    <impact>(1) `lib/eval-loader.cjs` is the canonical location for project-species composition; adding or changing merge rules is one file, one function. (2) `bin/gad.cjs eval list` and `site/scripts/build-site-data.mjs scanEvalProjects` now route through the loader, replacing hand-rolled project.json + species.json combinations. (3) Hermetic inheritance tests live at `tests/eval-loader-inheritance.test.cjs` and lock the contract (5 required cases + 2 smoke checks, all green via `node --test`). (4) The forge editor can safely present "edit project settings" vs "edit species settings" as separate surfaces: whatever the operator edits at each level round-trips through the same merge on reload. (5) Other legacy `gad.json` reads inside `bin/gad.cjs` (eval run metadata, eval verify, eval review rubric, eval report) are orthogonal rename fallout from 42.4-14 and should migrate to the loader in a follow-up surgical pass — not refactored here to keep 42.4-15 scoped. (6) Arrays replacing (not concatenating) is a load-bearing choice: callers who want to "extend" a project-level skill list must spell out the full species list explicitly until an `_extends` convention is designed.</impact>
  </decision></decisions>

</decision>
  <decision id="gad-185">
    <title>Canonical planning-root shape: minimum file set, optional set, legacy list, and audit binding</title>
    <summary>Every GAD planning root (`[[planning.roots]]` in `gad-config.toml`) has a canonical shape. The canonical MINIMUM file set that every `.planning/` directory MUST contain is four files: `STATE.xml`, `ROADMAP.xml`, `TASK-REGISTRY.xml`, `DECISIONS.xml`. This is the "everyone can run `gad snapshot`/`state`/`tasks`/`decisions` against it" bar and is present in 7/7 audited roots. The canonical OPTIONAL file set is `REQUIREMENTS.xml`, `ERRORS-AND-ATTEMPTS.xml`, `HUMAN-TODOS.xml`, `BLOCKERS.xml`, `PROJECT.xml` (reserved for future identity-file standardization — currently unused by any root), `DOCS-MAP.xml`, `AGENTS.md`, `CONVENTIONS.md`, `README.md`, plus the directories `phases/`, `plans/`, `proto-skills/`, `workflows/`, `notes/`, `specs/`, `todos/`, `debug/`, `reports/`, `sessions/`, `codebase/`, `docs/`, `archive/`, `templates/`, `context-frameworks/`, `quick/`, and transient `.eval-runs/`. The LEGACY/deprecated list that `gad projects audit` should warn on: `gad.json` (→ `species.json`, phase 43 / 42.4-14), `PROJECT.md`/`STATE.md`/`ROADMAP.md`/`DECISIONS.md`/`REQUIREMENTS.md` (→ canonical XML equivalents), `config.json` inside `.planning/` (superseded by repo-root `gad-config.toml`), `REPOPLANNER-TO-GAD-MIGRATION-GAPS.md` (one-shot migration note), and project-local `skills/` as a proto-skill staging dir (→ `.planning/proto-skills/` per decision gad-183). The definition lives at `vendor/get-anything-done/references/project-shape.md` with per-file minimum XML headers, the full audit matrix across all seven registered roots, and flags for out-of-canonical shape (grime-time has legacy `skills/`; `gad`/`global` have legacy `config.json`; magicborn/mb-cli/repub-builder are "thin" — missing optional `REQUIREMENTS.xml` and `ERRORS-AND-ATTEMPTS.xml` but still canonical-minimum-clean). That references doc is the contract that both `gad projects init` (task 42.4-08, not yet shipped) and `gad projects audit` (task 42.4-09, this task) agree on.</summary>
    <impact>(1) `bin/gad.cjs projects audit` now computes a canonical-shape report per root — new helper `computeCanonicalShape(planDir)` plus three additive checks (`canonical_minimum`, `canonical_optional`, `canonical_legacy`) per root and a new "Canonical planning-root shape" summary block under the existing table, each citing `references/project-shape.md` as the source of truth. Legacy required/recommended checks are kept untouched for backwards compatibility. (2) Task 42.4-08 (`gad projects init` canonical-XML scaffold) now has a concrete contract to implement: the six per-file minimum XML headers in §5 of the references doc are what it MUST write, and the four-file minimum in §2 is what it MUST produce on every invocation regardless of format flag. (3) `PROJECT.xml` is NOT in the canonical minimum — it is present in 0/7 audited roots, so promoting it would immediately flag every project as out-of-canonical. It is left in the optional list with a "reserved for a future phase" note; any future decision that wants to require it must also ship the backfill. (4) The audit command is additive, not a refactor: existing JSON consumers see three new `check` entries per project but no renames or removals. (5) Follow-up cleanup candidates surfaced by the audit: grime-time legacy `skills/` directory, framework + global legacy `config.json`, and three "thin" roots that would benefit from capturing `REQUIREMENTS.xml` / `ERRORS-AND-ATTEMPTS.xml`. None are blocking; all are informational.</impact>
  </decision>
</decisions>
