<task id="42.4-09" type="framework" status="done" skill="default" agent="general-purpose-subagent"><goal>Project shape format review and refresh. The "canonical XML file set" for a planning root has drifted over time (some projects have HUMAN-TODOS.xml, some have phases/, some have plans/, some have proto-skills/, some have notes/). There is no single documented shape that `gad projects init` and `gad health` agree on. Audit the actual file sets across all registered planning roots (global, get-anything-done, magicborn, app-forge, etc.), define the canonical minimum + canonical optional file set, document it in references/, and align both `projects init` and `gad health` against it. Surfaced 2026-04-14 by the user during app-forge scaffolding: "the project shape format needs to be reviewed/updated soon."</goal>
      <resolution>Shipped 2026-04-14. Audited all seven registered planning roots (`get-anything-done`, `global`, `app-forge`, `magicborn`, `grime-time`, `mb-cli-framework`, `repub-builder`) by listing every `.planning/` directory and building a present/absent matrix across every file and directory observed. All 7/7 roots are canonical-minimum-clean on the new four-file bar (`STATE.xml`, `ROADMAP.xml`, `TASK-REGISTRY.xml`, `DECISIONS.xml`) — no backfill needed. Wrote `vendor/get-anything-done/references/project-shape.md` (normative, 7 sections) defining: purpose + forge-init-md-misaction-2026-04-14 citation, canonical minimum (4 files with explicit note that `PROJECT.xml` is NOT promoted because it is present in 0/7 roots), canonical optional (9 files + 17 directories with per-entry conditions), legacy/deprecated list (8 file names + 1 directory name with deprecation reasons and replacements), per-file minimum valid XML headers for all six scaffold files (what 42.4-08 MUST write), the full audit matrix broken into three sub-tables (minimum / optional files / optional dirs), and out-of-canonical-shape flags (grime-time legacy `skills/`, gad + global legacy `config.json`, and three "thin" roots missing optional REQUIREMENTS/ERRORS-AND-ATTEMPTS). Aligned `gad projects audit` (the "gad health" surface) against the definition doc: new helper `computeCanonicalShape(planDir)` and four canonical-list constants (`CANONICAL_MINIMUM_FILES`, `CANONICAL_OPTIONAL_FILES`, `CANONICAL_LEGACY_FILES`, `CANONICAL_LEGACY_DIRS`) in `bin/gad.cjs`, each citing `references/project-shape.md` as source of truth. The audit command gains three additive per-root checks (`canonical_minimum`, `canonical_optional`, `canonical_legacy`) and a new "Canonical planning-root shape" summary block under the existing table; existing `required_files` / `recommended_files` / `sink_*` checks are untouched for backwards compat. Verification: `gad projects audit --project app-forge` reports `canonical_minimum 4/4 present` + `optional: REQUIREMENTS.xml, ERRORS-AND-ATTEMPTS.xml`; same command for `grime-time` correctly flags `legacy: skills (project-local skill staging is deprecated — use .planning/proto-skills/ per decision gad-183)`. Decision gad-185 captures the full contract. **42.4-08 consumes `references/project-shape.md` as the contract for `gad projects init`** — §5 of that doc is the literal XML each scaffold file must produce, and §2 is the four-file minimum it must write on every invocation regardless of format flag. Out of scope + untouched: `gad projects init` (42.4-08 territory), `gad sink`/`gad docs` (42.4-10 territory), eval loader (42.4-15, already shipped).</resolution>

{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"C:\Users\benja\Documents\custom_portfolio\vendor\get-anything-done\references\project-shape.md","content":"# Canonical Planning Root Shape

> **Status:** normative. Source of truth for `gad projects init` (task 42.4-08)
> and `gad projects audit` (task 42.4-09, the "gad health" check).
> **Last reviewed:** 2026-04-14 (task 42.4-09, decision gad-185).

## 1. Purpose

Every GAD-tracked project lives under a *planning root* — a directory containing
a `.planning/` subdirectory populated with a known set of XML planning files and
optional supporting directories. Over the 40+ phases of framework evolution the
"known set" has drifted: some projects have `HUMAN-TODOS.xml`, some have
`phases/`, some have `plans/`, some have `proto-skills/`, some have `notes/`,
and no single file names the canonical shape. `gad projects init` scaffolds a
4-file markdown set that nobody actually uses, while `gad projects audit` only
checks `STATE.xml` + `ROADMAP.xml` as required and treats `REQUIREMENTS.xml`,
`DECISIONS.xml`, and `AGENTS.md` as interchangeable "recommended" files. This
drift surfaced concretely on 2026-04-14 during app-forge scaffolding: `gad
projects init` wrote markdown templates that had to be hand-converted to XML,
and nothing caught the shape mismatch until a human noticed (see
`ERRORS-AND-ATTEMPTS.xml` entry `forge-init-md-misaction-2026-04-14`).

This document pins the canonical minimum file set, the canonical optional file
set, the legacy/deprecated names to avoid, and the minimum valid XML header for
each required file. It is the contract both `gad projects init` and `gad
projects audit` MUST agree on.

## 2. Canonical minimum file set

Every `.planning/` directory MUST contain all of the following files. A planning
root that is missing any of these is "out of canonical shape" and `gad projects
audit` will flag it.

| File | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `STATE.xml` | Current phase, status, and next-action for the project. |
| `ROADMAP
... [truncated 9496 bytes] ...
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `phases/` | Y | Y | - | - | Y | - | - |
| `plans/` | Y | - | Y | - | - | - | - |
| `proto-skills/` | Y | - | - | - | - | - | - |
| `workflows/` | Y | - | - | - | Y | - | - |
| `notes/` | Y | - | - | - | Y | - | - |
| `specs/` | Y | - | - | - | - | - | - |
| `context-frameworks/` | Y | - | - | - | - | - | - |

{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"C:\Users\benja\Documents\custom_portfolio\vendor\get-anything-done\references\sink-md-xml-audit.md","content":"# Sink MD → XML Conversion Audit (task 42.4-10)

## 1. Question

Does any existing `gad` command convert human-authored markdown planning prose
(`PROJECT.md`, `REQUIREMENTS.md`, `ROADMAP.md`, `STATE.md`) into the canonical
XML planning files (`STATE.xml`, `ROADMAP.xml`, `REQUIREMENTS.xml`,
`TASK-REGISTRY.xml`, `DECISIONS.xml`) that the rest of the framework reads?
Short answer: **no**. The sink surface is MD/XML-tolerant on the way *out* to
MDX, XML-only on the way *in* from MDX, and nothing in the tree parses MD
prose into the canonical XML shape. Details below.

## 2. Command surface audited

Read directly from `bin/gad.cjs` and `lib/docs-compiler.cjs` (not help text).

- **`gad docs compile`** (`bin/gad.cjs:1650`) — iterates configured roots, calls
  the same `compileDocs` path as `gad sink compile` under the hood. Planning
  root → MDX in `docs_sink`. One-way, source → sink.
- **`gad docs list`** (`bin/gad.cjs:1709`) — read-only enumeration of planning
  files, DOCS-MAP entries, and sink feature-docs. Does not transform.
- **`gad sink status`** (`bin/gad.cjs:7052`) — stat-based comparison between
  each `SINK_SOURCE_MAP` entry in `.planning/` and the compiled MDX. Reports
  `missing | stale | human-authored | ok`. No writes.
- **`gad sink diff`** (`bin/gad.cjs:7094`) — calls `diffSink()` in
  `lib/docs-compiler.cjs`, produces a unified diff between what `compile`
  *would* write and what's on disk in the sink. No writes.
- **`gad sink compile`** (`bin/gad.cjs:7132`) — delegates to
  `lib/docs-compiler.cjs:compile()`. For each `SOURCE_MAP` row it picks the
  first existing file among `[STATE.xml, STATE.md]`, etc. If the hit is `.xml`
  it runs `xmlToMdBody()` (dedicated per-file converters: `stateXmlToMd`,
  `roadmapXmlToMd`, `decisionsXmlToMd`, `taskRegistryXmlToMd`,
  `requirementsXmlToMd`, `errorsXmlToMd`, `b
... [truncated 7482 bytes] ...
ould be a new subcommand, not an extension. Do **not**
implement in 42.4-10; this is a spec for a future focused task.

Open design question for the follow-up:
- Do we want hydrate to be bidirectional-safe (i.e. after hydrate, can you
  `migrate-schema` back without loss)? Only relevant if the MD is
  round-trippable.
- Does `gad projects init` (42.4-08) want to call hydrate as a final step so
  markdown scaffolding users can get XML for free? Probably yes.

## 7. Related tasks

- **42.4-08** (`gad projects init`) — will benefit from hydrate when a user
  scaffolds a new project from MD templates. Not blocking, can wait.
- **42.4-09** (canonical project shape audit) — parallel; establishes which
  files *must* exist. Hydrate produces exactly that set.
- **42.4-11** (sink-pipeline coherence doc) — will cite this audit as the
  reference for "what MD→XML gap exists" and reference the hydrate spec as
  the planned fix. This doc stays narrow on the conversion gap; 42.4-11 owns
  the full pipeline map.

## Surprises from the audit

1. `sink decompile` is misnamed. It does not decompile. It creates empty
   shells. If a new developer read the help text they would expect it to read
   MDX and emit XML — it does neither. Worth a rename or a doc fix regardless
   of whether hydrate ships. (Not in scope for this task, flag for 42.4-11.)
2. `sink compile` silently accepts MD input and degrades to pass-through.
   There's no warning that "this MD file won't be queryable by `gad state` /
   `gad tasks` / etc." — the user just gets MDX in the sink and silence from
   the readers. A `gad health` warning (42.4-09's territory) for "MD present,
   XML missing" would be a cheap win.
3. `migrate-schema` archives XML after conversion. Running it against the
   decompile-generated empty stubs actively destroyed them (archived to
   `archive/xml/`). If anyone ever extends `decompile` to do real work, that
   interaction is a footgun.

{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"C:\Users\benja\Documents\custom_portfolio\vendor\get-anything-done\references\project-shape.md","content":"# Canonical Planning Root Shape

> **Status:** normative. Source of truth for `gad projects init` (task 42.4-08)
> and `gad projects audit` (task 42.4-09, the "gad health" check).
> **Last reviewed:** 2026-04-14 (task 42.4-09, decision gad-185).

## 1. Purpose

Every GAD-tracked project lives under a *planning root* — a directory containing
a `.planning/` subdirectory populated with a known set of XML planning files and
optional supporting directories. Over the 40+ phases of framework evolution the
"known set" has drifted: some projects have `HUMAN-TODOS.xml`, some have
`phases/`, some have `plans/`, some have `proto-skills/`, some have `notes/`,
and no single file names the canonical shape. `gad projects init` scaffolds a
4-file markdown set that nobody actually uses, while `gad projects audit` only
checks `STATE.xml` + `ROADMAP.xml` as required and treats `REQUIREMENTS.xml`,
`DECISIONS.xml`, and `AGENTS.md` as interchangeable "recommended" files. This
drift surfaced concretely on 2026-04-14 during app-forge scaffolding: `gad
projects init` wrote markdown templates that had to be hand-converted to XML,
and nothing caught the shape mismatch until a human noticed (see
`ERRORS-AND-ATTEMPTS.xml` entry `forge-init-md-misaction-2026-04-14`).

This document pins the canonical minimum file set, the canonical optional file
set, the legacy/deprecated names to avoid, and the minimum valid XML header for
each required file. It is the contract both `gad projects init` and `gad
projects audit` MUST agree on.

## 2. Canonical minimum file set

Every `.planning/` directory MUST contain all of the following files. A planning
root that is missing any of these is "out of canonical shape" and `gad projects
audit` will flag it.

| File | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `STATE.xml` | Current phase, status, and next-action for the project. |
| `ROADMAP
... [truncated 9496 bytes] ...
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `phases/` | Y | Y | - | - | Y | - | - |
| `plans/` | Y | - | Y | - | - | - | - |
| `proto-skills/` | Y | - | - | - | - | - | - |
| `workflows/` | Y | - | - | - | Y | - | - |
| `notes/` | Y | - | - | - | Y | - | - |
| `specs/` | Y | - | - | - | - | - | - |
| `context-frameworks/` | Y | - | - | - | - | - | - |
| `docs/` | Y | - | - | - | - | - | - |
| `archive/` | Y | - | - | - | - | - | - |
| `codebase/` | - | Y | - | - | - | - | - |
| `debug/` | - | Y | - | - | Y | - | - |
| `reports/` | - | Y | - | - | Y | - | - |
| `sessions/` | - | Y | - | - | - | - | - |
| `templates/` | - | - | - | - | Y | - | - |
| `todos/` | - | - | - | - | Y | - | - |
| `quick/` | - | - | - | - | Y | - | - |
| `skills/` (legacy) | - | - | - | - | Y | - | - |

### 6d. Out-of-canonical-shape flags

- **`grime-time`** — has legacy project-local `skills/` directory (decision
  gad-183 deprecated this in favor of `.planning/proto-skills/`). Remediation:
  move or archive. Otherwise canonical-minimum-clean.
- **`gad` (framework) and `global`** — have legacy `config.json` inside
  `.planning/`. Remediation: confirm no current CLI path reads it, then remove.
- **`magicborn`, `mb-cli-framework`, `repub-builder`** — missing both optional
  `REQUIREMENTS.xml` and `ERRORS-AND-ATTEMPTS.xml`. Not a shape violation (both
  are optional), but flagged as "thin" — these projects may benefit from
  capturing scope and negative results explicitly. Informational only.
- No root contains `PROJECT.xml`. Consistent with its optional status in §3.
- No root contains a legacy markdown `*.md` variant of the canonical XML set.
  The hand-converted forge scaffolding is the only known recent drift and has
  already been corrected.

## 7. Change log

| Date | Task | Change |
|---|---|---|
| 2026-04-14 | 42.4-09 | Initial version. Defined minimum (4 files), optional files + dirs, legacy list, per-file headers, and audit matrix. Decision gad-185. |

name: gad:plan-phase
description: Create detailed phase plan (PLAN.md) with verification loop
argument-hint: "[phase] [--auto] [--research] [--skip-research] [--gaps] [--skip-verify] [--prd <file>] [--reviews] [--text]"
agent: gad-planner
allowed-tools:
  - Read
  - Write
  - Bash
  - Glob
  - Grep
  - Task
  - WebFetch
  - mcp__context7__*
---
<objective>
Create executable phase prompts (PLAN.md files) for a roadmap phase with integrated research and verification.

**Default flow:** Research (if needed) → Plan → Verify → Done

**Orchestrator role:** Parse arguments, validate phase, research domain (unless skipped), spawn gad-planner, verify with gad-plan-checker, iterate until pass or max iterations, present results.
</objective>

<execution_context>
@workflows/gad-plan-phase.md
@references/ui-brand.md
</execution_context>

<context>
Phase number: $ARGUMENTS (optional — auto-detects next unplanned phase if omitted)

**Flags:**
- `--research` — Force re-research even if RESEARCH.md exists
- `--skip-research` — Skip research, go straight to planning
- `--gaps` — Gap closure mode (reads VERIFICATION.md, skips research)
- `--skip-verify` — Skip verification loop
- `--prd <file>` — Use a PRD/acceptance criteria file instead of discuss-phase. Parses requirements into CONTEXT.md automatically. Skips discuss-phase entirely.
- `--reviews` — Replan incorporating cross-AI review feedback from REVIEWS.md (produced by `/gad:review`)
- `--text` — Use plain-text numbered lists instead of TUI menus (required for `/rc` remote sessions)

Normalize phase input in step 2 before any directory lookups.
</context>

<process>
Execute the plan-phase workflow from @workflows/gad-plan-phase.md end-to-end.
Preserve all workflow gates (validation, research, planning, verification loop, routing).
</process>

{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"C:\Users\benja\Documents\custom_portfolio\vendor\get-anything-done\skills\gad-execute-phase\SKILL.md","content":"---
name: gad:execute-phase
description: Execute all plans in a phase with wave-based parallelization
argument-hint: "<phase-number> [--wave N] [--gaps-only] [--interactive]"
allowed-tools:
  - Read
  - Write
  - Edit
  - Glob
  - Grep
  - Bash
  - Task
  - TodoWrite
  - AskUserQuestion
---
<objective>
Execute all plans in a phase using wave-based parallel execution.

Orchestrator stays lean: discover plans, analyze dependencies, group into waves, spawn subagents, collect results. Each subagent loads the full execute-plan context and handles its own plan.

Optional wave filter:
- `--wave N` executes only Wave `N` for pacing, quota management, or staged rollout
- phase verification/completion still only happens when no incomplete plans remain after the selected wave finishes

Flag handling rule:
- The optional flags documented below are available behaviors, not implied active behaviors
- A flag is active only when its literal token appears in `$ARGUMENTS`
- If a documented flag is absent from `$ARGUMENTS`, treat it as inactive

Context budget: ~15% orchestrator, 100% fresh per subagent.
</objective>

<execution_context>
@workflows/gad-execute-phase.md
@references/ui-brand.md
</execution_context>

<context>
Phase: $ARGUMENTS

**Available optional flags (documentation only — not automatically active):**
- `--wave N` — Execute only Wave `N` in the phase. Use when you want to pace execution or stay inside usage limits.
- `--gaps-only` — Execute only gap closure plans (plans with `gap_closure: true` in frontmatter). Use after verify-work creates fix plans.
- `--interactive` — Execute plans sequentially inline (no subagents) with user checkpoints between tasks. Lower token usage, pair-programming style. Best for small phases, bug fixes, and verification gaps.

**Active flags must be derived from `$ARGUMENTS`:**
- `--wave N` is active only if the literal `--wave` token is present in `$ARGUMENTS`
- `--gaps-only` is active only if the literal `--gaps-only` token is present in `$ARGUMENTS`
- `--interactive` is active only if the literal `--interactive` token is present in `$ARGUMENTS`
- If none of these tokens appear, run the standard full-phase execution flow with no flag-specific filtering
- Do not infer that a flag is active just because it is documented in this prompt

Context files are resolved inside the workflow via `gad-tools init execute-phase` and per-subagent `<files_to_read>` blocks.
</context>

<process>
Execute the execute-phase workflow from @workflows/gad-execute-phase.md end-to-end.
Preserve all workflow gates (wave execution, checkpoint handling, verification, state updates, routing).
</process>

# Sink Pipeline — Coherence Map

> **Status:** normative. Pipeline map for the full `.planning/` → docs sink → site flow.
> **Last reviewed:** 2026-04-14 (task 42.4-11).
> **Companion docs:** `references/sink-md-xml-audit.md` (per-command behavior),
> `references/project-shape.md` (canonical file set).

## 1. Purpose

This doc is the **coherence map** for GAD's planning compilation pipeline. It
answers "which surface do I edit when?" at the granularity of a single command
per concrete task. It complements `sink-md-xml-audit.md` (which catalogs what
each `gad sink` / `gad docs` command *actually does*, including the broken
parts) and `project-shape.md` (which pins the canonical `.planning/` file set).
Intended reader: an agent who just ran `gad snapshot` and now needs to change
planning state without accidentally editing a derived artifact.

## 2. Source of truth

**XML files under `.planning/` are authoritative. Everything else is derived.**
MDX in `docs/<id>/planning/` is a compiled view for humans and the site. Static
sites under `dist/site/` (from `gad site compile`) are a further derived
surface. There is no supported reverse flow: `sink-md-xml-audit.md`
determination **(C)** establishes that no command parses MDX or MD prose back
into canonical XML — `sink decompile` only creates empty shells, and

# Sink Pipeline — Coherence Map

> **Status:** normative. Pipeline map for the full `.planning/` → docs sink → site flow.
> **Last reviewed:** 2026-04-14 (task 42.4-11).
> **Companion docs:** `references/sink-md-xml-audit.md` (per-command behavior),
> `references/project-shape.md` (canonical file set).

## 1. Purpose

This doc is the **coherence map** for GAD's planning compilation pipeline. It
answers "which surface do I edit when?" at the granularity of a single command
per concrete task. It complements `sink-md-xml-audit.md` (which catalogs what
each `gad sink` / `gad docs` command *actually does*, including the broken
parts) and `project-shape.md` (which pins the canonical `.planning/` file set).
Intended reader: an agent who just ran `gad snapshot` and now needs to change
planning state without accidentally editing a derived artifact.

## 2. Source of truth

**XML files under `.planning/` are authoritative. Everything else is derived.**
MDX in `docs/<id>/planning/` is a compiled view for humans and the site. Static
sites under `dist/site/` (from `gad site compile`) are a further derived
surface. There is no supported reverse flow: `sink-md-xml-audit.md`
determination **(C)** establishes that no command parses MDX or MD prose back

Default format is now `xml` and scaffolds six files: the canonical minimum (`STATE.xml`, `ROADMAP.xml`, `TASK-REGISTRY.xml`, `DECISIONS.xml`) plus the two strongly-recommended optionals called out in the task goal (`REQUIREMENTS.xml`, `ERRORS-AND-ATTEMPTS.xml`). Each template is a literal string from project-shape.md §5 with project id and today's date substituted — no `{{placeholder}}` tokens leak. `STATE.xml` seeds `status=active`, empty `current-phase`, and `next-action="Project initialized. Define requirements."`; `ROADMAP.xml` seeds a single `phase id="00" status="planned"`; `TASK-REGISTRY`/`DECISIONS`/`ERRORS-AND-ATTEMPTS` are empty roots; `REQUIREMENTS.xml` includes a TODO comment. Added `--format md` flag that restores the legacy markdown scaffold but still writes `TASK-REGISTRY.xml` / `DECISIONS.xml` / `ERRORS-AND-ATTEMPTS.xml` so the resulting root is canonical-minimum-clean regardless of format. Added `--projectid` arg (falls back to slug of `--name`). Added a pre-flight overwrite check: if any target file already exists, refuse with the blocker list unless `--force` is passed. Verification: `gad projects init --projectid test-xml --name "Test XML"` in a temp dir produced the six XML files, all parse under `fast-xml-parser`, canonical_minimum check is 4/4; re-run without `--force` was refused with the six-file blocker list; re-run with `--force` overwrote cleanly; `--format md` in a fresh temp dir produced the four legacy markdown files plus the three XML ledgers. Incident that prompted this task: ERRORS-AND-ATTEMPTS entry `forge-init-md-misaction-2026-04-14`. No new decision — `gad-185` frames the canonical shape and remains the authoritative reference for the init contract.</resolution>

description: Create detailed phase plan (PLAN.md) with verification loop
argument-hint: "[phase] [--auto] [--research] [--skip-research] [--gaps] [--skip-verify] [--prd <file>] [--reviews] [--text]"

<objective>
Create executable phase prompts (PLAN.md files) for a roadmap phase with integrated research and verification.

**Default flow:** Research (if needed) → Plan → Verify → Done

**Orchestrator role:** Parse arguments, validate phase, research domain (unless skipped), spawn gad-planner, verify with gad-plan-checker, iterate until pass or max iterations, present results.
</objective>

<execution_context>
@workflows/gad-plan-phase.md
@references/ui-brand.md
</execution_context>

<context>
Phase number: $ARGUMENTS (optional — auto-detects next unplanned phase if omitted)

**Flags:**
- `--research` — Force re-research even if RESEARCH.md exists
- `--skip-research` — Skip research, go straight to planning
- `--gaps` — Gap closure mode (reads VERIFICATION.md, skips research)
- `--skip-verify` — Skip verification loop
- `--prd <file>` — Use a PRD/acceptance criteria file instead of discuss-phase. Parses requirements into CONTEXT.md automatically. Skips discuss-phase entirely.
- `--reviews` — Replan incorporating cross-AI review feedback from REVIEWS.md (produced by `/gad:review`)
- `--text` — Use plain-text numbered lists instead of TUI menus (required for `/rc` remote sessions)

Normalize phase input in step 2 before any directory lookups.
</context>

<process>
Execute the plan-phase workflow from @workflows/gad-plan-phase.md end-to-end.
Preserve all workflow gates (validation, research, planning, verification loop, routing).
</process>

<objective>
Execute all plans in a phase using wave-based parallel execution.

Orchestrator stays lean: discover plans, analyze dependencies, group into waves, spawn subagents, collect results. Each subagent loads the full execute-plan context and handles its own plan.

Optional wave filter:
- `--wave N` executes only Wave `N` for pacing, quota management, or staged rollout
- phase verification/completion still only happens when no incomplete plans remain after the selected wave finishes

Flag handling rule:
- The optional flags documented below are available behaviors, not implied active behaviors
- A flag is active only when its literal token appears in `$ARGUMENTS`
- If a documented flag is absent from `$ARGUMENTS`, treat it as inactive

Context budget: ~15% orchestrator, 100% fresh per subagent.
</objective>

@references/ui-brand.md

- `--interactive` — Execute plans sequentially inline (no subagents) with user checkpoints between tasks. Lower token usage, pair-programming style. Best for small phases, bug fixes, and verification gaps.

**Active flags must be derived from `$ARGUMENTS`:**
- `--wave N` is active only if the literal `--wave` token is present in `$ARGUMENTS`
- `--gaps-only` is active only if the literal `--gaps-only` token is present in `$ARGUMENTS`
- `--interactive` is active only if the literal `--interactive` token is present in `$ARGUMENTS`
- If none of these tokens appear, run the standard full-phase execution flow with no flag-specific filtering
- Do not infer that a flag is active just because it is documented in this prompt

Context files are resolved inside the workflow via `gad-tools init execute-phase` and per-subagent `<files_to_read>` blocks.

{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"C:\Users\benja\Documents\custom_portfolio\vendor\get-anything-done\.planning\TASK-REGISTRY.xml","content":"      </commands>
      <keywords>schema,project-shape,canonical-format,health,audit,references,gad-185</keywords>
    </task>
    <task id="42.4-10" type="framework" status="done" skill="default" agent="general-purpose-subagent"><goal>Audit existing `gad sink` and `gad docs` command surface for the markdown→XML conversion gap before adding new commands. CORRECTION 2026-04-14: original framing of this task assumed no conversion path existed and proposed `gad migrate-schema --from md --to xml`. User pointed out we already have `gad docs compile`, `gad sink compile/decompile/sync/diff/status/validate` — bidirectional sink flow exists. `gad sink decompile` specifically "ensures .planning/ dirs exist for all projects; creates stubs for missing source files" which is close to but not identical to the MD→XML conversion needed. Audit: does any existing command convert markdown PROJECT/REQUIREMENTS/ROADMAP/STATE prose into canonical XML? If yes, document it and close this task. If no, decide whether to extend `gad sink decompile` to handle the case or add a focused subcommand. Don't add new commands until we've ruled out the existing surface.</goal>
      <keywords>cli,sink,docs,convert,md-to-xml,audit,decompile</keywords>
      <resolution>Determination (C) — no existing command handles it. `sink compile` reads MD but passes it through into MDX without structuring; `sink decompile` creates empty XML shells (name is misleading, it does not read sink content); `migrate-schema` goes XML→MD, opposite direction. Verified by running all three against a temp dir with a markdown-only planning root; zero commands transferred MD prose into canonical XML. Full audit + spec for new `gad planning hydrate` subcommand (spec only, not implemented) in references/sink-md-xml-audit.md. Extending `sink decompile` was rejected because its documented invariant "never overwrites existing source files" conflicts with the useful MD→XML case. Follow-up task needed to implement hydrate (~150 LOC).</resolution>
    </task>
    <task id="42.4-11" type="framework" status="planned"><goal>Sink coherence investigation: map the full doc compilation pipeline so agents know which surface to edit when. Concern surfaced 2026-04-14 by user: "I am pretty sure we have a planning sink set up. We need to investigate why at some point and make sure all our docs compile to the same place." The repo has `[planning] docs_sink = "docs"` in gad-config.toml, `[[docs.projects]]` and `[[planning.roots]]` registries, `gad docs compile`, `gad sink compile/sync/decompile/diff/status/validate`, plus `gad site compile` (phase 10). It's unclear from the outside which command is canonical for: (a) editing a planning root's ROADMAP/DECISIONS/etc, (b) updating a docs.projects entry, (c) keeping the compiled MDX in `docs/<id>/planning/` in sync with `.planning/` source, (d) what happens when both source and sink drift. Output: a short references/sink-pipeline.md doc explaining the full flow + a quick-reference table mapping "I want to edit X" → "use command Y" → "verify with Z." Then update relevant skills (new-project, plan-phase, execute-phase) to reference it.</goal>","numLines":8,"startLine":1149,"totalLines":1239}}

inputs.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat(framework): default gad projects init to canonical XML scaffold (42.4-08)

Rewrites `gad projects init` against references/project-shape.md §5
(decision gad-185). Default --format is xml and writes the six-file
canonical set:

  STATE.xml / ROADMAP.xml / TASK-REGISTRY.xml / DECISIONS.xml  (minimum)
  REQUIREMENTS.xml / ERRORS-AND-ATTEMPTS.xml                   (strongly recommended)

Each template substitutes real project id and today's date — no literal
{{placeholder}} tokens. STATE.xml seeds status=active and next-action
"Project initialized. Define requirements."; ROADMAP.xml seeds
phase id=00 status=planned; TASK-REGISTRY/DECISIONS/ERRORS-AND-ATTEMPTS
are empty roots; REQUIREMENTS has a TODO comment.

Also adds:

- `--format md` flag for the legacy markdown path (still writes the
  three canonical XML ledgers so the resulting root is audit-clean
  regardless of format)
- `--projectid` arg (falls back to slug of --name)
- Pre-flight overwrite check with `--force` escape hatch
- Post-init next-steps hint pointing at projects audit / discuss-phase
  / plan-phase

Preserves existing gad-config.toml root registration behavior. No new
decision — gad-185 remains the authoritative contract. Incident that
prompted this: ERRORS-AND-ATTEMPTS entry forge-init-md-misaction-2026-04-14.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" && git log -1 --oneline

The launch affordance routes to a dev-only handler that invokes `gad eval run --project <id> --execute` via the phase-44.5 command bridge (or a stub that prints the command when the bridge isn't present yet). Any GAD-based project that vendors this site inherits the same surface automatically — the site is "about the project it is launched from" and its children. First cut: static card grid driven by build-site-data reading the workspace roots; launch button is wired to a placeholder route until 44.5-02 lands. inputs.new_string:
<task id="44-01" type="site" status="done" skill="default" agent="default"><goal>/project-market ProjectCard gains a dev-only Launch eval button that actually runs `gad eval run --project <id> --execute` via a Node child_process spawn from the dev server and streams stdout/stderr back to the browser. POST /api/dev/launch-eval refuses unless NODE_ENV=development. First cut is the live run path; legacy planning-root migration and app-forge build-preservation are split into separate tasks.</goal>
      <keywords>project-market,launcher,child-process,site,dev-bridge</keywords>
    </task>
    <task id="44-02" type="cleanup" status="planned"><goal>Migrate legacy planning roots (grime-time-site, repub-builder, mb-cli-framework, magicborn, global) from planning-only shape into the canonical eval-project shape under `evals/<project>/project.json` + `evals/<project>/species/<species>/species.json` so they appear on /project-market with launch affordance. Per user alignment 2026-04-14: "every project IS an eval project — no separate non-eval card shape." For each root: (1) decide canonical species (likely just one — the project itself), (2) write project.json + species.json with minimum fields (id, name, description, domain, techStack, contextFramework=gad), (3) register under [[evals.roots]] in gad-config.toml if not already, (4) verify it shows up in `gad eval list` and on /project-market. Does not require porting existing planning files — legacy .planning/ trees stay where they are; the eval shape is a thin overlay for launcher purposes.</goal>
      <keywords>migration,eval-shape,planning-roots,project-market</keywords>
    </task>
    <task id="44-03" type="pipeline" status="planned"><goal>App-forge build preservation: today `gad eval verify` flags app-forge v1 MISSING because preservation expects a statically-servable dist/ but forge is a Next.js app producing `.next/` (server components, no output:export). Decide the path: (a) add `output: "export"` to `apps/forge/next.config.mjs` and rewrite any server-only code so `next build` produces `out/`, then teach `gad eval preserve` to copy `out/` for Next.js eval species; (b) skip static preservation for forge entirely and instead preserve a headless Playwright trace / screenshot bundle as the "build artifact"; (c) run forge as a live dev server from the portfolio site when viewing its eval (no static artifact). Capture the decision, update `gad eval preserve` per the chosen path, re-run preservation for app-forge v1, and confirm `gad eval verify` goes green. Also file a runtime-identity record so the preservation audit's other MISSING column clears.</goal>
      <keywords>eval-preservation,next-js,app-forge,gad-eval-verify</keywords>
    </task>
  </phase>

inputs.old_string: <next-action>44-01 shipped 2026-04-14: /project-market ProjectCard now has a dev-only "Launch eval" button (gated on process.env.NODE_ENV === 'development' at build time) that POSTs to new route handler `site/app/api/dev/launch-eval/route.ts`. The route is a stub per user alignment — refuses unless NODE_ENV=development, returns the resolved `gad eval run --project <id> --execute` command string and logs it to server stdout. Phase 44.5-02 will replace the stub body with a real child_process bridge while keeping the same request/response shape. User clarified the mental model: every project IS an eval project — no separate "non-eval" card shape; planning roots like grime-time/repub-builder/mb-cli-framework that don't yet live under evals/ are migration debt, not a different UI. Needs a new task to migrate them into the eval shape. Typecheck clean (5 pre-existing errors unrelated). 43-11 shipped earlier same day: phase 44.5 renamed "Brood Editor" → "Species Editor" with /project-editor + /species-editor routes reserved and Lovable/Bolt visualization scope embedded; phase 45 stripped of "v4 brood" phrasing. Next: (a) migration task for legacy planning roots into eval shape; (b) 43-09 (CLI vocabulary) to close phase 43 cleanup; (c) app-forge v1 `gad eval verify` still flags MISSING build/runtime — preservation followup task still unfiled. Phase 43 context gathered (discuss mode).
inputs.new_string: <next-action>44-01 promoted from stub to live child_process bridge 2026-04-14. `site/app/api/dev/launch-eval/route.ts` now runs `node bin/gad.cjs eval run --project <id> --execute` via `node:child_process.spawn` from the repo root and streams stdout/stderr/exit back as SSE. `ProjectCard.tsx` parses the event-stream, renders stdout (green) / stderr (red) / meta (grey) lines in a scrollable log panel under the card, tracks running/done/err states, and exposes an Abort button that signals the server via AbortController → SIGTERM to the child. NODE_ENV=development gate enforced at both route and button level; prod cannot render or hit it. Strict projectId/species validation (`/^[a-zA-Z0-9_\-./]+$/`, <128 chars) guards against argv injection. Smoke-tested end-to-end on localhost:3000 against `__smoke_test__` (expected "project not found" error path) and `app-forge` (real CLI run → created v3 scaffolding with TRACE.json/EXEC.json/PROMPT.md). Surfaced + fixed `bin/gad.cjs` bug: commit 17043d7 restored eval_type/workflow fields in RUN.md template after 42.4-18 left stray `gadJson` references that crashed every `gad eval run` invocation. New follow-up tasks filed: 44-02 (migrate legacy planning roots grime-time/repub-builder/mb-cli-framework/magicborn/global into canonical eval-project shape per user alignment "every project IS an eval project"), 44-03 (decide app-forge static-export vs alt preservation path — confirmed `.next/` is correct for a Next.js app but forge has no `output: "export"` so nothing statically servable lands under apps/portfolio/public/evals/app-forge/; preservation audit still MISSING). 43-11 shipped earlier same day: phase 44.5 renamed "Brood Editor" → "Species Editor" with /project-editor + /species-editor routes reserved and Lovable/Bolt visualization scope embedded; phase 45 stripped of "v4 brood" phrasing. Next loop pick: 44-02 (migrate legacy roots — fastest win, makes the launcher actually cover the whole workspace) or 44-03 (decide app-forge preservation strategy — unblocks `gad eval verify` green).

-- grime-time-site --
# Grime Time (`grime_time_site`)

Exterior-cleaning marketing site and admin, based on the official Payload **[with-vercel-website](https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/tree/main/templates/with-vercel-website)** template (Next.js App Router, [layout builder](https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/tree/main/templates/with-vercel-website#layout-builder), SEO, forms, drafts).

**Planning & workflows:** [`.planning/`](.planning/) (RepoPlanner). **Roadmap / phases:** [`.planning/ROADMAP.xml`](.planning/ROADMAP.xml), [`.planning/phases/`](.planning/phases/). **CRM:** EngageBay — [`.planning/workflows/crm-and-integrations.md`](.planning/workflows/crm-and-integrations.md), checklist [`.planning/workflows/engagebay-integration-review.md`](.planning/workflows/engagebay-integration-review.md). **Lead process (staff, in portal):** sign in as admin → **Docs** → *Lead to customer runbook* ([`src/content/docs/lead-to-customer-runbook.md`](src/content/docs/lead-to-customer-runbook.md)). **Admin GitHub login:** [`.planning/workflows/supabase-github-admin-auth.md`](.planning/workflows/supabase-github-admin-auth.md). **Content & seeding:** [`.planning/workflows/content-and-seeding.md`](.planning/workflows/content-and-seeding.md). **UX backlog (portal / admin / quotes):** [`.planning/workflows/admin-portal-quotes-ux.md`](.planning/workflows/admin-portal-quotes-ux.md).
-- repub-builder --
# @portfolio/repub-builder

Build **RichEPub** (`.repub`) files from a Vite + React + Tailwind project, and reuse the same reader/runtime package in host apps. The output is a ZIP container with a manifest (`repub.json`), entry HTML, and bundled assets.

## Install
-- mb-cli-framework --
# @magicborn/mb-cli-framework

Shared helpers for **Magicborn operator CLIs** (`magicborn`, vendor CLIs, …): completion tokens, vendor scope, TTY/plain mode, CLI theme tokens, and an optional **terminal chat** built with [assistant-ui for Ink](https://www.assistant-ui.com/docs/ink).

## Install

    </task>

Fixes the Launch eval bug surfaced by user testing and lands the
marketplace polish pass.

"app-forge/gad") so POSTing projectId=project.id routed the CLI to
`gad eval run --project app-forge/gad`, which errored with "project
not found". Handler now resolves `project.project ?? id.split("/")[0]`.

project.id hash + domain lightness, with large monospace initials as a
placeholder-image stand-in. Featured star and species badge move onto
the hero with backdrop blur; run/playable counts appear as a corner
overlay. Tag row expanded to domain + techStack (sky) +
contextFramework (purple) + workflow, deduplicated so we don't repeat
gad/gad/gad. Card body switches to flex col for consistent alignment.

the user raised — today the catalog is prebuild-static and eval runs
between dev-server restarts go invisible on the grid until the next
predev rebuild. Proposed path: GET /api/dev/projects in dev mode that
re-runs the 44-02 multi-root scanner on demand, with production mode
keeping the static catalog.

Typecheck clean. Launch endpoint reprobed with projectId=app-forge,
streamed `Eval run: app-forge v2 (execute mode)` as expected.

EOF
)"

inputs.old_string: <task id="44-05" type="site" status="done" skill="default" agent="default"><goal>Collapse the site back to one maintained dev-panel surface:
inputs.new_string:
<task id="44-10" type="site" status="done" skill="default" agent="default"><goal>Pull eval launch off /project-market cards and onto /projects/[id] as a dev-only ProjectOperator panel. User feedback: card-level Launch eval was scoped wrong — accidental scaffolding of new generations when the user expected "show me the latest build." Cards now read-only (View project only). New `ProjectOperator.tsx` client component on /projects/[id] renders: (a) dev-only amber-tinted operator section, (b) status strip with preserved-build info (resolved server-side from apps/portfolio/public/evals/<project>/v<N>/ via fs walk — file count, size, path) + latest-generation info, (c) explicit "Run new eval" button with confirm-first intermediate state that spells out "this will scaffold a NEW eval generation via `gad eval run --project X --execute`. It does NOT run the agent — just produces EXEC.json," (d) live 320px terminal panel consuming the existing /api/dev/launch-eval SSE, (e) abort/clear/run-again affordances with exit-code display. Server-side resolvers walk [apps/forge/evals, evals, vendor/get-anything-done/evals] for latest generation and apps/portfolio/public/evals for preserved build. Solves "right now I am blind" complaint — user can see what's running, what ran, and what's preserved without leaving the site.</goal>
      <keywords>project-operator,terminal,detail-page,dev-panel,sse</keywords>
    </task>
    <task id="44-11" type="site" status="planned"><goal>In-browser preview of the preserved static-export build on /projects/[id]. Forge now builds to out/ with Next.js static export (task 44-03), and builds are preserved under apps/portfolio/public/evals/<project>/v<N>/. BLOCKER: Next.js static exports reference assets at absolute `/_next/...` paths, so iframing the preserved build at /preserved/<project>/<version>/ doesn't resolve its own assets (browser requests /_next/static from the iframe origin — the vendor site — which returns 404). Three candidate paths: (a) configure forge with `assetPrefix` + `basePath` per-version so the export uses paths like `/preserved/app-forge/v1/_next/...` and can live under any URL; (b) dev-only Next.js rewrite that maps `/preserved/...` to filesystem reads plus an HTML transform to inject matching base; (c) serve the preserved build via a separate dev server on another port and iframe that origin. Option (a) is cleanest — requires a forge next.config change + per-version rebuild. Must preserve build correctness: script order, hydration, client JS, user interaction.</goal>
      <keywords>project-market,iframe,preview,static-export,basePath,asset-prefix</keywords>
    </task>
    <task id="44-12" type="site" status="planned"><goal>Persistent server-side process tracker for dev eval runs. Today `/api/dev/launch-eval` spawns a child and streams output via SSE, but the process state is tied to the request lifetime — close the tab or refresh the page and the running eval disappears from the UI (and if the request is aborted, SIGTERM kills the child). Build an in-memory Map<id, {command, startedAt, ringBuffer, exitCode, child}> and expose: (a) POST /api/dev/processes (start; returns id and survives the request), (b) GET /api/dev/processes (list active + recent), (c) GET /api/dev/processes/:id/tail (SSE tail that works after reconnect), (d) DELETE /api/dev/processes/:id (SIGTERM). Refactor /api/dev/launch-eval to delegate through it. ProjectOperator on mount hydrates from GET /api/dev/processes filtered to the current project so refresh preserves visibility. Ring buffer ~2000 lines/process. Dev-only; prod stays 403.</goal>
      <keywords>dev-api,process-tracker,ring-buffer,sse-reconnect,project-operator</keywords>
    </task>
    <task id="44-05" type="site" status="done" skill="default" agent="default"><goal>Collapse the site back to one maintained dev-panel surface:

feat(site): move eval launcher to /projects/[id] ProjectOperator (44-10)

User feedback: card-level "Launch eval" was miscategorized — clicking it
accidentally scaffolded new eval generations when the user wanted to see
the latest preserved build. It also left them blind to what was running.

- ProjectCard.tsx: drops all client state, launch button, abort button,
  and log panel. Cards are now read-only visuals + a single "View project"
  link. No more "use client" directive.
- app/projects/[id]/ProjectOperator.tsx: new dev-only client component.
  Amber-tinted operator section with (a) preserved-build status strip
  (resolved from apps/portfolio/public/evals/<project>/v<N>/ via fs walk:
  version, file count, size, path), (b) latest-generation status, (c)
  explicit "Run new eval" with confirm-first intermediate state that
  spells out what the command does, (d) 320px terminal panel consuming
  /api/dev/launch-eval SSE with stdout/stderr/meta color coding and
  autoscroll, (e) abort/clear/run-again affordances with exit-code
  display.
- app/projects/[id]/page.tsx: resolvePreservedBuild() walks REPO_ROOT/
  apps/portfolio/public/evals/<project> for the highest vN dir and sums
  sizes; resolveLatestGeneration() walks [apps/forge/evals, evals, vendor/
  get-anything-done/evals] for TRACE.json + run/ presence. Both gated by
  IS_DEV so prod builds stay unchanged.

Task renames to resolve collision: earlier 44-04 (marketplace polish) →
44-08, earlier 44-05 (live-sync) → 44-09. User had added older dev-id
tasks with the same 44-04/05 ids. New tasks filed: 44-11 (static-export
iframe preview — blocked on Next.js basePath/assetPrefix strategy),
44-12 (persistent server-side process tracker with ring buffer + SSE
reconnect so page refresh doesn't drop visibility).

Typecheck clean.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

inputs.command:
git -C "C:/Users/benja/Documents/custom_portfolio/vendor/get-anything-done" commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix(site): route /projects to [...id] catch-all for composite species ids

EVAL_PROJECTS.id is "<project>/<species>" (e.g. "app-forge/gad") but the
route was a single-segment [id], so every /projects/<project>/<species>
link 404'd — every composite-id project has been broken since phase 43
merged project+species into one id. User hit this on /projects/app-forge/
gad.

- Rename app/projects/[id]/ → app/projects/[...id]/ via git mv
- page.tsx: generateStaticParams emits id as string[] (split on /);
  params typed as Promise<{ id: string[] }>; join with "/" for the
  EVAL_PROJECTS lookup.

You are planning phase 01 of a new GAD project called **project-editor**, scaffolded just now at `C:\Users\benja\Documents\custom_portfolio\projects\project-editor\`.

## Context (you are a cold-start agent, here's what you need to know)

The user is building a monorepo that includes:
- `vendor/get-anything-done/` — a git submodule containing the GAD framework (Node CLI + skills/agents + a Next.js marketing/tracking site at `vendor/get-anything-done/site/`). The vendor site is what the user sees at localhost:3000 when they run `npm run dev` in the site dir. It already has `/project-market` (card grid of eval projects) and `/projects/[...id]` (detail page with a dev-only `ProjectOperator` panel that has a terminal + "Run new eval" button + preserved build status).
- `apps/forge/` — a Next.js app that was generated as the first eval run (`app-forge v1`). It's the first pass at a "project/species editor". Phase 01 of forge (before this turn) shipped: index route card grid of generations, `/g/[project]/[species]/[version]` detail route, `lib/generations.ts` that fs-walks eval roots. It uses `output: "export"` now (task gad 44-03) and its preserved static build lives at `apps/portfolio/public/evals/app-forge/v1/`.
- `apps/portfolio/` — the main public portfolio Next.js app where preserved eval builds are served from.
- `projects/magicborn/`, `projects/project-editor/` — sibling GAD planning roots for domain-specific projects.
- GAD planning convention: every project has `.planning/` with XML ledgers — STATE.xml, ROADMAP.xml, TASK-REGISTRY.xml, DECISIONS.xml, REQUIREMENTS.xml, ERRORS-AND-ATTEMPTS.xml. Phases live in ROADMAP.xml. Tasks in TASK-REGISTRY.xml with skill/agent/type attribution.

## What the user is asking for (copy of the directive that spawned this work)

> so we need a way to get to the project editor page to create new species and update requirements. do the whole up front research loop. we want to be able to at least be able to see the file folder and terminal with the options to start up clau
... [truncated 3700 bytes] ...
the project's directory structure server-side (respect gitignore) and render as a collapsible tree in the ProjectOperator panel. Reuse the terminal panel pattern.

Things to research first (use Read, Grep, Glob, and context7 if needed):
1. What is the current shape of `apps/forge/app/page.tsx` and `apps/forge/lib/generations.ts`? What does it actually render today?
2. How does the existing vendor site ProjectOperator panel work (`vendor/get-anything-done/site/app/projects/[...id]/ProjectOperator.tsx`)? What SSE pattern does it use?
3. What's in `apps/portfolio/public/evals/app-forge/v1/`? Is the `index.html` self-contained or does it reference `/_next/static/*` asset paths (confirming the 44-11 blocker)?
4. Does the monorepo have any existing "spawn claude-code with dangerous flag" patterns? (grep for `dangerously-skip-permissions`)
5. What are the existing registered planning roots in `gad-config.toml` and how does `gad eval list` discover them?

Things NOT to do:
- Don't write implementation code. This is planning only.
- Don't modify `apps/forge/` or the vendor site. Only write to `projects/project-editor/.planning/`.
- Don't dispatch subagents yourself — do the research inline.
- Don't hand-wave. Every task in PLAN.md must have concrete file paths and acceptance criteria.

## Deliverable shape

- KICKOFF.md: 1-2 page phase intent. Include: goal, scope, non-goals, definition of done, open questions, decisions to defer.
- PLAN.md: ordered task list. Each task: id, goal (1-3 sentences), affected files, acceptance criteria, dependencies on prior tasks, estimated complexity (S/M/L), skill suggestion.
- ROADMAP.xml append: one `<phase id="01">` block with title + goal + status=planned + depends.
- TASK-REGISTRY.xml append: one `<phase id="01">` block with one `<task>` per PLAN.md task, status=planned, matching ids and shortened goals.

Report back with a short summary (under 300 words) listing: what you read, the phase goal you settled on, the number of tasks, and any open decisions you're flagging for the human.

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<requirements project="project-editor" schema="1">
  <!--
    Project Editor is a first-class GAD project whose purpose is to BE the
    in-browser operator surface for every other GAD project in the monorepo.
    It is a meta-project: its own species are different implementations of
    this operator surface, tried against the same requirements.

    The first species was informally attempted as `app-forge` (phase 01 of
    that eval) and its v1 generation is preserved at
    apps/portfolio/public/evals/app-forge/v1/. Requirements captured here
    are derived from the user directive on 2026-04-14 (see STATE.xml
    next-action) and the observable gaps that blocked forge v1 from being
    end-to-end usable from the vendor site.

    Capture model:
      Project   = this spec (requirements, non-goals, acceptance)
      Species   = a configuration of context framework + tech stack used
                  to implement the project (e.g. `forge/nextjs-static`,
                  `forge/vite-spa`, `forge/shell-in-box`)
      Generation = one eval run of a species (v1, v2, ...), preserved
      Integration = the generation currently wired into the vendor site
                    as the live editor surface
  -->

  <requirement id="REQ-001" priority="must">
    <goal>Operator can open a GAD project from the vendor site (/project-market → /projects/[...id]) and see that project's file and folder tree rendered server-side, with .gitignore-respecting filters, without leaving the browser.</goal>
    <rationale>Today the site shows catalog metadata only. The operator has to drop to a terminal to see what's in a project. The editor surface must replace that step.</rationale>
    <acceptance>Clicking a project card loads a tree view whose contents match `git ls-files` for that project's path within 1 second on a warm cache. Directories expand/collapse. File count and total size are visible.</acceptance>
  </requirement>

  <requirement id="REQ-002" priority="must">
    <goal>Operator can 
... [truncated 6032 bytes] ...
ment extends that framing: the editor itself should be a swappable implementation, not a hardcoded vendor-site page.</rationale>
    <acceptance>Documented in DECISIONS.xml with an explicit species-of-editor registry. The vendor site reads the current "active editor species" from a manifest and renders it accordingly. Can be deferred to a future phase but should not be architecturally precluded.</acceptance>
  </requirement>

  <non-goals>
    <item>Production-hosted mutation of any project state. All write paths are dev-only (NODE_ENV=development gate enforced at route and button level). The live public portfolio site remains read-only.</item>
    <item>General-purpose IDE features (IntelliSense, LSP, multi-file editor tabs, split panes). If the user wants that they open VS Code. The editor is a GAD operator surface, not a code editor.</item>
    <item>Remote / hosted agent execution. Dangerous-mode CLI runs on the operator's own machine only.</item>
    <item>Authentication / multi-user. Single operator assumed.</item>
  </non-goals>

  <open-questions>
    <question id="Q-001">Where does the Project Editor UI live: inside `vendor/get-anything-done/site/` (current ProjectOperator location) or extracted into `apps/forge/` (which IS the generated editor from the first eval)? The user said "lets take it as the foundation" — implying we integrate forge, not replicate it. Decision needed in phase 01.</question>
    <question id="Q-002">Species promotion: manifest format (JSON in repo? symlink? git tag?) and where the manifest lives. Blocks REQ-005.</question>
    <question id="Q-003">Dangerous-mode terminal: do we spawn `claude` / `codex` directly, or go through a runtime-installer registration (similar to how `gad install` already handles runtime hooks)? Latter is more extensible.</question>
    <question id="Q-004">Should the operator surface be a route inside the vendor site (/projects/[...id]/edit) or its own top-level app (/editor) with project selection inside?</question>
  </open-questions>
</requirements>

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<roadmap project="project-editor" schema="1">
  <milestone id="v1" status="active">
    <title>Project Editor v1 — dogfood the loop on the vendor site</title>
    <description>Take the app-forge v1 preserved build as the starting point and land a usable operator surface (file tree + dangerous terminal + generation preview + species/requirements edit) inside the vendor site. By the end of v1 the user can open any GAD project in the browser, see its files, launch a dangerous coding agent in it, view the latest preserved generation, edit requirements, and promote a favored generation to integration status.</description>

    <phase id="00" status="in-progress">
      <title>Research + integration plan for app-forge v1 baseline</title>
      <goal>Dispatched to the Plan subagent on 2026-04-14. Read apps/forge/ source, the preserved v1 build at apps/portfolio/public/evals/app-forge/v1/, the existing vendor-site ProjectOperator panel, and the gad CLI multi-root eval plumbing. Produce phase 01 KICKOFF.md + PLAN.md + append to TASK-REGISTRY.xml. Decide Q-001 (integrate forge vs replicate in vendor site), Q-004 (route placement), and pick a first-cut path for the Next.js static-export preview problem (vendor task 44-11).</goal>
      <depends></depends>
    </phase>

    <phase id="01" status="planned">
      <title>Integrate app-forge v1 baseline as the initial editor surface</title>
      <goal>Land the first cut of the editor on /projects/[...id] using whatever was decided in phase 00. Deliverables at minimum: preview of the preserved v1 build visible from the vendor site (even if imperfect), file tree view server-side-rendered, existing ProjectOperator terminal panel retained and generalized to accept multiple command tokens beyond `gad eval run`. No schema changes to eval projects. All writes dev-only. Phase completes when the operator can reload /projects/app-forge/gad and see (a) file tree, (b) terminal, (c) preserved generation view (or an explicit working link to it), with
... [truncated 454 bytes] ...
nces in ProjectOperator for "Launch Claude Code (dangerous)" and "Launch Codex exec" with confirm-first gates. Stream output via SSE with the same protocol as today. Resolves REQ-002. Does NOT resolve REQ-006 (process persistence) — that's phase 04.</goal>
      <depends>01</depends>
    </phase>

    <phase id="03" status="planned">
      <title>Species and requirements editor</title>
      <goal>Read + write path for species.json, project.json, REQUIREMENTS.xml, ROADMAP.xml. Form-driven for structured fields, raw-XML for planning files. Validates on save. Dev-only. Resolves REQ-004. Also surfaces resolved inheritance (REQ-007) using the existing lib/eval-loader.cjs helpers. No new backend — all via /api/dev/projects/<id>/write plus optimistic client refresh.</goal>
      <depends>01</depends>
    </phase>

    <phase id="04" status="planned">
      <title>Persistent server-side process tracker</title>
      <goal>In-memory Map<id, child + ring buffer> with GET /api/dev/processes (list), GET /api/dev/processes/:id/tail (SSE replay + tail), DELETE /api/dev/processes/:id (SIGTERM). Refactor phase 02's /api/dev/spawn to register into it. ProjectOperator hydrates process list on mount so refresh preserves visibility. Ring buffer ~2000 lines. Resolves REQ-006 (and vendor task 44-12).</goal>
      <depends>02</depends>
    </phase>

    <phase id="05" status="planned">
      <title>Formal promotion loop: generation → integrated site</title>
      <goal>New skill `gad-promote-generation` + matching CLI subcommand that takes (project, species, version), writes or updates a promotion manifest (location TBD in phase 00 open question Q-002), copies or symlinks the preserved build into the vendor site's served path, appends a DECISIONS entry with timestamp + rationale, and commits. Revert is a one-command inverse. Site reads the promotion manifest at build time to decide which generation to render for each project. Resolves REQ-005.</goal>
      <depends>04</depends>
    </phase>
  </milestone>
</roadmap>

  <next-action>Project scaffolded via `gad projects init` on 2026-04-14 following the user directive to formalize the Project Editor as its own GAD project with its own planning root. REQUIREMENTS.xml drafted with 8 requirements (REQ-001..REQ-008) covering: file tree, dangerous terminal, generation preview, species/requirements editing, promotion loop, process persistence, inheritance visibility, and species-of-editor extensibility. 4 open questions captured (Q-001 integrate forge vs replicate, Q-002 promotion manifest format, Q-003 runtime registration via gad install, Q-004 route placement). ROADMAP.xml seeded with 6 phases: 00 research, 01 integrate forge baseline, 02 dangerous terminal, 03 species/req editor, 04 process tracker, 05 promotion loop. Registered as a new planning root in the top-level `gad-config.toml` (note: `gad projects init` has a bug — it wrote the registration into the submodule's own gad-config.toml instead of the workspace root; corrected manually, follow-up task owed to the framework). Phase 00 dispatched to the Plan subagent to read apps/forge/, the preserved v1 at apps/portfolio/public/evals/app-forge/v1/, the vendor-site ProjectOperator panel, and the gad multi-root eval plumbing, then write phases/01-integrate-forge-baseline/KICKOFF.md + PLAN.md + append to TASK-REGISTRY.xml + ROADMAP.xml. Agent in flight at time of last update.</next-action>
</state>

Hardening (explicit allow-list, argument sanitization, per-command timeout tuning) is deferred to a follow-up task after the editor's happy path is proven.     <task id="44-13" type="cli" status="planned"><goal>Fix `gad projects init` config-write location: today it registers the new planning root into "the nearest gad-config.toml" relative to the CLI binary, which when the CLI runs from `vendor/get-anything-done/bin/gad.cjs` resolves to the submodule's own `vendor/get-anything-done/.planning/gad-config.toml` instead of the workspace root's `gad-config.toml`. That pollutes the submodule and hides the new root from the outer workspace. Fix: `projects init` should walk upward from `process.cwd()` (NOT from __dirname) to find the first parent with a `gad-config.toml` or `.planning/gad-config.toml` and write there. Also make the path stored in the registration relative to THAT config file's dir, not to wherever the CLI binary lives (the current entry used backslash-escaped `..\..\projects\project-editor` which is a second bug). Surfaced 2026-04-14 by the project-editor scaffold workflow; manually corrected both the main-repo config and reverted the submodule pollution after init.</goal>
      <keywords>cli,projects-init,config-resolution,bugfix</keywords>
    </task>
    <task id="44-14" type="cleanup" status="planned"><goal>Register `projects/project-editor` as an `[[evals.roots]]` entry once phase 00 of that project has produced a species/generation shape. Today it's registered only as a `[[planning.roots]]` entry — project-editor is a meta-project and doesn't yet have species. When phase 01 of project-editor lands (integrating app-forge v1 as the initial species), project-editor will have at least one species and should become discoverable via `gad eval list` and /project-market alongside the other eval projects. Low effort: one toml append + a species.json stub pointing at app-forge v1 as the inherited baseline.</goal>
      <keywords>eval-roots,project-editor,registration,multi-root</keywords>
    </task>
  </phase>
  <phase id="44.5">

  entry so it shows up on /project-market.

EOF
)"

inputs.content:
# Phase 01 — Integrate app-forge v1 as project-editor baseline

## Intent
The Project Editor is the operator surface for every GAD project in the
monorepo. Phase 01 takes the first eval generation (app-forge v1, preserved
at apps/portfolio/public/evals/app-forge/v1/) and wires it into the vendor
site as a viewable baseline, then layers the three operator affordances the
user asked for: file tree, scoped terminal, and dangerous-mode agent spawn.

This phase is the seed of the project/species/generation loop:

  project (requirements) → species (config) → generation (eval run)
                                                ↓
                                           promotion to site

Phase 01 only covers "see the generation + operate on the project dir."
Species CRUD and promotion workflow are phases 03 and 05 respectively.

## Scope (IN)
- Render app-forge v1 in-browser from the vendor site via a path-rewriting
  dev proxy (no copy, no rebuild).
- Dedicated `/project-editor` and `/project-editor/[project]` routes on the
  vendor site that surface the forge baseline plus operator controls.
- Generalized `/api/dev/spawn` SSE endpoint that accepts a command token
  (`claude-code` | `codex` | `gad-eval-run`) and a validated project slug →
  spawns with scoped cwd, dangerous flags, streams stdout/stderr.
- Server-side `/api/dev/tree` endpoint returning a shallow JSON tree of a
  project directory, respecting `.gitignore` and a hardcoded denylist
  (`node_modules`, `.next`, `out`, `dist`, `.git`, `.turbo`).
- Collapsible `ProjectTree` client component rendered alongside the existing
  terminal panel pattern.
- Phase 01 tasks registered in ROADMAP.xml and TASK-REGISTRY.xml.

## Scope (OUT / non-goals)
- Editing requirements files from the browser (phase 03).
- Creating new species from a form (phase 03).
- Promotion workflow — marking a generation "blessed" and copying into
  apps/portfolio/public/evals/ (phase 05).
- Fixing vendor task 44-11 properly via a forge basePath rebuild (phase 02
  can revisit �
... [truncated 2128 bytes] ...
*Q-001 integrate forge vs replicate (resolved — integrate):** the vendor
  site embeds the preserved forge v1 build via a dev proxy; forge is the
  rendered surface, vendor site is the runtime and operator controls.
- **Q-004 route placement (resolved — vendor site):** the operator surface
  lives at `/project-editor` on the vendor site, not as a sibling Next
  app. Future species can swap the embedded surface without changing the
  route.
- **Q-002 promotion manifest (deferred to phase 05):** phase 01 does not
  require a manifest; we hardcode `apps/portfolio/public/evals/app-forge/
  v1/` as the single integration point.
- **Q-003 runtime registration via `gad install` (deferred):** phase 01
  uses a hardcoded whitelist in the spawn route handler. A future phase
  can make this pluggable.

## Open questions surfaced BY phase 01 (resolve during execution)

- **Q-101 HTML rewriting strategy:** regex replace vs. streaming parser.
  Phase 01 decision: naive regex on `/_next/`, `href="/`, `src="/` in the
  `forge-view` route. Log misses. If the forge index fails to render
  after rewriting, fall back to documenting `next dev -p 3001` in
  apps/forge and iframing `http://localhost:3001` instead.
- **Q-102 Windows claude/codex spawn semantics:** the operator is on
  Windows 11. Phase 01 must use `shell: false` + resolve the binary via
  `where` at spawn time and explicitly handle `.cmd`/`.ps1` shims.
  Acceptance: spawning claude-code on Windows produces stdout within 5s,
  not an ENOENT.
- **Q-103 dangerous-mode authentication:** phase 01 relies only on
  `NODE_ENV=development` + localhost binding. A future phase can add a
  per-session token cookie. Flagged but not a blocker.
- **Q-104 terminal persistence:** phase 01 is ephemeral in-browser state
  only. Persistence is tracked as vendor task 44-12 / project-editor
  phase 04.
- **Q-105 project enumeration source of truth:** phase 01 uses a union of
  `[[planning.roots]]` + forge's generation scan, deduped by slug.
  Single source of truth deferred to phase 02.

inputs.content:
# Phase 01 — PLAN

Tasks are ordered. Each task is atomic and ends with a commit.

---

## 01-01 — Enumerate projects from gad-config.toml + generations

**Goal:** Add a server-only helper to the vendor site that reads
`custom_portfolio/gad-config.toml`, extracts `[[planning.roots]]`, and
merges with a forge-style generations scan. Produces a typed
`ProjectSummary` list consumed by the `/project-editor` index.

**Affected files (new):**
- `vendor/get-anything-done/site/lib/project-editor/projects.ts`
- `vendor/get-anything-done/site/lib/project-editor/toml-lite.ts` (minimal
  TOML parser OR import `smol-toml` if already present — check
  `vendor/get-anything-done/site/package.json` first; do not add deps
  if avoidable).

**Acceptance:**
- `getProjectSummaries()` returns at least `project-editor`, `app-forge`,
  `magicborn`, `get-anything-done`, `grime-time`, `repub-builder`,
  `mb-cli-framework` when run from the vendor site's cwd.
- Each summary carries `{ slug, rootPath (absolute), latestGeneration? }`.

**Depends on:** none. **Complexity:** S. **Skill:** none.

---

## 01-02 — Forge-view dev proxy route

**Goal:** Serve the preserved app-forge v1 build through the vendor site so
its `/_next/static/*` references resolve under the vendor origin.

**Affected files (new):**
- `vendor/get-anything-done/site/app/api/dev/forge-view/route.ts` — serves
  rewritten HTML for a request path, content-type `text/html`.
- `vendor/get-anything-done/site/app/api/dev/forge-asset/[...path]/route.ts`
  — streams arbitrary files from the preserved build dir with correct
  content-type (js, css, woff2, svg, png).

**Acceptance:**
- `NODE_ENV !== "development"` → 403.
- Request path normalized; any `..` segment → 400.
- Asset route resolves absolute path under
  `apps/portfolio/public/evals/app-forge/v1/` only. Files outside → 404.
- Forge-view route reads `index.html` (or the requested sub-route's
  `.html`), rewrites `/_next/`, `href="/`, `src="/` occurrences under
  the HTML body to `/api/dev/forge-asset/...`, 
... [truncated 5060 bytes] ...
confirm"`).

**Depends on:** 01-02, 01-05, 01-06, 01-08. **Complexity:** M.
**Skill:** none.

---

## 01-08 — Extract `useSseSpawn` hook

**Goal:** Refactor the SSE parsing loop out of `ProjectOperator.tsx` into
a reusable hook so the new panel doesn't duplicate 100 lines of reader/
decoder code.

**Affected files:**
- `vendor/get-anything-done/site/lib/dev/useSseSpawn.ts` (new)
- `vendor/get-anything-done/site/app/projects/[...id]/ProjectOperator.tsx`
  (modified — adopt hook, keep existing behavior byte-identical).

**Acceptance:**
- Existing `/projects/[...id]` page's "Run new eval" flow continues to
  work end-to-end (manual smoke: click button, see stdout stream in).
- Hook signature:
  `useSseSpawn({ endpoint, body }) → { state, run, abort, clear }`.
- No behavioral change to existing route.

**Depends on:** none. **Complexity:** S. **Skill:** `refactor`.

---

## 01-09 — Update planning ledgers

**Goal:** Commit the ROADMAP.xml and TASK-REGISTRY.xml updates for phase 01
so the GAD loop has an accurate view.

**Affected files:**
- `projects/project-editor/.planning/ROADMAP.xml`
- `projects/project-editor/.planning/TASK-REGISTRY.xml`
- `projects/project-editor/.planning/STATE.xml` (set current phase = 01).

**Acceptance:**
- `<phase id="01" status="planned">` block present in ROADMAP.xml.
- Ten `<task>` entries (`01-01` … `01-10`) in TASK-REGISTRY.xml.
- STATE.xml reflects active phase 01.

**Depends on:** none (can run first in execution). **Complexity:** S.
**Skill:** `gad-add-phase`.

---

## 01-10 — Smoke verification

**Goal:** End-to-end manual verification against the DoD from KICKOFF.md.

**Affected files (new):**
- `projects/project-editor/.planning/phases/01-integrate-forge-baseline/VERIFICATION.md`

**Acceptance:**
- Checklist matches KICKOFF.md DoD items 1–6, each with PASS/FAIL and
  evidence (screenshot paths, curl output, console logs).
- Any FAIL creates a follow-up task in the phase 02 backlog.

**Depends on:** 01-01 through 01-09. **Complexity:** S.
**Skill:** `verify-phase`.

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<decisions project="project-editor" schema="1">

  <decision id="project-editor-001" phase="00" date="2026-04-14">
    <title>Integrate forge v1 via a path-rewriting dev proxy rather than rebuild or copy</title>
    <summary>The preserved app-forge v1 static export at apps/portfolio/public/evals/app-forge/v1/ references `/_next/static/*` at absolute paths, which breaks any iframe served from a path prefix on the vendor site. Three integration options were considered: (a) rebuild forge with `basePath`/`assetPrefix` set per-version, (b) add a dev proxy that reads files from the preserved build and rewrites HTML `/_next/` references to a prefixed path, (c) run `next dev` on forge at a sibling port and iframe that origin. Phase 00 chose (b) as the default with (c) as the documented fallback if rewriting proves brittle.</summary>
    <impact>Phase 01 ships `/api/dev/forge-view` + `/api/dev/forge-asset/[...path]` route handlers. No changes to forge source. No changes to the preserved build. Vendor task 44-11 (the blocker for true iframe support) is NOT resolved by this decision — the proxy is a workaround scoped to dev mode only. A proper basePath rebuild is deferred to phase 02 or later. Tradeoff: regex rewriting can miss edge cases (dynamic imports, CSS url()); phase 01 logs misses and phase 02 can switch to a streaming HTML parser or the basePath rebuild if misses are frequent.</impact>
  </decision>

  <decision id="project-editor-002" phase="00" date="2026-04-14">
    <title>Operator surface lives at /project-editor inside the vendor site</title>
    <summary>Question Q-001 (REQUIREMENTS.xml): build the operator surface inside the vendor site at /project-editor (present at localhost:3000 next to /project-market) or extract it into apps/forge and let vendor site iframe forge's own routes. Phase 00 chose the vendor site: it already has dev-only SSE endpoints (/api/dev/launch-eval), a spawn pattern, a ProjectOperator panel, and runs Node APIs. forge is static-export only and c
... [truncated 891 bytes] ...
 dangerous-mode coding agents (claude, codex, future) pluggable via the existing `gad install` runtime registration, or hardcode a whitelist inside the spawn route handler. Phase 00 chose the hardcoded whitelist for v1 because (a) only two agents matter today (claude-code and codex), (b) the existing runtime-installer plumbing is oriented toward eval tracing hooks, not dangerous-mode flags, and (c) a pluggable surface can be retrofitted once we know the shape.</summary>
    <impact>Phase 01 task 01-06 ships `lib/project-editor/spawn-registry.ts` with three entries: claude-code, codex, gad-eval-run. Adding a new agent requires an entry + a commit. A future phase can extract this into a gad-installed runtime contract once the shape stabilizes. Security posture is unchanged: NODE_ENV gate + localhost binding + shell:false + identifier regex. No auth layer.</impact>
  </decision>

  <decision id="project-editor-004" phase="00" date="2026-04-14">
    <title>Project enumeration is the union of [[planning.roots]] and generation scan for phase 01</title>
    <summary>Question Q-005 (phase 01 open questions): which source of truth determines the list of projects on /project-editor? Options: (a) read [[planning.roots]] from gad-config.toml, (b) read [[evals.roots]] and walk for species.json/project.json, (c) reuse forge's `lib/generations.ts` fs walker, (d) union all of the above. Phase 00 chose (d) — union + dedupe by slug — because the legacy planning roots (grime-time, repub-builder, mb-cli-framework, magicborn) registered in task gad 44-02 don't yet have species and would be invisible under (b) alone, while app-forge needs the generation scan to surface its preserved v1 build.</summary>
    <impact>Phase 01 task 01-01 ships `lib/project-editor/projects.ts` that reads both sources and merges. Single source of truth is deferred to phase 02 (alongside vendor task 44-14 which is the inverse — registering project-editor itself as an eval root). No CLI change required for phase 01.</impact>
  </decision>

</decisions>

    <task id="44-05" type="site" status="done" skill="default" agent="default"><goal>Collapse the site back to one maintained dev-panel surface: the compact panel becomes the canonical UI for both section scans and page/band panels, with the same sticky in-section positioning controls and the same rich source-searchable identifiers. Remove the old long-form section panel divergence so users always see one list format, one header, and one copy/handoff behavior while scanning large sections.</goal>
      <keywords>dev-panel,ux,sticky,compact,section-scan</keywords>
    </task>
    <task id="44-13" type="cli" status="planned"><goal>Fix `gad projects init` config-write location: today it registers the new planning root into "the nearest gad-config.toml" relative to the CLI binary, which when the CLI runs from `vendor/get-anything-done/bin/gad.cjs` resolves to the submodule's own `vendor/get-anything-done/.planning/gad-config.toml` instead of the workspace root's `gad-config.toml`. That pollutes the submodule and hides the new root from the outer workspace. Fix: `projects init` should walk upward from `process.cwd()` (NOT from __dirname) to find the first parent with a `gad-config.toml` or `.planning/gad-config.toml` and write there. Also make the path stored in the registration relative to THAT config file's dir, not to wherever the CLI binary lives (the current entry used backslash-escaped `..\..\projects\project-editor` which is a second bug). Surfaced 2026-04-14 by the project-editor scaffold workflow; manually corrected both the main-repo config and reverted the submodule pollution after init.</goal>
      <keywords>cli,projects-init,config-resolution,bugfix</keywords>
    </task>
    <task id="44-14" type="cleanup" status="planned"><goal>Register `projects/project-editor` as an `[[evals.roots]]` entry once phase 00 of t
... [truncated 34 bytes] ...
/generation shape. Today it's registered only as a `[[planning.roots]]` entry â€” project-editor is a meta-project and doesn't yet have species. When phase 01 of project-editor lands (integrating app-forge v1 as the initial species), project-editor will have at least one species and should become discoverable via `gad eval list` and /project-market alongside the other eval projects. Low effort: one toml append + a species.json stub pointing at app-forge v1 as the inherited baseline.</goal>
      <keywords>eval-roots,project-editor,registration,multi-root</keywords>
    </task>
      <task id="44-15" type="site" status="done" skill="create-skill" agent="default"><goal>Lock the component identity pattern for the Visual Context Panel: use explicit SiteSection cid="...-site-section" literals as the default searchable section handle, keep Identified optional for inner-block granularity, and remove duplicate in-section panel rendering so each section shows one canonical panel surface. Document the pattern and human-in-the-loop prompt handoff workflow in a reusable skill.</goal>
      <resolution>Shipped 2026-04-14. SiteSection auto-id fallback was removed in favor of explicit section ids, panel copy/handoff now preserves source-searchable literals, duplicate panel rendering was collapsed to the single outside/band panel path, and skills/gad-visual-context-panel-identities/SKILL.md was added to codify the workflow and guardrails.</resolution>
      <keywords>visual-context-panel,site-section,cid,identifiers,human-in-the-loop,skill</keywords>
    </task>
