inputs.new_string:
<task id="44-20" type="site" status="done" skill="default" agent="default"><goal>Compact the /planning Workflows tab (Identified `PlanningTabWorkflows`) per 2026-04-14 feedback: (a) authored workflows now render as compact React-Flow-primary cards in a grid (matching emergent layout) instead of large Mermaid+React-Flow side-by-side panels; Mermaid is tucked under a `<details>` disclosure. (b) Section blurbs trimmed from 2-3 sentences to one. (c) MethodologyCallout collapsed from a big amber block to a one-line `<details>` disclosure. (d) Authored tree now flattens into the same grid instead of indented nested cards so the grid density is uniform. Noise panel remains deferred (user: "still under discussion"). Build clean.</goal>
      <keywords>planning-tab,workflows,compact,react-flow,ui</keywords>
    </task>
    <task id="44-21" type="site" status="planned"><goal>Trace-based agent-behavior analytics on /planning. New tab (Identified `PlanningTabSignal` or similar) that mines `.trace-events.jsonl` for: (a) most-visited files ranked by tool_use count across Read/Edit/Write/Glob, normalized to repo-relative paths; (b) tool mix histogram (Bash/Read/Edit/Grep/WebFetch share %); (c) file-path frequency heatmap (by directory); (d) Bash command extraction (separate path refs inside `inputs.command` from the tool name); (e) URL route frequency from WebFetch. Cross-reference the observed patterns against authored workflow participants (44-20 is the authored surface) to surface conformance gaps at the file-path level instead of just the tool level. User ask: "I want to be able to see where the coding agent goes most of the time and what tool it is using — looking for repeating patterns." Data source already exists; gap is the aggregation/rank/visualization layer.</goal>
      <keywords>planning-tab,signal,trace-analytics,file-visits,tool-mix,heatmap</keywords>
    </task>
    <task id="44-22" type="site" status="planned"><goal>Human workflows tab on /planning (Identified `PlanningTabHumanWorkflows`). Separate surfac
... [truncated 1704 bytes] ...
text identity workflow that we have here which is where I go into I have the UI up the sign up and then I go to a page and then when I have the visual context panel up I can select identified components things that are going to be in place that are static that is a part of our UI workflow that we do when we develop components they must be a part of this system." Both need trigger + participants + Mermaid source + related decisions. Authors: human, dogfooded against the Visual Context Panel.</goal>
      <keywords>authored-workflows,evolution,visual-context-identity,skill-loop,ui-development</keywords>
    </task>
    <task id="44-24" type="skill" status="planned"><goal>Skill evolution pass for the monorepo's daily-driver agent (root-of-monorepo work, not get-anything-done-only). Steps: (1) run `gad self-eval` or equivalent pressure-analysis to surface high-pressure phases + skill gaps, (2) invoke `create-skill` or `gad-create-skill` on the candidates, (3) drop outputs as proto-skills under `.planning/proto-skills/`, (4) verify coding agents can SEE proto-skills (via `gad evolution install` surface — per task 42.4-07 they should already be installable at runtime), (5) run `merge-skill` on any redundant proto-skills, (6) promote the stable ones to canonical `skills/`. User explicitly called out: "we dont need to do evals for our skills like we have been or the rigorous skill-creator suite. we pull in quite a bit of context for creating the skills with the candidates." Goal is a lean evolution loop that runs from the monorepo root and makes the results immediately available to the next coding agent session. Also review gaps/confusion in existing create-skill and merge-skill skills while we're in there.</goal>
      <keywords>skill-evolution,self-eval,proto-skills,create-skill,merge-skill,daily-driver</keywords>
    </task>
    <task id="44-14" type="cleanup" status="planned"><goal>Register `projects/project-editor` as an `[[evals.roots]]` entry once phase 00 of that project has produced a species/generation shape.

git -C "C:/Users/benja/Documents/custom_portfolio/vendor/get-anything-done" commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat(site): visual-context-panel identities + workflows tab compact + follow-up tasks

Bundle of work across 178 files: (a) the visual-context-panel identity
rollout from the prior session (tasks 44-15, 44-16, 44-17 — explicit
SiteSection cid literals as the default searchable section handle,
Identified kept optional for inner-block targets, single maintained
DevPanel surface, methodology doc at references/visual-context-panel-
methodology.md, reusable skill at skills/gad-visual-context-panel-
identities/SKILL.md); (b) my /planning Workflows tab compactness refactor
(task 44-20); (c) new follow-up tasks 44-20..44-24.

Workflows tab (44-20):
- PlanningWorkflowsTab: Identified id fixed from `PlanningWorkflowsTab`
  to `PlanningTabWorkflows` (matches user-requested source-searchable
  token). Authored section now renders as a compact React-Flow-primary
  grid (matching emergent layout) instead of big Mermaid+React-Flow
  side-by-side cards. Authored tree flattens into the same grid with
  depth-aware indent preserved. Blurbs trimmed from 2-3 sentences to
  one. MethodologyCallout collapsed from big amber block to one-line
  <details> disclosure. Noise panel stays deferred per user ("still
  under discussion").
- WorkflowCard: new compact-authored branch renders a React-Flow-primary
  small card with Mermaid tucked under a <details> disclosure and
  participants collapsed. Non-compact legacy layout kept for detail-page
  callers.

New follow-up tasks filed (44-20..44-24):
- 44-20 workflows tab compactness (done, this commit)
- 44-21 trace-based agent-behavior analytics on /planning — most-visited
  files, tool mix, path heatmap, URL route frequency, cross-referenced
  with authored workflow participants
- 44-22 human workflows tab on /planning — daily routine + expected
  sequences for operating from the monorepo root, eventually cross-
  referenced with trace data
- 44-23 author gad-evolution.md and gad-visual-context-identity.md
  workflows under .planning/workflows/ so they surface on the compact
  tab
- 44-24 skill evolution pass (self-eval → pressure → candidates →
  create-skill → proto-skills → merge-skill → promote) for the monorepo
  daily-driver

Also includes: generated-data rebuilds (catalog.generated.ts, eval-data.
generated.ts, self-eval.json, llms.txt, zip artifacts) and per-session
trace/hook logs (.planning/.gad-log/, .trace-events.jsonl, .trace-seq).

Typecheck clean. Pushing both repos after this commit for a new-session
restart.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

inputs.content:
---
name: Always include review instructions during evolution stages
description: When running GAD evolution (monorepo or single project), always tell the user exactly how to review each proto-skill — not just the CLI surface but a concrete per-slug walkthrough
type: feedback
---

When the user asks to "evolve" the monorepo or any singular GAD project, and the
evolution loop produces proto-skills staged under `.planning/proto-skills/`, the
end-of-turn report MUST include explicit, per-slug review instructions. Not just
a generic `gad evolution promote <slug>` blurb — a concrete walkthrough:

- Per proto-skill: slug, one-line purpose, line count + validator pass rate,
  file to open, and the exact CLI command the user runs next.
- Which proto-skills to consider merging vs keeping separate (cite likely
  overlapping existing skills in `skills/`).
- The blocking relationship — next evolution cannot run until this batch is
  cleared.
- A suggested review order (usually highest-leverage first).

**Why:** Feedback from 2026-04-14. The user explicitly said "always tell me how
to review when we are in the stages of evolution — for the evolutionary workflow
and I am asking you to evolve the monorepo or any singular project, I should be
told exactly how to review it." Review is the human gate in this loop, and
vague pointers make the gate friction-y enough that batches rot in
`.planning/proto-skills/`.

**How to apply:** After any `gad evolution evolve` run, any manual proto-skill
staging, or any turn that leaves `.planning/proto-skills/` non-empty, close the
turn with a "How to review" section containing the per-slug walkthrough above.
Also applies symmetrically to any future merge-skill or promote-skill batches.

{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"C:\Users\benja\Documents\custom_portfolio\vendor\get-anything-done\.planning\workflows\gad-evolution.md","content":"---
slug: gad-evolution
name: GAD Evolution Loop
description: Pressure → candidate → proto-skill → validated → promoted/discarded. The mechanism by which GAD's skill set evolves from observed high-pressure phases into new distributable skills.
trigger: `compute-self-eval` detects a phase with high pressure metrics (tool-use burst, repeated corrections, long loops, novel failure patterns), OR an operator manually runs `gad evolution` against pending candidates.
participants:
  skills: [create-proto-skill, gad-evolution-evolve, gad-evolution-validator, gad-skill-creator]
  agents: [default]
  cli: [gad evolution status, gad evolution validate, gad evolution promote, gad evolution discard]
  artifacts: [skills/candidates/<slug>/CANDIDATE.md, .planning/proto-skills/<slug>/PROVENANCE.md, .planning/proto-skills/<slug>/SKILL.md, .planning/proto-skills/<slug>/VALIDATION.md, skills/<name>/]
parent-workflow: gad-loop
related-phases: [42, 42.2, 42.3]
---

GAD's self-improvement loop. Pressure in a phase is a signal that some
repeatable pattern is missing from the skill set; the evolution loop
turns that signal into a distributable skill over three stages.

Stage 1 is automatic (compute-self-eval writes raw CANDIDATE.md). Stage 2
is agentic (the `create-proto-skill` skill drafts in bulk with per-
candidate checkpoints per decision gad-171, then `gad-evolution-validator`
writes VALIDATION.md advisorily). Stage 3 is human — review and decide
promote-or-discard. The loop gates on zero pending proto-skills so
humans stay in the approval path.

Proto-skill is a permanent type (decision gad-167): PROVENANCE.md
travels with the skill through promotion so we can triage by pressure
origin forever.

```mermaid
flowchart TD
  A[compute-self-eval detects pressure] --> B[write skills/candidates/<slug>/CANDIDATE.md]
  B --> C[gad-evolution-evolve skill orchestrates]
  C --> D[create-proto-skill skill: bulk draft]
  D --> E[write PROVENANCE.md lock marker]
  E --> F[draft SKILL.md in dot-agent format]
  F --> G[gad-evolution-validator skill: VALIDATION.md advisory]
  G --> H[human review SKILL.md + VALIDATION.md]
  H --> I{promote?}
  I -->|yes| J[gad evolution promote -> skills/<name>/]
  I -->|no| K[gad evolution discard]
  J --> L[PROVENANCE.md travels with skill]
```

{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"C:\Users\benja\Documents\custom_portfolio\vendor\get-anything-done\.planning\workflows\README.md","content":"# GAD Workflows

Workflows are a first-class planning artifact. Each workflow describes an
**expected** sequence of skills, agents, CLI calls, and planning-doc updates
for a recurring kind of work (the main loop, a decision, an evolution pass,
a debug session, etc.).

At runtime the trace pipeline computes an **actual** graph from `.gad-log/`
events (skill invocations, agent spawns, tool calls, CLI commands, planning
file edits). The diff between expected and actual is rendered on the
`/planning` Workflows tab and emitted as a `workflow_conformance` score that
upgrades the flat `framework_compliance` metric (decision gad-173).

No enforcement. Advisory signal only. Drift is a tuning input, not a fault.

## File layout

```
.planning/workflows/
  README.md                         # this file
  <slug>.md                         # one workflow per file
```

Slugs are kebab-case, prefixed by origin: `gad-loop.md`, `gad-decide.md`,
`gad-evolution.md`, etc. Slugs MUST be unique across the project.

## File format

Each workflow file has YAML frontmatter followed by a body. The body may
contain explanatory prose and MUST contain exactly one mermaid fenced block.

```markdown
---
slug: gad-decide
name: GAD Decide
description: Structured decision pattern — open question → options → pros/cons → recommendation → commit.
trigger: User asks an open question with multiple viable answers.
participants:
  skills: []
  agents: [default]
  cli: [gad decisions]
  artifacts: [.planning/DECISIONS.xml]
parent-workflow: gad-discuss-plan-execute
related-phases: [42.3]
---

Short explanatory prose about why this workflow exists and how to read
the graph.

```mermaid
flowchart LR
  A[user asks open question] --> B[gather options]
  B --> C[structured pros/cons]
  C --> D[recommend with rationale]
  D --> E{user accepts?}
  E
... [truncated 1853 bytes] ...
order_nodes) / expected_nodes
```

Matching nodes: actual node matches an expected node by participant identity
(same skill / agent / cli command / artifact path) AND position in the
topological order of the expected graph.

Extra nodes: actual nodes with no expected counterpart.

Out-of-order nodes: actual nodes that match an expected node but ran
before/after their expected topological predecessors/successors.

Score is advisory. Negative scores clamp to zero. When every run deviates
the same way, the expected graph is wrong. When one run deviates, that
run is worth investigating.

## Trace self-report (decision gad-173 answer (c))

GAD-aware skills and agents emit explicit workflow events into
`.trace-events.jsonl`:

```jsonl
{"ts": "...", "event": "workflow_enter", "slug": "gad-decide", "instance_id": "..."}
{"ts": "...", "event": "workflow_exit",  "slug": "gad-decide", "instance_id": "...", "status": "complete"}
```

The synthesizer prefers these explicit markers when available and falls
back to sequence inference (matching observed tool/skill/agent patterns
against expected graphs) when they are missing. Legacy runs and non-GAD
skills always go through inference.

## Authoring rules

1. **Start from the actual pattern you observe.** Don't design a workflow
   you've never seen. Author `gad-decide` because we just did a decide.
2. **Keep the graph small.** ~8-12 nodes per workflow max. Nest to break
   up bigger flows.
3. **Use decision diamonds sparingly.** One or two per workflow. More than
   that and the graph is describing two workflows.
4. **Name nodes as verbs.** `gather options` not `options gathered`.
5. **Participants are a contract.** Only list skills/agents/cli commands
   that MUST appear. Optional participants belong in prose.
6. **Related phases let the workflows tab link back.** A workflow tied to a
   phase should list the phase ID so readers can jump to its plan.

{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"C:\Users\benja\Documents\custom_portfolio\vendor\get-anything-done\.planning\workflows\gad-findings.md","content":"---
slug: gad-findings
name: GAD Findings Authoring
description: Experiment → raw results → surprise identification → whitepaper writeup → publish. Findings are articles/whitepapers documenting what we tried, what we learned, and what changed in the architecture as a result.
trigger: An experiment completes (eval round, framework change, research pass) AND produced a result that is surprising, counter-intuitive, or load-bearing for a decision.
participants:
  skills: [eval-report, write-feature-doc]
  agents: [default]
  cli: [gad eval verify, gad eval report]
  artifacts: [vendor/get-anything-done/evals/FINDINGS-YYYY-MM-DD-<slug>.md, site/lib/catalog.generated.ts, site/app/findings/[slug]/page.tsx]
parent-workflow: gad-loop
related-phases: [42.2, 42.3]
---

Findings are the public whitepaper output of the GAD research loop
(decision gad-169). Each finding documents one experiment: what the
hypothesis was, what we ran, what the raw results showed, what was
surprising, and what architectural change it drove (or failed to).

The "surprise identification" step is load-bearing. A finding that
just summarizes a result nobody doubted is not worth publishing.
Findings earn their place by changing someone's mental model — the
framework's, the operator's, or a future reader's.

After the writeup lands in `evals/FINDINGS-YYYY-MM-DD-<slug>.md`,
`build-site-data.mjs` picks it up into the FINDINGS catalog and both
`/findings` (index) and `/findings/[slug]` (detail) start rendering
it. Related surfaces (rubric, run hero callouts, phase 45 hero copy)
link to it directly.

```mermaid
flowchart LR
  A[experiment runs] --> B[eval-report skill: capture raw results + metrics]
  B --> C[identify what is surprising]
  C --> D{worth a finding?}
  D -->|no| E[log in .planning/notes/ only]
  D -->|yes| F[write-feature-doc skill: draft FINDINGS-YYYY-MM-DD-<slug>.md]
  F --> G[hypothesis, evidence, interpretation, architectural impact]
  G --> H[commit finding file]
  H --> I[build-site-data picks up into FINDINGS catalog]
  I --> J[/findings index + /findings/[slug] render]
  J --> K[link from related site surfaces]
```

{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"C:\Users\benja\Documents\custom_portfolio\vendor\get-anything-done\skills\gad-visual-context-system\SKILL.md","content":"---
name: gad-visual-context-system
description: >-
  Build and maintain a UX pattern for visual-context identity in any app where a coding
  agent needs to act on "the thing I'm pointing at". The skill has two workflows —
  one for bringing the system into a project that doesn't have it yet (design/spec),
  and one for enforcing/extending the system in a project that already has it
  (maintenance/rollout). Use when a user says "I can't find the component I'm
  looking at", "we need a dev panel", "hook up the visual context system in
  <new app/game>", or "normalize the ids across <existing app>".
source_phase: "44"
source_evolution: 2026-04-14-001
status: proto
parent_skill: gad-visual-context-panel-identities
---

# gad-visual-context-system

A **UX pattern for visual-context-building during software development with
coding agents.** The value is not the DOM highlighting — it's the bridge
between *what a human sees in the UI* and *what a coding agent can unambiguously
act on from source*.

The skill has **one entrypoint** and **two workflows**, chosen by the shape of
the project it's running in.

## Core invariant (both workflows)

Every user-facing region has a **source-searchable identity literal** — a token
that appears verbatim in a source file and can be found with plain `grep`. No
runtime-generated ids. No template-literal concatenation that only exists after
render. No `useId()` for anything the user is meant to reference.

The three landmark kinds:

- **Section shell** — `SiteSection cid="<route>-<section>-site-section"` (stable literal).
- **Inner landmark** — `Identified as="<ComponentName>"` for targets more specific than the section shell (heading, row, action).
- **Page wrapper (optional)** — only when a page has multiple sections that share a
  single dev-panel band. See
... [truncated 8001 bytes] ...
their own `gad-config.toml` scope,
  Workflow B needs to know which project's `site/app/` to scan. Read
  `planning-config.toml` / `gad-config.toml` first and scope by the project the
  user is currently on.
- **CI guard** — a repo-level script that fails CI when a `SiteSection` lacks
  `cid=` or uses a banned generic literal (`page-site-section`). Mentioned as a
  follow-up in the 44-16 rollout notes.

## References (GAD site — canonical Workflow B target)

- `vendor/get-anything-done/site/components/site/SiteSection.tsx` — section shell
- `vendor/get-anything-done/site/components/devid/Identified.tsx` — inner landmark
- `vendor/get-anything-done/site/components/devid/DevPanel.tsx` — panel surface
- `vendor/get-anything-done/site/components/devid/DevIdAgentPromptDialog.tsx` — handoff dialog
- `vendor/get-anything-done/site/components/devid/SectionRegistry.tsx` — registry of component kinds
- `vendor/get-anything-done/skills/gad-visual-context-panel-identities/SKILL.md` — the existing policy-only skill this proto-skill extends/supersedes
- `vendor/get-anything-done/references/visual-context-panel-methodology.md` — durable methodology doc (phase 44-17)

## Relationship to parent skill

The existing `gad-visual-context-panel-identities` skill in `skills/` is **the
policy-only contract** for the GAD site. This proto-skill **supersedes** it by
adding Workflow A (design/build in a new project) on top of the same invariant.
On promotion, consider either:

- **Promote and deprecate parent** — this skill fully contains the parent's
  content. Parent moves to `skills/.archive/` or is deleted.
- **Promote as sibling** — parent remains the canonical GAD-site policy, this
  skill becomes the meta/multi-project version. Higher surface area, clearer
  separation.

Recommendation: **promote and deprecate parent**. The policy lives in this
skill's Workflow B section; the parent file adds no information the new skill
doesn't.

inputs.content:
---
slug: gad-visual-context-identity
name: GAD Visual Context Identity
description: UI development workflow where every user-facing region gets a source-searchable identity literal (`SiteSection cid="..."` / `Identified as="..."`) so the Dev Panel → copy-token → agent-prompt handoff path works. The bridge between what a human sees in the UI and what a coding agent can unambiguously act on from source.
trigger: A component, section, page, or route is being added, restructured, or reviewed in a project that uses the Visual Context Panel system — OR the operator reports "I copied a token from the dev panel and can't find it in source" or a CI audit flags `SiteSection` without `cid=`.
participants:
  skills: [gad-visual-context-system, gad-visual-context-panel-identities]
  agents: [default]
  cli: []
  artifacts: [site/components/site/SiteSection.tsx, site/components/devid/Identified.tsx, site/components/devid/DevPanel.tsx, site/components/devid/DevIdAgentPromptDialog.tsx, site/app/**/page.tsx]
parent-workflow: gad-loop
related-phases: [44, 44.5]
---

The Visual Context Panel is a dev-only overlay that lets the operator
hover a UI region, copy a stable source-search literal, and hand that
literal off to a coding agent as a landmark. It only works if the
landmark appears **verbatim in a source file** — no runtime-generated
ids, no template-literal concatenation, no `useId()`. Decision gad-187
locks `SiteSection cid="<route>-<section>-site-section"` as the
default section identity; `Identified as="<ComponentName>"` handles
inner landmarks more specific than the section shell.

This workflow is the development-time discipline that keeps the
invariant true as the UI grows. Every new section, every new route,
every component rename walks through it. The `gad-visual-context-system`
skill is the policy source of truth; this workflow is the expected
graph the trace pipeline matches against when mining `/planning`
Workflows tab.

The operator-facing loop is: (1) bring up the dev-server with the Dev
Panel mounted, (2) hover the target region in the browser, (3) click
the token to copy it, (4) paste-search the token in the editor and
confirm exactly one hit in source. Zero hits = typo or runtime id.
Multiple hits = ambiguity that needs a route-qualified literal. The
single-hit invariant is what the agent-prompt handoff rides on.

```mermaid
flowchart TD
  A[new section / route / rename lands] --> B[wrap in SiteSection cid=route-section-site-section]
  B --> C[add Identified as=ComponentName for inner landmarks]
  C --> D[dev-server up, Dev Panel mounted]
  D --> E[hover target, click to copy token]
  E --> F[paste-search token in editor]
  F --> G{one source hit?}
  G -->|zero| H[fix typo or remove runtime id]
  G -->|multi| I[qualify with route slug]
  G -->|one| J[copy-with-agent-prompt handoff]
  H --> F
  I --> F
  J --> K[agent edits the exact landmark]
```

outputs:
---
slug: gad-visual-context-identity
name: GAD Visual Context Identity
description: UI development workflow where every user-facing region gets a source-searchable identity literal (`SiteSection cid="..."` / `Identified as="..."`) so the Dev Panel → copy-token → agent-prompt handoff path works. The bridge between what a human sees in the UI and what a coding agent can unambiguously act on from source.
trigger: A component, section, page, or route is being added, restructured, or reviewed in a project that uses the Visual Context Panel system — OR the operator reports "I copied a token from the dev panel and can't find it in source" or a CI audit flags `SiteSection` without `cid=`.
participants:
  skills: [gad-visual-context-system, gad-visual-context-panel-identities]
  agents: [default]
  cli: []
  artifacts: [site/components/site/SiteSection.tsx, site/components/devid/Identified.tsx, site/components/devid/DevPanel.tsx, site/components/devid/DevIdAgentPromptDialog.tsx, site/app/**/page.tsx]
parent-workflow: gad-loop
related-phases: [44, 44.5]
---

The Visual Context Panel is a dev-only overlay that lets the operator
hover a UI region, copy a stable source-search literal, and hand that
literal off to a coding agent as a landmark. It only works if the
landmark appears **verbatim in a source file** — no runtime-generated
ids, no template-literal concatenation, no `useId()`. Decision gad-187
locks `SiteSection cid="<route>-<section>-site-section"` as the
default section identity; `Identified as="<ComponentName>"` handles
inner landmarks more specific than the section shell.

This workflow is the development-time discipline that keeps the
invariant true as the UI grows. Every new section, every new route,
every component rename walks through it. The `gad-visual-context-system`
skill is the policy source of truth; this workflow is the expected
graph the trace pipeline matches against when mining `/planning`
Workflows tab.

The operator-facing loop is: (1) bring up the dev-server with the Dev
Panel mounted, (2) hover the target region in the browser, (3) click
the token to copy it, (4) paste-search the token in the editor and
confirm exactly one hit in source. Zero hits = typo or runtime id.
Multiple hits = ambiguity that needs a route-qualified literal. The
single-hit invariant is what the agent-prompt handoff rides on.

```mermaid
flowchart TD
  A[new section / route / rename lands] --> B[wrap in SiteSection cid=route-section-site-section]
  B --> C[add Identified as=ComponentName for inner landmarks]
  C --> D[dev-server up, Dev Panel mounted]
  D --> E[hover target, click to copy token]
  E --> F[paste-search token in editor]
  F --> G{one source hit?}
  G -->|zero| H[fix typo or remove runtime id]
  G -->|multi| I[qualify with route slug]
  G -->|one| J[copy-with-agent-prompt handoff]
  H --> F
  I --> F
  J --> K[agent edits the exact landmark]
```

{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"C:\Users\benja\Documents\custom_portfolio\vendor\get-anything-done\.planning\workflows\gad-evolution.md","content":"---
slug: gad-evolution
name: GAD Evolution Loop
description: Pressure → candidate → proto-skill → validated → promoted/discarded. The mechanism by which GAD's skill set evolves from observed high-pressure phases into new distributable skills.
trigger: `compute-self-eval` detects a phase with high pressure metrics (tool-use burst, repeated corrections, long loops, novel failure patterns), OR an operator manually runs `gad evolution` against pending candidates.
participants:
  skills: [create-proto-skill, gad-evolution-evolve, gad-evolution-validator, merge-skill, gad-skill-creator]
  agents: [default]
  cli: [gad evolution status, gad evolution validate, gad evolution similarity, gad evolution shed, gad evolution promote, gad evolution discard]
  artifacts: [skills/candidates/<slug>/CANDIDATE.md, .planning/proto-skills/<slug>/PROVENANCE.md, .planning/proto-skills/<slug>/SKILL.md, .planning/proto-skills/<slug>/VALIDATION.md, skills/.shed/<slug>, skills/<name>/]
parent-workflow: gad-loop
related-phases: [42, 42.2, 42.3, 44]
---

GAD's self-improvement loop. Pressure in a phase is a signal that some
repeatable pattern is missing from the skill set; the evolution loop
turns that signal into a distributable skill over three stages.

Stage 1 is automatic (compute-self-eval writes raw CANDIDATE.md). Stage 2
is agentic (the `create-proto-skill` skill drafts in bulk with per-
candidate checkpoints per decision gad-171, then `gad-evolution-validator`
writes VALIDATION.md advisorily). Stage 2b folds in housekeeping: run
`gad evolution similarity --against sprint` to surface candidates that
have gone obsolete against the current sprint window and bulk-remove
them with `gad evolution shed`, and run the `merge-skill` skill against
any remaining proto-skill pairs that cover the same ground. Stage 3 is
human — review and decide promote-or-discard. The loop gates on zero
pending proto-skills so humans stay in the approval path.

Proto-skill is a permanent type (decision gad-167): PROVENANCE.md
travels with the skill through promotion so we can triage by pressure
origin forever. Shed markers in `skills/.shed/` persist against
self-eval regeneration so obsolete candidates don't reappear.

```mermaid
flowchart TD
  A[compute-self-eval detects pressure] --> B[write skills/candidates/<slug>/CANDIDATE.md]
  B --> C[gad-evolution-evolve skill orchestrates]
  C --> D[create-proto-skill skill: bulk draft]
  D --> E[write PROVENANCE.md + SKILL.md]
  E --> F[gad-evolution-validator: VALIDATION.md advisory]
  F --> G[gad evolution similarity against sprint]
  G --> H[gad evolution shed obsolete]
  H --> I[merge-skill: fold overlapping proto-skills]
  I --> J[human review]
  J --> K{promote?}
  K -->|yes| L[gad evolution promote -> skills/<name>/]
  K -->|no| M[gad evolution discard]
```

<summary>The canonical GAD loop for any agent, any coding tool:
1. `gad snapshot --projectid <id>` â€” one command, full context. No reading 15 files manually.
2. Pick one task from TASK-REGISTRY.xml (status=planned).
3. Implement it.
4. Update TASK-REGISTRY.xml (mark done), STATE.xml (next-action), DECISIONS.xml (if new decisions).
5. Commit.

That's it. AGENTS.md files in each project reference this loop but do NOT redefine it. The vendor/get-anything-done/ project is the canonical source. Root AGENTS.md and .planning/AGENTS.md are consumers that point here, not parallel definitions.

Skills are methodology documents (SKILL.md) â€” agents read and follow them. They are not CLI commands or executable plugins. The GAD CLI provides context efficiency. Skills provide methodology.</summary>
  <impact>Root AGENTS.md mandatory read order must be simplified. Agents should NOT read 15 files on startup â€” gad snapshot exists for this. AGENTS.md files across vendor projects reference the GAD loop, they don't each define their own version. inputs.content:
# Human workflows

Hand-authored descriptions of what the **human operator** does at the
keyboard — daily routines, sit-down-to-work procedures, review gates, and
similar "meatspace" procedures. These are NOT agent workflows.

## How this differs from `.planning/workflows/`

| Aspect | `.planning/workflows/` (agent) | `.planning/human-workflows/` (human) |
|---|---|---|
| Subject | AI agent's expected skill / agent / CLI graph | Operator's expected sequence of actions |
| Trace-matched? | Yes — conformance score from `.trace-events.jsonl` | No — no trace pipeline matches these |
| Frontmatter | `participants`, `trigger`, `parent-workflow`, `origin` | `operator`, `frequency`, `triggers`, `projects-touched` |
| Mermaid | Required (parser skips files without it) | Optional — body is mostly prose |
| Surface | Planning → Workflows tab | Planning → Human Workflows tab |

Agent workflows describe what a model is expected to do when invoked.
Human workflows describe what the operator is expected to do when they sit
down at the monorepo root. Keep them separate: merging them would confuse
trace matching and dilute both surfaces.

## Authoring

Each file is a Markdown doc with YAML frontmatter. Required fields:

- `slug` — stable id, usually same as filename
- `name` — human-readable title
- `operator` — always `human` (reserved for future `pair` / `team` values)
- `frequency` — `daily`, `weekly`, `on-demand`, etc.
- `triggers` — list of situations that kick off this workflow
- `projects-touched` — list of GAD project ids the workflow touches
- `related-phases` — list of related planning phase ids

The body is free-form prose describing the procedure. A `flowchart LR`
Mermaid block is encouraged but optional.

## Why this exists

Expectation mismatches between the operator and the planning docs (e.g.
"I didn't expect tasks to land in the global `.planning/`") should show up
as explicit workflow gaps, not tribal knowledge. Eventually these will be
cross-referenced against trace data (task 44-21) to detect when an observed
session diverges from the expected human routine.

inputs.content:
---
slug: daily-driver
name: Daily driver routine
operator: human
frequency: daily
triggers: [sit-down, post-compact, standup]
projects-touched: [get-anything-done, global]
related-phases: [44]
---

# Daily driver routine

What the operator does every time they sit down at the `custom_portfolio`
monorepo root. This is the meatspace counterpart to the canonical GAD agent
loop in decision **gad-18**, and it is what keeps the planning docs honest
across sessions.

The first action is always the same: open the repo at its root (not inside
a vendored project), eyeball `git status` for drift carried over from the
previous session, then run `gad snapshot --projectid <id>` for whichever
project is the day's focus. The snapshot is the single hydration point —
never re-read planning files by hand, never guess state from memory.
If the day is "framework work on GAD itself," the project id is
`get-anything-done`; if it is "tracking monorepo-level chores" it is
`global`; anything else, ask.

Once state is loaded, the operator picks exactly one planned task from
the snapshot's task registry, reads the task goal, and begins implementing
it in the normal Claude Code loop. Mid-task, no restarts and no context
re-hydrations happen unilaterally — per gad-17, restart suggestions are
allowed but only at clean planning boundaries.

When the implementation is done and verified (build + typecheck + any
verify command the task specifies), the operator updates three files in
lockstep: `TASK-REGISTRY.xml` (mark the task done with `skill`, `agent`,
and `type` attributes per gad-18), `STATE.xml` (set the `next-action` so
the next snapshot is self-describing), and `DECISIONS.xml` if any new
decisions were captured. Only then is a commit made — one task per commit
where possible — and the loop repeats.

The cadence is deliberately boring. The whole point of the GAD framework
is that the operator does not need a novel procedure each morning; the
snapshot tells them where they are and the loop tells them what to do next.

```mermaid
flowchart LR
  open[Open monorepo root] --> status[git status]
  status --> snapshot[gad snapshot --projectid]
  snapshot --> pick[Pick one planned task]
  pick --> work[Implement + verify]
  work --> update[Update TASK-REGISTRY + STATE + DECISIONS]
  update --> commit[Commit one task]
  commit --> pick
```

---

inputs.content:
---
slug: evolution-review
name: Evolution review gate
operator: human
frequency: weekly
triggers: [new-proto-skill, evolution-status-nonzero]
projects-touched: [get-anything-done]
related-phases: [42.2, 44]
---

# Evolution review gate

The periodic human-in-the-loop review of proto-skills drafted by the
`create-proto-skill` pipeline. Proto-skills are a permanent skill type
(decision gad-167), but before they earn a canonical home in the framework
they need a human to rubber-stamp, reshape, or reject them.

The operator runs `gad evolution status` to see which proto-skills are
awaiting review. For each one, they open the proto-skill's SKILL.md plus
the companion VALIDATION.md that the pipeline produced and read both end
to end. The decision for each candidate is one of three: **promote** it
to a canonical skill (`gad evolution promote <slug>`), **discard** it as
not carrying its own weight (`gad evolution discard <slug>`), or **shed**
it — meaning the idea is good but belongs somewhere else, usually merged
into an existing skill (`gad evolution shed <slug>`).

The review is intentionally gated: the trace pipeline will happily mint
proto-skills all week, but the operator owns the decision about what
becomes part of the canonical surface. Always finish a review batch by
re-running `gad evolution status` to confirm the queue is empty (or that
any remaining items are consciously deferred).

```mermaid
flowchart LR
  status[gad evolution status] --> read[Read SKILL.md + VALIDATION.md]
  read --> decide{Decide}
  decide -->|keep| promote[gad evolution promote]
  decide -->|kill| discard[gad evolution discard]
  decide -->|merge| shed[gad evolution shed]
  promote --> status
  discard --> status
  shed --> status
```

inputs.prompt:
IMPLEMENT task 44-22 in the get-anything-done project. Write code, verify it builds, report back.

Working dir: C:\Users\benja\Documents\custom_portfolio\vendor\get-anything-done

## Context

Prior research already mapped the file surface — use it directly:

- **Tab registry**: `site/app/planning/PlanningTabbedContent.tsx`
  - `BASE_PLANNING_TABS` Set around L30-40
  - `TabsTrigger` list L77-108
  - `TabsContent` blocks L111-171
- **Precedent tab**: `site/app/planning/PlanningWorkflowsTab.tsx` (clone this for the human-workflows tab — the React-Flow/mermaid renderer is kind-agnostic and reusable)
- **Data source**: `site/scripts/build-site-data.mjs`
  - `WORKFLOWS_DIR` const around L165 is hardcoded to `.planning/workflows` — **do NOT break this**
  - `parseWorkflows` function around L1053-1096 parses authored agent workflows
  - Insertion point around L1032-1037 is where catalog.workflows gets wired
  - Exports around L2213 (`export const WORKFLOWS = ...`)
- **Catalog consumer**: `site/lib/catalog.generated.ts` (generated by build-site-data.mjs)
- **Authored workflows dir**: `.planning/workflows/` — do NOT touch, that's the agent-workflows dir

## Goal

Add a `PlanningTabHumanWorkflows` tab that shows hand-authored human-operator workflows (daily routines, sit-down-to-work procedures) as a separate surface from the authored agent workflows that already show on the Workflows tab.

## Deliverables

### 1. Authoring dir + seed files

Create `.planning/human-workflows/` and seed **two** files:

**`.planning/human-workflows/README.md`** — short explainer distinguishing this dir from `.planning/workflows/`: agent workflows describe expected skill/agent/CLI graphs the trace pipeline matches against; human workflows describe what the operator does at the keyboard (no trace matching, no conformance score). Frontmatter shape is intentionally different.

**`.planning/human-workflows/daily-driver.md`** — the operator's daily routine. Frontmatter:
```yaml
---
slug: daily-driver
name: Daily driver routine
operator
... [truncated 1484 bytes] ...
arse-or-die (body is mostly prose; Mermaid is optional). Parse these fields: `slug`, `name`, `operator`, `frequency`, `triggers`, `projects-touched`, `related-phases`, plus the Mermaid block if present.
- Keep the diff minimal; don't refactor unrelated code.

### 3. Tab component

Create `site/app/planning/PlanningTabHumanWorkflows.tsx`:
- Clone shape of `PlanningTabbedContent.tsx`'s existing `PlanningWorkflowsTab` usage — don't reinvent.
- Import `HUMAN_WORKFLOWS` from `@/lib/catalog.generated`.
- Render each workflow as a card: title, operator, frequency, triggers as chips, projects-touched as chips, prose body (first paragraph), and the Mermaid graph if present.
- Wrap the tab body root in `<Identified as="PlanningTabHumanWorkflows" />` per decision gad-187.
- Use `SiteSection cid="planning-human-workflows-site-section"` as the shell (decision gad-187).

### 4. Tab registration

In `site/app/planning/PlanningTabbedContent.tsx`:
- Add `"human-workflows"` to `BASE_PLANNING_TABS`.
- Add `<TabsTrigger value="human-workflows">Human Workflows <Badge>{HUMAN_WORKFLOWS.length}</Badge></TabsTrigger>` next to the existing Workflows trigger.
- Add the matching `<TabsContent value="human-workflows"><PlanningTabHumanWorkflows workflows={humanWorkflows} /></TabsContent>`.
- Thread `humanWorkflows` prop through from `page.tsx` sourcing it from the catalog.
- Keep diffs minimal; match surrounding style.

## Verify

Run these from the submodule root:
```
node bin/gad.cjs workflow validate --projectid get-anything-done 2>&1 | tail -20
cd site && bunx tsc --noEmit 2>&1 | tail -30
```

Fix any tsc errors before reporting done. Ignore site-data warnings unrelated to your changes.

## Report back

Under 300 words. Include:
1. Files created (full repo-relative paths)
2. Files modified (full repo-relative paths + line ranges)
3. tsc result (clean or errors)
4. Any design calls you had to make that differ from this brief and why
5. Whether the parseWorkflows parameterization risked breaking existing WORKFLOWS export (confirm or flag)

inputs.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat: ship 44-22 (human workflows tab) + 44-26 (cross-config domain-change proto-skill)

Two tasks closed in parallel subagents:

## 44-22: PlanningTabHumanWorkflows
- .planning/human-workflows/ — new authoring dir distinct from agent
  workflows. Two seeds: daily-driver.md (the sit-down-to-work routine),
  evolution-review.md (the weekly human-review gate on proto-skills).
  Frontmatter shape diverges from agent workflows: operator, frequency,
  triggers, projects-touched; no participants, no parent-workflow,
  Mermaid optional.
- site/scripts/build-site-data.mjs — parseHumanWorkflows sibling
  function + HumanWorkflow interface + HUMAN_WORKFLOWS export. Chose
  sibling over parameterizing parseWorkflows: smaller diff, zero risk
  to the existing WORKFLOWS export (still parses 7 agent workflows).
- site/app/planning/PlanningTabHumanWorkflows.tsx (new) — tab component
  wrapped in Identified as="PlanningTabHumanWorkflows" (decision gad-187).
  Renders each workflow as a card with chips for triggers / projects
  touched, prose body, and optional Mermaid.
- site/app/planning/PlanningTabbedContent.tsx + page.tsx — registered
  as "human-workflows" in BASE_PLANNING_TABS, threaded HUMAN_WORKFLOWS
  from catalog through TabsTrigger/TabsContent.
- tsc clean.

## 44-26: gad-cross-config-domain-change proto-skill
- .planning/proto-skills/gad-cross-config-domain-change/{SKILL.md,
  PROVENANCE.md,VALIDATION.md} — single-entrypoint skill, two workflows
  (A monorepo-wide across all [[planning.roots]] + [[evals.roots]],
  B single-project scoped to one .planning/ root). Replaces the
  discarded phase-43-eval-vocabulary draft that baked in
  apps/public/evals. Pre-flight config audit, git-mv-through-temp,
  planning-doc migration, translation-note DECISIONS template, worked
  example for an evals → species rename. Core invariant: every
  occurrence of the old term across every declared root is either
  rewritten or preserved behind an aliased shim with a DECISIONS
  translation-note, post-rename grep census returns zero outside that
  shim, every project's snapshot still parses clean.
- gad evolution validate: 11/13 refs, 2/4 CLI; non-blocking for proto.
- Sits in .planning/proto-skills/ pending human review gate.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -8

## Context

Prior research already mapped the surface — act on it, don't re-research:

- **Tab registry** (just got a precedent from 44-22 for adding new tabs): `site/app/planning/PlanningTabbedContent.tsx` — `BASE_PLANNING_TABS` Set, `TabsTrigger`, `TabsContent` pattern. Look at how `human-workflows` was just registered there and mirror it.

  - `loadTraceEvents({ scope: "gad-framework" })` around L1145-1197 already exposes the parsed event stream

  - Exports at the bottom of the file (search for `export const WORKFLOWS`, `export const HUMAN_WORKFLOWS` — add `export const SIGNAL` alongside)
- **Trace events shape** (`.planning/.trace-events.jsonl`): each line has `ts`, `seq`, `type` (`tool_use` / `file_mutation`), `tool` (Bash/Read/Edit/Write/Glob/Grep/WebFetch), `inputs.file_path`, `inputs.command`, `inputs.url`, `trigger_skill` (nullable), `agent.agent_id`, `agent.agent_role`, `scope` (`gad-framework` / `eval-agent`), `runtime.session_id`.
- **Catalog consumer**: `site/lib/catalog.generated.ts` (generated, do not hand-edit — but your new export must land there via the build script)

## Goal

Add a `PlanningTabSignal` tab under `/planning` that mines `.planning/.trace-events.jsonl` for agent-behavior analytics. It reads today's tool/skill/file mix — NO new pipeline work, NO new trace events, just reducers over what loadTraceEvents already returns.

## Scope — keep it tight

v1 ships these views only:

1. **Top files by tool_use count** — group by `inputs.file_path` (fall back to `inputs.notebook_path`), skip nulls, normalize to
... [truncated 2054 bytes] ...
th rank, path (monospace), count
- **Top Skills** — table with rank, skill name, count
- **Tool Mix** — horizontal bar chart using existing UI primitives if any, else a simple flex bar row per tool (label, width=pct%, count). No new chart library.
- **Agent Split** — two-cell summary (Default N, Subagents M) + one-line "Top subagent roles: …"

Wrap body in `<Identified as="PlanningTabSignal" />` and shell in `SiteSection cid="planning-signal-site-section"` (decision gad-187).

Same pattern as the just-landed `human-workflows` tab:
- Add `"signal"` to `BASE_PLANNING_TABS`
- Thread `signal` prop
- Add `TabsTrigger` with total-events badge
- Add `TabsContent` rendering `<PlanningTabSignal signal={signal} />`

### 4. Page wiring in `site/app/planning/page.tsx`

Import `SIGNAL` from `@/lib/catalog.generated`, pass as `signal={SIGNAL}` prop.

### 5. Mark task done

Update `.planning/TASK-REGISTRY.xml` task `id="44-21"`:
- `status="done"`, `skill="default"`, `agent="general-purpose"`, `type="site"`
- Add `<resolution>` block summarizing what shipped and what was deferred to v2

### 6. STATE next-action

Use `node bin/gad.cjs state set-next-action --projectid get-anything-done "..."` — one line describing 44-21 landed and what the next task is (44-28 site-install discuss-phase). Hard cap 600 chars.

## Verify

```
node bin/gad.cjs workflow validate --projectid get-anything-done 2>&1 | tail -20
cd site && bunx tsc --noEmit 2>&1 | tail -30
```

`workflow validate` should still show 7 agent workflows + 2 human workflows unchanged. tsc must be clean. Fix any type errors before reporting.

## Report back

Under 400 words. Include:
1. Files created (full repo-relative paths)
2. Files modified (full repo-relative paths + short diff summary)
3. tsc result (clean or errors)
4. A sample of the generated SIGNAL analytics (just the topFiles top 5 + topSkills top 5 + toolMix) so I can sanity-check the reducer output
5. Any design calls you had to make

inputs.old_string:
<task id="44-21" type="site" status="planned"><goal>Trace-based agent-behavior analytics on /planning. New tab (Identified `PlanningTabSignal` or similar) that mines `.trace-events.jsonl` for: (a) most-visited files ranked by tool_use count across Read/Edit/Write/Glob, normalized to repo-relative paths; (b) tool mix histogram (Bash/Read/Edit/Grep/WebFetch share %); (c) file-path frequency heatmap (by directory); (d) Bash command extraction (separate path refs inside `inputs.command` from the tool name); (e) URL route frequency from WebFetch. Cross-reference the observed patterns against authored workflow participants (44-20 is the authored surface) to surface conformance gaps at the file-path level instead of just the tool level. User ask: "I want to be able to see where the coding agent goes most of the time and what tool it is using — looking for repeating patterns." Data source already exists; gap is the aggregation/rank/visualization layer.</goal>
      <keywords>planning-tab,signal,trace-analytics,file-visits,tool-mix,heatmap</keywords>
    </task>
inputs.new_string:
<task id="44-21" type="site" status="done" skill="default" agent="general-purpose"><goal>Trace-based agent-behavior analytics on /planning. New tab (Identified `PlanningTabSignal` or similar) that mines `.trace-events.jsonl` for: (a) most-visited files ranked by tool_use count across Read/Edit/Write/Glob, normalized to repo-relative paths; (b) tool mix histogram (Bash/Read/Edit/Grep/WebFetch share %); (c) file-path frequency heatmap (by directory); (d) Bash command extraction (separate path refs inside `inputs.command` from the tool name); (e) URL route frequency from WebFetch. Cross-reference the observed patterns against authored workflow participants (44-20 is the authored surface) to surface conformance gaps at the file-path level instead of just the tool level. User ask: "I want to be able to see where the coding agent goes most of the time and what tool it is using — looking for repeating patterns." Data source already exists; gap is the aggregation/rank/visualization layer.</goal>
      <resolution>Closed 2026-04-15. v1 ships PlanningTabSignal with four pure-reducer views over the gad-framework slice of `.planning/.trace-events.jsonl`: top 20 files (normalized repo-relative, abs-prefix stripped), top 20 skills (trigger_skill — currently empty until skills start tagging per gad-178), tool mix percentage histogram, default vs subagent split with role breakdown. Added `synthesizeSignalAnalytics(events)` to site/scripts/build-site-data.mjs, lifted the gad-framework loadTraceEvents call out of synthesizeWorkflowTraceData so both consumers share one array (no double-load). New `Signal` interface + `SIGNAL` export in catalog.generated.ts. Tab registered in PlanningTabbedContent (`signal` in BASE_PLANNING_TABS, badge shows totalEvents) and wired through page.tsx. Sample on current trace: 798 tool_use events, top file vendor/get-anything-done/bin/gad.cjs (90), tool mix Bash 45.7% / Read 21.4% / Edit 20.8%, default 382 vs sub 416. Deferred to v2 (TODO comment in synthesizer): Bash command path extraction, WebFetch URL frequency, tool/skill n-gram sequences, per-session trends. workflow validate still shows 7 authored + 2 human workflows; tsc clean.</resolution>
      <keywords>planning-tab,signal,trace-analytics,file-visits,tool-mix,heatmap</keywords>
    </task>

52: project: string; 293: "bodyHtml": "

framework-upgrade

The GAD framework is the experimental apparatus. When you upgrade the apparatus while running experiments, cross-round comparisons get noisy — a run might score better because the agent got better, OR because you rewrote a skill, OR because you swapped in a different subagent. This skill is the methodology for shipping framework changes without poisoning the eval dataset.

When to trigger this skill

  • Adding, removing, or meaningfully editing a skill document.
  • Adding, removing, or changing the tools list of a subagent.
  • Rewiring a command's agent: frontmatter (different subagent spawned).
  • Editing AGENTS.md anywhere in the framework root.
  • Changing the gad snapshot / gad context output format.
  • Revising the composite scoring formula or weights.
  • Upgrading the trace schema.
  • Anything else that would cause "re-run the v5 escape-the-dungeon test" to behave materially differently than the original v5 run.

What NOT to trigger it for

  • Typo fixes in documentation comments.
  • Adding a test that doesn't change runtime behaviour.
  • Refactoring internal CLI code where the public interface is unchanged.
  • Cosmetic edits to the portfolio site (that's portfolio-sync territory).

Branch strategy

main                 — current framework, what eval agents run against by default
version/v1.0         — frozen snapshot, tagged at the v1.0 milestone close
version/v1.1         — frozen snapshot, tagged at the v1.1 milestone close
version/v1.2-wip     — work in progress for an upcoming version bump

Rules:

  1. main = current. Eval agents always run against main unless explicitly told to check out a frozen ... [truncated 25904 bytes] ... s="language-bash">MARKER=".planning/.eval-runs/${PROJECT}-${VERSION}.json" if [ -f "$MARKER" ]; then echo "Run id $PROJECT-$VERSION already has a marker at $MARKER. Refusing to overwrite. Inspect, resolve, then delete the marker to retry." exit 1 fi mkdir -p .planning/.eval-runs

  2. Create an isolated worktree:

    WORKTREE_PATH=".claude/worktrees/agent-${PROJECT}-${VERSION}"
git worktree add "$WORKTREE_PATH" "$BASELINE"

    Prefer .claude/worktrees/agent-* (already managed by gad worktree list/clean) over mktemp so the orchestrator can find the worktree later even across session boundaries.

  3. Write the session marker (status: running):

    {
  "runId": "escape-the-dungeon-v7",
  "project": "escape-the-dungeon",
  "version": "v7",
  "species": "gad",
  "generation": 7,
  "runtime": "claude-code",
  "baseline": "HEAD",
  "worktreePath": ".claude/worktrees/agent-escape-the-dungeon-v7",
  "runDir": "vendor/get-anything-done/evals/escape-the-dungeon/v7",
  "agentId": null,
  "startedAt": "2026-04-14T18:00:00Z",
  "status": "running"
}

    Write this to .planning/.eval-runs/<runId>.json. The species / generation split follows the phase 43 vocabulary; until that rename lands, version (e.g. v7) is the authoritative field and generation is the numeric suffix.

  4. Spawn the builder subagent via the Agent tool: Launch with:

    • subagent_type: "general-purpose"

inputs.prompt:
RESEARCH ONLY — do not write code. I need a scoping report on the "current project" signal inside the GAD site at vendor/get-anything-done/site/.

Context: the site renders data for multiple GAD projects (get-anything-done itself, grime-time-site, repub-builder, mb-cli-framework, etc.). Projects are registered in `vendor/get-anything-done/gad-config.toml` under `[[planning.roots]]` and `[[evals.roots]]`. Right now some surfaces filter by project (e.g. /planning tabs seem to), others don't. I want to add full-surface scoping — a single current-project signal that every page filters against.

Report the following under 600 words, tables where structured, full repo-relative file paths:

1. **Existing project-ID plumbing.** How does the site know "which project am I looking at" today? Look at:
   - `site/lib/catalog.generated.ts` — which exports are project-scoped vs global?
   - `site/app/planning/page.tsx` and `PlanningTabbedContent.tsx` — do they accept a projectid prop or query param?
   - `site/app/projects/[...id]/page.tsx` if it exists — that's clearly scoped
   - any `useSearchParams` / `projectid` query params / context providers
   - Is there a global React context for current project, or is it threaded prop-by-prop?

2. **What's already scoped vs not.** For each top-level route, report scoped | partial | global:
   - `/` (home)
   - `/planning`
   - `/skills`
   - `/findings` (+ /findings/[slug])
   - `/project-market`
   - `/projects/[id]` (if exists)
   - `/workflows` (if exists — else note absent)
   - `/formulas`
   - anything else notable

3. **Data-source scoping in build-site-data.mjs.** Does the build script attach project IDs to every record it emits? For each major catalog export (`WORKFLOWS`, `HUMAN_WORKFLOWS`, `SIGNAL`, `SKILLS`, `FINDINGS`, `PROJECTS`, etc.), note whether records carry a `projectid` field. This is the pivot for filtering.

4. **Top-level layout / nav**. Where is the site shell (layout.tsx)? Is there already a project picker in the nav? If so, where's its state stored (URL, context, localStorage)? If not, where would one go?

5. **gad-config.toml reader.** How does the build script know the set of registered projects? Cite the file + function.

6. **Recommended scoping architecture.** In one paragraph, propose the simplest architecture that would let every route filter by current project without a big refactor. Options:
   - URL segment: `/[projectid]/planning`, `/[projectid]/skills`, etc. — Next.js dynamic route rewrite
   - Query param: `?projectid=<id>` threaded through every page via `useSearchParams`
   - React context + cookie/localStorage persistence
   - Build-time split: separate static builds per project
   
   Pick one, explain why, cite the constraints (must not break existing routes, must let consumer's installed copy default to "their" project).

Use full file paths. Skip any surface that is clearly out of scope for project-filtering (e.g. /about, /methodology).

<task id="44-28" type="meta" status="planned"><goal>UMBRELLA (see decision gad-188): site-as-project-management-UI + Windows installer + release pipeline. Discuss-phase 2026-04-14 reframed this from a thin "gad site install" task into a 5-sub-task spine. User's atomic decisions: no npm publishing ever (distribution is GitHub releases of get-anything-done, pulled from our site); Windows-first with a real lightweight installer; no contamination of consumer repo (installer writes only to .planning/, .claude/, .codex/, .agent/, etc. — .planning is the safe zone); site lives at .planning/site/ as a self-contained Next.js server runtime with a launcher.exe for double-click read-only operation; installer prompts {site, skills, planning, node} with args to bypass prompts; skills branch gated on node+gad.exe present; no backward-compat; consumer baseline is "has a computer, finds the repo, downloads the installer, reads README, double-clicks launcher"; AI coding agent install is on the consumer. Split into: 44-30 (A1 data scoping — ships standalone), 44-31 (B0 release pipeline foundation), 44-32 (B2 installer extension — new flags + release-zip source), 44-33 (B3 site packaging for .planning/site/), 44-34 (B4 portable node bootstrap). Dropped from original plan: B1 (gad.exe build — already exists as Node SEA, see dist/release/), and B5 (runtime rewriters — already shipped in bin/install.js for 9 coding agents). This task stays open as the tracking umbrella until all 5 sub-tasks close.</goal>

    </task>
    <task id="44-30" type="site" status="planned"><goal>44-28.A1 — Full-surface data scoping by current-project signal. Ships independent of the installer track. Add a global `currentProject` React context (populated from `?projectid=<id>` query param, falling back to the first `[[planning.roots]]` id in gad-config.toml, falling back to "get-anything-done"). Thread the hook `useCurrentProject()` through every us

(e) Build target + release artifact: `site-<version>.zip` containing site/ + .node/ + launcher.exe + README.txt. (f) Wire into 44-32's `--site` branch: installer downloads the zip from the release and extracts it to `<target>/.planning/site/`. Depends on 44-31 (release pipeline) + 44-32 (installer --site flag). Decision point during planning: does the packaged site rebuild its lib/catalog.generated.ts against the consumer's planning data on first launch, or does it ship with prebuilt GAD framework data baked in and only read live project data? Recommend: first-launch rebuild against consumer's gad-config.toml.</goal>

{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"C:\Users\benja\Documents\custom_portfolio\vendor\get-anything-done\.planning\DECISIONS.xml","content":"  <decision id="gad-187">
    <title>Visual Context Panel naming contract: section cid is the default identity; Identified is optional depth</title>
    <summary>Human-in-the-loop feedback from the /skeptic and /projects surfaces converged on one stable requirement: operators must be able to copy a visible identifier and grep it directly in source for fast update/delete handoff prompts. Policy: the primary section identity is an explicit literal cid on SiteSection (example: project-skills-scope-section-site-section), and this is the default token the Visual Context Panel should surface for section-level targeting. Identified as="..." remains supported but optional; it is reserved for inner-block granularity where a section-level token is insufficient. Runtime-generated ids are not acceptable as primary prompt handles because they are not source-searchable.</summary>
    <impact>(1) Authoring rule for new work: every user-facing SiteSection gets an explicit literal cid. (2) Refactor rule: remove redundant Identified wrappers that only restate the same section identity; keep them only when they add useful sub-region targeting. (3) UX rule: maintain one panel surface (Visual Context Panel) and avoid duplicate inside/outside panel variants that diverge in identifier quality. (4) Skill support: this contract is documented in skills/gad-visual-context-panel-identities/SKILL.md so future agents apply the same pattern consistently.</impact>
  </decision>
</decisions>

<decision id="gad-187">
    <title>Visual Context Panel naming contract: section cid is the default identity; Identified is optional depth</title>
    <summary>Human-in-the-loop feedback from the /skeptic and /projects surfaces converged on one stable requirement: operators must be able to copy a visible identifier and grep it directly in source for fast update/delete handoff prompts. Policy: the primary section identity is an explicit literal cid on SiteSection (example: project-skills-scope-section-site-section), and this is the default token the Visual Context Panel should surface for section-level targeting. Identified as="..." remains supported but optional; it is reserved for inner-block granularity where a section-level token is insufficient. Runtime-generated ids are not acceptable as primary prompt handles because they are not source-searchable.</summary>
    <impact>(1) Authoring rule for new work: every user-facing SiteSection gets an explicit literal cid. (2) Refactor rule: remove redundant Identified wrappers that only restate the same section identity; keep them only when they add useful sub-region targeting. (3) UX rule: maintain one panel surface (Visual Context Panel) and avoid duplicate inside/outside panel variants that diverge in identifier quality. (4) Skill support: this contract is documented in skills/gad-visual-context-panel-identities/SKILL.md so future agents apply the same pattern consistently.</impact>
  </decision>
</decisions>

  <decision id="gad-188">
    <title>44-28 umbrella: installer + release pipeline + data scoping — distribution is GitHub releases, not npm, with a Windows-first lightweight installer writing only into .planning/ and agent dirs</title>
    <summary>Discuss-phase 2026-04-14 on task 44-28 produced a 12-point decision set that reframes the original thin "gad site install" scope into an umbrella covering 5 sub-tasks (44-30 through 44-34). Locked: (1) NO npm publishing ever — we ship from GitHub releases of github.com/MagicbornStudios/get-anything-done pulled from our sit
... [truncated 1701 bytes] ...
READY shipped in bin/install.js which is a 5993-line GSD-forked installer supporting 9 coding-agent runtimes with per-runtime path rewrites). What's actually new work: 44-30 (A1 data scoping, ships immediately), 44-31 (B0 release pipeline foundation), 44-32 (B2 installer extension: --site/--planning/--node flags + --from-release download source mode + feature-picker interactive reshape + skills gating), 44-33 (B3 self-contained site packaging with launcher.exe), 44-34 (B4 portable Node bootstrap). Dependency spine: A1 independent + parallelizable. B0 must land before B2/B3/B4 (nothing for a consumer to pull from otherwise). B3 and B4 can run in parallel after B2.</summary>
    <impact>(1) Phase 44 gains a 5-task umbrella that sprawls beyond original phase-44 scope — candidate for promoting to a standalone phase 44.6 or similar in a follow-up cleanup, but leaving in phase 44 for now to avoid roadmap churn. (2) The dist/release/gad-v1.32.0-windows-x64.exe on the current machine is 3 days stale (10+ commits behind bin/gad.cjs) and has never been published anywhere — every "consumer downloads from our release" claim is aspirational until 44-31 lands. (3) bin/install.js is the single biggest asset we have for 44-32 — fork-and-extend, not rewrite. (4) Data scoping (44-30) can ship immediately and independently; the Track B installer work is sequenced behind release-pipeline foundation. (5) pnpm-workspace layout is killed — earlier discuss-phase hypothesis about apps/site + packages/gad-cli is abandoned in favor of the fenced .planning/site/ model. (6) "Consumer baseline has nothing but a computer" drives the portable-Node branch (44-34) and the bundled-Node site-launcher design (44-33). (7) No GitHub Actions in scope — skills + workflows authored via create-skill + gh CLI are the release automation. (8) This decision is the authoritative discuss-phase output for task 44-28 in lieu of a phases/44-28/KICKOFF.md file — sub-task goal bodies carry the per-task KICKOFF content.</impact>
  </decision>
</decisions>

<task id="44-30" type="site" status="planned"><goal>44-28.A1 — Full-surface data scoping by current-project signal. Ships independent of the installer track. Add a global `currentProject` React context (populated from `?projectid=<id>` query param, falling back to the first `[[planning.roots]]` id in gad-config.toml, falling back to "get-anything-done"). Thread the hook `useCurrentProject()` through every user-facing route and filter records by project where records already carry a project field (FINDINGS.projects string[], BUGS.project, EVAL_RUNS.project, EVAL_PROJECTS.project, REQUIREMENTS_HISTORY.project, PLANNING_ZIPS.project). Add a project picker dropdown in NavActions that reads the list of registered projects (new helper `site/lib/project-config.ts` that loads gad-config.toml at build time into a typed constant), writes the selection to the URL, and persists nothing (URL is the source of truth). Routes that MUST filter: /planning (already filters BUGS hardcoded to "gad" — replace with context), /findings (+ /findings/[slug]), /project-market. Routes that stay framework-global for now: /skills, /formulas, /glossary, /, /about, /methodology, /workflows, /lineage. Planning/tasks/phases/decisions data stays single-project at build time per the build script's GAD_PROJECT_ROOT env var — broaden in a follow-up (Option B in the scoping research: read all projects' planning into ALL_TASKS_BY_PROJECT etc.). Verify: tsc clean, `/` renders for a consumer's repo where their planning root is NOT get-anything-done, /findings filters to the picked project, /project-market collapses to the picked project's species rows.</goal>
      <keywords>data-scoping,project-context,nav-picker,findings-filter,bugs-filter,query-param</keywords>
    </task>

IMPLEMENT task 44-30 (sub-task 44-28.A1) in the get-anything-done project. Write code, verify, report back.

Working dir: C:\Users\benja\Documents\custom_portfolio\vendor\get-anything-done

## Context

This is the first of five sub-tasks split out from phase-44 task 44-28. Read decision gad-188 via `node bin/gad.cjs decisions --projectid get-anything-done | grep -A5 "gad-188"` for the umbrella scope. Your task is the independent data-scoping piece — it has no dependency on the installer work.

**Prior scoping research already mapped the file surface — act on it:**

- The site today has NO unified current-project signal. Some surfaces filter partially (e.g. /planning hardcodes BUGS filter to "gad"), most are global.
- Records that ALREADY carry a project field: `FINDINGS.projects: string[]`, `BUGS.project: string`, `EVAL_RUNS.project: string`, `EVAL_PROJECTS.project?: string` (+ species), `REQUIREMENTS_HISTORY.project: string`, `PLANNING_ZIPS.project: string`.
- Records that do NOT carry projectid (stay global for v1): `SKILLS`, `AGENTS`, `COMMANDS`, `WORKFLOWS`, `HUMAN_WORKFLOWS`, `SIGNAL`, `ALL_TASKS`, `ALL_PHASES`, `ALL_DECISIONS`.
- Build-script project discovery: `getEvalRoots()` in `site/scripts/build-site-data.mjs` (L96-142) reads `gad-config.toml` upward from REPO_ROOT for up to 8 parent dirs, returns `[{id, dir}]` for every `[[evals.roots]]` entry plus the default.
- Canonical config file in the current consumer monorepo: `C:\Users\benja\Documents\custom_portfolio\gad-config.toml` — also has `[[planning.roots]]` you'll want to surface in the picker.
- Root layout: `site/app/layout.tsx` — wraps children in `DevIdProvider`, `ClientDebugShell`, `KeyboardShortcutsProvider`, `Toaster`. No current-project context.
- Marketing shell: `site/components/site/MarketingShell.tsx` wraps every page in `<Nav /> + <Footer />`.
- Nav surface: `site/components/landing/nav/Nav.tsx` + `NavDesktop.tsx` + `NavActions.tsx` (not yet read — NavActions is where the picker goes).

## Deliverables

### 1. New: `site/lib/pr
... [truncated 3613 bytes] ...
eal eval project (has entries in `EVAL_PROJECTS`), collapse the marketplace to just that project's species rows. Otherwise (e.g. the default "get-anything-done"), show the full marketplace.

### 8. Leave alone for v1

- `/skills`, `/formulas`, `/glossary`, `/`, `/about`, `/methodology`, `/workflows`, `/lineage` — stay framework-global
- Planning tasks/phases/decisions (`ALL_TASKS`, etc.) — stay single-project at build time, handled by `GAD_PROJECT_ROOT` env var; broadening these is deferred to a follow-up per the scoping research's Option B

### 9. Mark task 44-30 done

Update `.planning/TASK-REGISTRY.xml` task `id="44-30"`:
- `status="done"`, `skill="default"`, `agent="general-purpose"`, `type="site"`
- Add `<resolution>` block summarizing what shipped and what was deferred to Option-B follow-up

### 10. STATE next-action

Run `node bin/gad.cjs state set-next-action --projectid get-anything-done "..."` — one line summarizing the A1 landing and pointing at the next active task (44-31 B0 release pipeline, or another sprint-9 picker). Hard cap 600 chars.

## Verify

```
node bin/gad.cjs workflow validate --projectid get-anything-done 2>&1 | tail -20
cd site && bunx tsc --noEmit 2>&1 | tail -30
```

`workflow validate` should still show 7 agent workflows + 2 human workflows unchanged. tsc must be CLEAN. Fix any type errors before reporting. If you hit a Suspense boundary error from `useSearchParams`, wrap the consumer in `<Suspense>` and move on — that's a known Next.js requirement.

## Report back

Under 500 words. Include:
1. Files created (full repo-relative paths)

4. One-line description of the picker UI shape you landed on

(already shipped in bin/install.js 5993-line GSD fork supporting 9
coding-agent runtimes). New sub-tasks: 44-30 (A1 data scoping, this
commit), 44-31 (B0 release pipeline foundation), 44-32 (B2 installer
extension), 44-33 (B3 site packaging), 44-34 (B4 portable node).

## 44-30 — A1 data scoping (shipped)

New `useCurrentProject()` React hook reads `?projectid=<id>` from the
URL, validates against the registered-project list, falls back to the
first [[planning.roots]] entry in gad-config.toml. Build-time helper
walks gad-config.toml files upward from REPO_ROOT (up to 8 parents) and
unions all [[planning.roots]] + [[evals.roots]] into a single
project-config.generated.ts — 9 projects discovered on the current
monorepo. Current project threaded through server-side pages via
searchParams prop.

New files:
- site/lib/project-config.generated.ts — emitted by build-site-data.mjs
- site/lib/project-config.ts — re-export shim
- site/components/ProjectContext.tsx — Suspense-wrapped provider + hook
- site/components/landing/nav/NavProjectPicker.tsx — rounded-pill dropdown

Surfaces scoped:
- /planning — BUGS filter: replaced `b.project === "gad"` hardcode with
  alias-pair `[get-anything-done, gad]` when current project is the
  framework (keeps legacy records visible)
- /findings — filters FINDINGS by `projects.includes(currentProject)`;
  empty `projects[]` = framework-level, shows everywhere
- /findings/[slug] — soft amber out-of-scope banner when tag mismatch;
  never 404
- /project-market — collapses to current project's species rows when
  EVAL_PROJECTS has matching entries; full marketplace otherwise

Surfaces left global for v1: /, /skills, /formulas, /glossary, /about,
/methodology, /workflows, /lineage, and planning ALL_TASKS/ALL_PHASES/
ALL_DECISIONS (still build-time single-project via GAD_PROJECT_ROOT
env var — broadening deferred to Option B follow-up).

Nav picker: rounded-pill button showing current project id + chevron,
dropdown lists all 9 registered projects with kind badge (planning /
evals / both). Wrapped in Identified as="NavProjectPicker" per gad-187.

Verify: tsc clean. workflow validate: 7 agent + 2 human workflows
unchanged.

Known follow-up: project-config.generated.ts currently embeds absolute
Windows paths — fine for dev-local, must be repo-relative before
shipping to a consumer machine (tracked for B2/B3 phase).
